issues.summary: Code changes for [CORE - 14427]
SQL code changes for [CORE - 14427]
Add IsCaddyRequested parameter support to EZL/EZLRTS tee sheet interface
issues.description:
Background

In 2023, we added support for passing the IsCaddyRequested parameter as a pass-through to BRS and BRSG1 tee sheet interfaces to support ClubUp (caddy service) integrations. However, this parameter was not added to the EZL tee sheet interface.

Current State

- ✅ BRS tee sheet: Passes IsCaddyRequested parameter (line 163 in BRSTeeSheet.cs)

- ✅ BRSG1 tee sheet: Passes IsCaddyRequested parameter (line 199 in BRSG1TeeSheet.cs)

- ❌ EZL tee sheet: Does NOT pass IsCaddyRequested parameter

Issue

The EZL tee sheet (code: "EZL") does not handle the IsCaddyRequested parameter when making reservations. This means courses using EZLRTS tee sheet systems cannot indicate caddy requests through our booking flow, creating an inconsistent experience across different tee sheet providers.

Expected Behavior

When a booking is made through the EZL tee sheet interface with IsCaddyRequested = true, the caddie request should be submitted to the EZLinks Integrators API via the dedicated Caddie Request endpoint (see RTS-2163), similar to how BRS and BRSG handle it through their respective interfaces.

---
Technical Details

Important Update (per Viitalii Slobodianyk): A completely new endpoint was added in RTS-2163 to support caddie requests for EZL/EZRTS. The EZL implementation should use this dedicated endpoint rather than adding a parameter to the existing booking payload.

⚠️ BRS/BRSG1 must remain untouched. BRS and BRSG1 are still using the old method (passing IsCaddyRequested directly in the booking payload). Do NOT modify BRS/BRSG1 — only EZL will use the new Caddie Request API endpoint.

New Caddie Request Endpoint (from RTS-2163):

- Type: POST

- URL path: Under the "Tee Time" path in the Integrators API

- GrantArea: APIReservationSystem

- Input Parameters:

- SponsorID (required)

- ConfirmID (required)

- Caddie-Requested (required, Boolean)

- Behavior: Writes value to ETNv4.dbo.TTQuick.QuickEntryCaddy (False = 0, True = 1)

- Response: Indicates success or failure

- Failure reasons: missing parameters, SponsorID lacks access to course, ConfirmID doesn't exist

File to modify: Framework/GolfNow.Framework.TeeSheets/Interfaces/EZL/EZLinksTeeSheet.cs

Method to update: BookTeeTimeAsync (starting around line 2208)

What's needed: After a successful booking, if request.TeeTime.IsCaddyRequested is true, call the Caddie Request POST endpoint with the SponsorID and the ConfirmID from the booking response.

Reference implementations:

- BRSTeeSheet.cs:163 (old method — DO NOT CHANGE)

- BRSG1TeeSheet.cs:199 (old method — DO NOT CHANGE)

- RTS-2163 — Caddie Request endpoint spec (EZL uses this)

---
Acceptance Criteria

- After a successful EZL booking with IsCaddyRequested = true, the Caddie Request endpoint is called with the appropriate SponsorID and ConfirmID

- Parameter defaults to false/0 when not explicitly set (no endpoint call needed)

- BRS and BRSG1 implementations remain unchanged (they use the existing pass-through method)

- Existing EZL bookings continue to work without regression

- Handles endpoint failure gracefully (booking should not fail if caddie request fails)

---
Additional Notes: The parameter is already available in the TR API (Reservation.IsCaddyRequested) and flows through to the MakeReservationRequest object, so the plumbing exists—we just need to call the new Caddie Request endpoint after a successful EZL booking when the flag is set.

issues.summary: Update Geo Search configuration
issues.description:
We got some feedback on our GN-EU app from one of our German teammates over in Marketing about our Geo Searches endpoint (/v1/geoplaces/lookup), its items number 4 & 5 in this doc: https://bwt3-my.sharepoint.com/:w:/p/206637259/IQCCZ1Lb_3afTbWNEbrvHj2vAeYmlNAvtjZY54yJYeSp1AQ?e=fIlcxl . Brian said mentioned it should be a simple fix by adding alternate spelling

- Test that typing in one of the alternate spellings of cities returns the result in the same priority as the primary spelling

- Example

- Primary - Munich

- Alternate - München

- Typing in München would still return as Munich in the UI but will be first on the list - Future ticket will be created to support translating location names

- Typing in Munich would be first on the list
issues.descriptionHtml:
We got some feedback on our GN-EU app from one of our German teammates over in Marketing about our Geo Searches endpoint (/v1/geoplaces/lookup), its items number 4 & 5 in this doc: https://bwt3-my.sharepoint.com/:w:/p/206637259/IQCCZ1Lb_3afTbWNEbrvHj2vAeYmlNAvtjZY54yJYeSp1AQ?e=fIlcxl . Brian said mentioned it should be a simple fix by adding alternate spelling

  • Test that typing in one of the alternate spellings of cities returns the result in the same priority as the primary spelling

    • Example

      • Primary - Munich

      • Alternate - München

      • Typing in München would still return as Munich in the UI but will be first on the list - Future ticket will be created to support translating location names

      • Typing in Munich would be first on the list

issues.summary: Update EZ Legacy API Call (AddConfirmationNote) to Include Param5 – SponsorID
issues.description:
Summary

When calling the EZ Legacy API endpoint AddConfirmationNote, we are currently not passing Param5 (SponsorID).

We need to update the API call to include this parameter going forward.

---
Background

Endpoint: AddConfirmationNote

Current Parameters:

- Param1 – InternetUserID

- Param2 – InternetPassword

- Param3 – ConfirmID

- Param4 – ConfirmationNote

- Param5 – SponsorID (int) ← Currently not being sent

Example documentation snippet shows:

Param5  SponsorID  int

However, current logs confirm the call only sends up through Param4 (ConfirmationNote).

---
Requested Change

Modify the EZ Legacy API call to include:

Param5 = SponsorID

This value should be populated appropriately based on the booking context.

---
Reason for Change

The EZ Dev Team is implementing a workaround related to recent Hot Deal work.
Passing SponsorID will help ensure related functionality works properly and prevent edge case issues.

---
Acceptance Criteria

-  AddConfirmationNote API call includes Param5.

-  SponsorID is correctly populated and passed as an integer.

-  No regression issues with confirmation note creation.

-  Verified in logs that Param5 is being transmitted.

comments.author: Tyler Olechnowicz
comments.body:
QA Test Instructions

- Book a tee time that is configured for EZLinks Legacy API.

- Verify the reservation is created successfully.

- Coordinate with the EZLinks team and review the AddConfirmationNote request.

- Confirm that SponsorID is being sent as Param5 in the request.

- Verify the SponsorID value matches the course configuration.

issues.summary: Add cart/tax include parameters to tee-time/book for EZL (EZTP and EZ-RTS) integration
issues.description:
Summary

CORE needs to support the new cart/tax include parameter contract required by https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/RTS-7614#icft=RTS-7614 so RTS/EZRTS/EZP can receive a per-player/per-slot breakdown during tee-time/book, persist it in ETNv4, and send the correct values to G1/EZSuite POS during tender.

This is part of the forward path on the new booking API. It should not be treated as a legacy API remediation effort.

Check with @Juan Gonzalez
https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/RTS-7614

---
Expected Behavior

- CORE can provide the RTS-required optional cart/tax fields per player slot in the tee-time/book flow.

- When values are present, CORE sends them exactly as calculated/known by CORE.

- When values are absent, CORE omits them or sends null; CORE should not default or fabricate values.

- RTS can use the received values as its exclusive source for the ETNv4 cart/tax breakdown tracked under https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/RTS-7614#icft=RTS-7614.

---
Current Behavior / Problem

- https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/RTS-7614#icft=RTS-7614 is waiting on a CORE-side ticket to track the dependency for sending the new cart/tax parameter set.

- Existing CORE/reservation models include external charge detail concepts, but RTS needs a specific per-slot parameter contract on tee-time/book for greens fee tax, cart fee, cart fee tax, and partial-prepay status.

- Without CORE sending this breakdown, RTS cannot reliably persist the values needed for EZTP/EZRTS tender flows without deriving from unreliable ETNv4 fields.

---
Scope Guardrails

- Include the CORE-side request/DTO/model/mapping work needed to support the RTS-required parameter set on the new tee-time/book API path.

- Include tests proving optional/null behavior and value preservation.

- Do not implement a separate fix for the legacy booking API. For legacy API impact, the intended resolution is migration/use of the new API path.

- Do not require RTS storage, ETNv4 schema changes, or POS tender packet changes in this CORE ticket; those are tracked by https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/RTS-7614#icft=RTS-7614.

- Do not derive, supplement, or fall back to ETNv4 fields such as dbo.TeeOffTransactionAuditItem, dbo.CCAuthorization, or dbo.TTPrepaid.PrepaidFeeAmount.

---
Requested Parameters

All fields are optional and should be represented per slot/player where the booking contract supports slot-level detail.

- includes_greens_fee_tax

- greens_fee_tax_pct

- greens_fee_tax_amount_ppd

- greens_fee_tax_amount_due

- includes_cart_fee

- cart_fee_amount_ppd

- cart_fee_amount_due

- includes_cart_fee_tax

- cart_fee_tax_pct

- cart_fee_tax_amount_ppd

- cart_fee_tax_amount_due

- is_partial_prepaid

---
Technical Notes

- Source dependency: https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/RTS-7614#icft=RTS-7614, "Support Cart/Tax Include Parameters on tee-time/book".

- Existing CORE code paths that may be relevant:

- DotNet2/TeeTimesAPI/GolfNow.API.2.1/GolfNow.API.Business/Reservation/MakeReservationRequest.cs

- DotNet2/TeeTimesAPI/GolfNow.API.2.1/GolfNow.API.Business/Reservation/Workflow/MakeReservation/Activities/MakeReservationActivity.cs

- DotNet2/TeeTimesAPI/GolfNow.API.2.1/GolfNow.API.Contracts/DataContracts/Order/Items/ExternalChargeDetails.cs

- DotNet2/TeeTimesAPI/GolfNow.API.2.1/GolfNow.API.Contracts/DataContracts/Order/Items/ExternalChargeLineItem.cs

- DotNet2/TeeTimesAPI/GolfNow.API.2.1/GolfNow.API.Service/Converters/ServiceBusinessMapingExtensions.cs

- Internal Services/GolfNow.Services.Reservation/GolfNow.Services.Reservation.Provider/Workflow/Reservation/Activities/External/ApplyExternalChargeDetailsActivity.cs

- Current local model names already include ExternalChargeDetails and ExternalChargeLineItem; engineering should decide whether to extend that structure or add a dedicated RTS-facing cart/tax breakdown model.

---
Acceptance Criteria

Existing Behavior Preserved

- Existing booking flows continue to work when no cart/tax include parameters are supplied.

- Existing external charge detail behavior is not regressed for current callers.

New Behavior

- [ ] CORE supports all RTS-requested parameters above as optional values in the new tee-time/book flow.

- [ ] CORE sends provided values through to RTS without recalculating, defaulting, or deriving from ETNv4 fallback fields.

- [ ] Omitted parameters remain absent/null downstream.

- [ ] Per-slot/player data remains associated with the correct booked player/slot.

- [ ] Unit and/or integration coverage validates populated, partially populated, and omitted parameter sets.

Not Included Unless Product Expands Scope

- No legacy booking API remediation is included in this story.

- No RTS ETNv4 persistence or POS tender payload implementation is included in this story.

---
Test Matrix

Scenario

Expected Result

tee-time/book with all cart/tax fields populated

Values are accepted and passed downstream exactly as supplied.

tee-time/book with only greens fee tax values populated

Populated values pass downstream; omitted cart/cart tax values remain null/absent.

tee-time/book with no new fields

Existing booking behavior remains unchanged.

Multi-player booking with different per-slot values

Each slot retains its own cart/tax breakdown.

Partial prepaid booking

is_partial_prepaid and ppd/due splits reflect the supplied values.

---
Open Questions / Product Decisions

- Confirm the final wire-format and field casing expected by RTS for tee-time/book.

- Confirm whether the CORE source of truth should be the existing ExternalChargeDetails/line-item model or a dedicated cart/tax include DTO.

- Confirm whether is_partial_prepaid should remain a Boolean even though https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/RTS-7614#icft=RTS-7614 currently describes both not-prepaid and fully-prepaid as No.

---
Related

- https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/RTS-7614#icft=RTS-7614

- https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/RTS-4339#icft=RTS-4339

- https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/TTSUPPORT-7211#icft=TTSUPPORT-7211

Communication Note

For https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/TTSUPPORT-7307#icft=TTSUPPORT-7307/new-vs-legacy wording: CORE resolved the issue on the new booking API path. The intended resolution for legacy API exposure is migration/use of the new API, not a separate legacy investigation under this ticket.

comments.author: Tyler Olechnowicz
comments.body:
QA Test Instructions

- Book a tee time reservation using a rate set configured for the EZL tee sheet and the new EZL API.

- Verify the reservation is created successfully.

- Coordinate with the EZL team to review the tee-time/book request received by EZL.

- Confirm the new prepaid_and_tax fields are present and populated as expected by EZL.

- Repeat the test with:

- A tax-exclusive tee time.

- A tax-inclusive tee time.

- For both scenarios, validate with the EZL team that the fee, tax, and tax-inclusion values received by EZL match the expected reservation pricing.
comments.bodyHtml:
QA Test Instructions

  1. Book a tee time reservation using a rate set configured for the EZL tee sheet and the new EZL API.

  2. Verify the reservation is created successfully.

  3. Coordinate with the EZL team to review the tee-time/book request received by EZL.

  4. Confirm the new prepaid_and_tax fields are present and populated as expected by EZL.

  5. Repeat the test with:

    • A tax-exclusive tee time.

    • A tax-inclusive tee time.

  6. For both scenarios, validate with the EZL team that the fee, tax, and tax-inclusion values received by EZL match the expected reservation pricing.

issues.summary: Home Course Selection/Management
issues.description:
We need to create the ability to assign a Home Course for a user and manage the restrictions around Home Course changes.

Requirements

- One customer can have a configurable amount of GolfFacilityIds assigned as Home Course

- Create a platform configuration for maximum number of Home Courses Allowed

- Configuration should be set to 1 for launch but we should support >1 in the future where a customer could potentially have more than 1

- Do not allow selection of Master Facilities

- Create an exclusion list of GolfFacilityIds just in case certain courses refuse to participate

- If a user selects a course on this list, throw an error saying it is not applicable for Home Course selection

- In Prod, this should be empty but we should test by adding some in Dev

- Log the CreatedTimeStamp, LastModifiedTimestamp and LastModifiedBy for selection and modifications

- LastModifiedBy should be the CustomerNumber or CustomerID or SystemUserId if it was done by a service member through GNC

- Create a history log of the changes made for each customer

- Home Course Selection/Modifications

- Any Full Customer without an applicable membership (without a product with BenefitID ##) or no membership at all (Created in https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-14428)

- You are allowed two selections/modifications in a 1 year period

- One initial selection would start the 1 year clock

- One additional modification within that 1 year period

- Purchasing a product with BenefitId ##

- This will restart the 1 year period with the start date matching the subscription start date

- This new period will exclude any previous modifications and restart at 2

- Multiple purchases of a product within the 1 year period will not affect the date range

- Example: The initial purchase of a monthly product will start the 1 year period but the renewal of the monthly product will not restart at the end of 30 days, it will still be the original 1 year period. After the 1 year period of 12 renewed monthly memberships, you will then start a new 1 year

- As a user that has already selected their home course and they either purchase an applicable product, renew their applicable product or are a non member and have crossed the 365 day mark

- Your previous selection will remain as is and roll over to the new period as a new Home Course record

- You are allowed two modifications with the new 365 day period

- Monthly product renewals do not reset the modification count or 1 year period

- Guest customers should not be able to select a Home Course

- GNC should have the ability to override the Home Course selection at any time for support purposes

- GNC Modifications should be logged in Change Tracking

- GNC Modifications should not count towards the two allowed modification count

- If you have two stacked memberships with one starting in the future, you should not be able to make modifications to the future dated membership Home Course until the subscription starts

- Future dated memberships are now out of scope since all users get a 365 day period no matter what

- There should be an optional reason for choosing a home course

- Reasons:

- Convenience/location

- Price/value

- Course conditions

- Social/community

- Course amenities

- Personal history/familiarity

- There should be an optional reason for modifying home course

- Reasons:

- Course closed

- Course conditions deteriorated

- I moved

- Snow bird

- Accidental selection

- Course no longer fits my wants/needs

Deliverables

- An API for frontend to set a Home Course

- Needs ChannelId in the request so we can store where the Home Course was set/modified

- An API for frontend to modify a Home Course selection

- Needs ChannelId in the request so we can store where the Home Course was set/modified

- Error messaging around modification limit restrictions

- You have reached your Home Course modification limit. Please reach out to GolfNow Support for further assistance.

- Error messaging around selecting the same facility twice when modifying

- [FacilityName] is already selected as your Home Course.

- Can GetCustomer and GetCustomerSubscription APIs also return the Home Course GolfFacilityId? This is a nice to have

- A GNC UI for managing/Supporting Home Course - https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-14437

comments.author: Luis Vallejo Mursuli
comments.body:
Hi @Brian Pugh  and @Daniel McNaughton ,

I’ve been reviewing CORE-14427 in detail to prepare for implementation, and I want to make sure we align on a few areas where the requirements are currently ambiguous or open-ended. Clarifying these upfront will help avoid rework and ensure the APIs and data model are designed correctly for both launch and future extensibility.

---
1. Data Model / ReferenceId

- The ticket mentions ReferenceId = CustomerSubscriptionId for members, and possibly 1 (or another value) for non-members to avoid nulls.

- Should ReferenceId be nullable for non-members, or do we want a sentinel value?

- If using a sentinel, should we instead model this explicitly (e.g., IsMember flag or ReferenceType) to avoid overloading semantics?

---
2. Benefit Eligibility

Since I’m also assigned to CORE-14428, and it needs to be implemented in the same sprint, I want to clarify the eligibility rule before wiring it into CORE-14427.

CORE-14428 asks us to insert the new "Home Course Eligible" benefit and associate it with the applicable products. Should CORE-14427 rely only on that new benefit to determine whether a user is eligible for member-based Home Course behavior, or are there any other existing/current benefits that should also be considered?

---
3. Selection / Modification Limits

There appears to be a potential inconsistency:

- Non-member:

- “Two selections/modifications in a 365-day period” (initial + 1 modification)

- Member:

- “Two selections per subscription period” (initial + 1 modification)

- Later:

- “You are allowed two modifications with the new period”

Can we confirm:

- Is the total allowance 2 actions per period (initial + 1 modification)?

- Or initial selection + 2 modifications (3 total actions) after rollover?

---
4. Rollover Behavior

- When a user renews or crosses the 365-day mark:

- A new Home Course record is created and previous selection “rolls over”

Questions:

- What event triggers the rollover? (subscription renewal event, scheduled job, lazy creation on next request, etc.)

- Does the rollover count as the initial selection of the new period?

- If a user does nothing (no modification), do they still retain full modification allowance?

---
5. Future-Dated Memberships

- Users cannot modify Home Course for a future subscription until it starts

Questions:

- How do we determine the active subscription context when multiple exist?

- What happens if:

- Current subscription is active and future one exists → user modifies now?

- Future subscription starts → do we automatically create a new record?

- What is the expected error response when trying to modify a future-dated record?

---
6. Multiple Home Courses (Future Support)

- Configurable max is 1 at launch, but should support >1 in future

Questions:

- Should we design APIs and DB to support multiple Home Courses now (array-based), even if config = 1?

- Are modification limits:

- Per customer globally, or

- Per Home Course slot, or

- Per subscription period regardless of count?

---
7. GNC Override Behavior

- GNC can override “at any time”

Questions:

- Does override bypass:

- Modification limits?

- Future membership restrictions?

- Exclusion list?

- Master facility restriction?

- Should overrides:

- Require a reason?

- Reset modification counters?

- Be flagged differently in audit/history?

---
8. Exclusion List

I already know how to identify whether a facility is a Master Facility, so the main open question here is around the exclusion list.

Should the exclusion list of GolfFacilityIds already exist somewhere, or do we need to include it as part of the data model/configuration changes for this work?

Additionally, should this exclusion list be managed through database/configuration only, or should there eventually be an admin/GNC management path for it?

---
9. Audit / History Logging

- We log Created/Modified timestamps and “LastModifiedBy”

Questions:

- Should we standardize LastModifiedBy as a single identifier type (e.g., always CustomerId or SystemUserId)?

- Should history include:

- Old value vs new value

- Reason (if provided)

- Source (frontend vs GNC)

- ReferenceId / period context?

---
10. API Contracts

The ticket lists APIs but does not define contracts.

We need clarity on:

- Request/response structure for:

- Set Home Course

- Modify Home Course

- Idempotency:

- If user selects the same course again → does it count as modification?

- Error responses:

- Modification limit reached

- Future membership restriction

- Invalid facility (master/excluded)

---
11. Integration with Existing APIs

- Question raised: should GetCustomer and GetCustomerSubscription return Home Course?

Can we confirm:

- Is this required for launch?

- Should we return:

- Single GolfFacilityId, or

- Full Home Course object(s)?

---
12. Acceptance Criteria / Edge Cases

To ensure completeness, we should confirm expected behavior for:

- 3rd modification attempt → error

- Rollover without user interaction

- Switching from non-member → member

- Membership expiration → non-member behavior

- Stacked memberships with overlapping dates

---
If we can align on these points, I can proceed with a concrete design (data model + API contracts + validation flow) and avoid rework later.

Thanks!

issues.description:
We need to have the strokeAdjustment per hole  - and subsequently the netStrokes value - reflect the roundTypeId (all 18, front 9, back 9)

- the strokeAdjustment will change for each hole based on handicap calculations / GPA services

- right now the strokeAdjustment is only referencing ALL 18 hole round type

This ticket requires that the strokeAdjustment of each hole reflects the RoundType | FrontNine => 1 , BackNine => 2 , Eighteen => 3

comments.author: Alex McNay
comments.body:
Outstanding issues

- USGA sourced courses are always showing the all 18 handicap value for the playingHandicap - even if the roundTypeId is Front9 (1) or Back9 (2)

- Example is 38017 in AWS Dev

- Dots are being distributed correctly, it’s the playingHandicap value that isn’t displaying correctly APPEARS RESOLVED BUT WILL MONITOR

- The 9 hole side that is not be played/set by the roundType is still getting half of it’s strokeAdjustments on the most difficult holes on that side. Example - first four holes are the Front9 most difficult which is correct. But the 2 on the Back9 shouldn’t get any strokes since this is a Front9 round

- Still not seeing the right flow for updating roundTypes

- When the playerRound is created it is inheriting the roundType value from the Outing Round, regardless of what is being passed in through the creation of the playerRound here - /api/v2/golfOuting/{golfOutingId}/rounds/{golfOutingRoundId}/players/{playerId}/playerRounds

- it is the Outing round roundHoleType value that initially determines how the playingHandicap is split between the holes in the hole array at the top of the playerRound call. So if the Outing Round is created with a "roundHoleType": 3 then the playingHandicap value will be divided between all 18 holes.

- The roundType value that is passed at the creation of the playerRound is determining what handicap value is used for playingHandicap. The correct value is being assigned to this field when based on what is provided to the create playerRound endpoint above.

- Updating the playerRound roundTypeId doesn’t change the playingHandicap, so a user isn’t able to move between 9 and 18 hole rounds on the same playerRound at the moment.

- however when this value is updated the strokeAdjustments are being moved around correctly for the most part,

- Front 9 and Back 9 give the correct strokeAdjustments - with the exception of issue 2 above where the other side gets a few lingering strokes

- All 18 is taking the playingHandicap and dividing it in half, which is correct except it’s using the wrong number since the playerHandicap isn’t changing from the creation of the playerRound - point 3.a.iii above
comments.bodyHtml:
Outstanding issues

  1. USGA sourced courses are always showing the all 18 handicap value for the playingHandicap - even if the roundTypeId is Front9 (1) or Back9 (2)

    1. Example is 38017 in AWS Dev

    2. Dots are being distributed correctly, it’s the playingHandicap value that isn’t displaying correctly APPEARS RESOLVED BUT WILL MONITOR

  2. The 9 hole side that is not be played/set by the roundType is still getting half of it’s strokeAdjustments on the most difficult holes on that side. Example - first four holes are the Front9 most difficult which is correct. But the 2 on the Back9 shouldn’t get any strokes since this is a Front9 round

  1. Still not seeing the right flow for updating roundTypes

    1. When the playerRound is created it is inheriting the roundType value from the Outing Round, regardless of what is being passed in through the creation of the playerRound here - /api/v2/golfOuting/{golfOutingId}/rounds/{golfOutingRoundId}/players/{playerId}/playerRounds

      1. it is the Outing round roundHoleType value that initially determines how the playingHandicap is split between the holes in the hole array at the top of the playerRound call. So if the Outing Round is created with a "roundHoleType": 3 then the playingHandicap value will be divided between all 18 holes.

      2. The roundType value that is passed at the creation of the playerRound is determining what handicap value is used for playingHandicap. The correct value is being assigned to this field when based on what is provided to the create playerRound endpoint above.

      3. Updating the playerRound roundTypeId doesn’t change the playingHandicap, so a user isn’t able to move between 9 and 18 hole rounds on the same playerRound at the moment.

        1. however when this value is updated the strokeAdjustments are being moved around correctly for the most part,

          1. Front 9 and Back 9 give the correct strokeAdjustments - with the exception of issue 2 above where the other side gets a few lingering strokes

          2. All 18 is taking the playingHandicap and dividing it in half, which is correct except it’s using the wrong number since the playerHandicap isn’t changing from the creation of the playerRound - point 3.a.iii above

issues.summary: Updates to Reservation Confirmation Template
issues.description:
On the Tee Time Reservation email sent to golfers, there are two different bodies that reference benefits of GolfPass. One as a member and one as a non-member. We need to update the text in these versions. See attached for the two versions.

GolfPass Member - Anyone with ProductID [36, 37, 42, 62, 69, 71] - Attached image Screenshot 2026-03-02 at 3.23.43 PM.png

- Can we confirm what validation we are using to determine what member they are? We got a report that some members are receiving the non member version. Confirm with Brian when you find the logic - Luis Confirmed the logic is based on ProductId

- Change “Extra GolfPass Rewards Points” to: Earn or redeem GolfPass Points on tee time bookings

issues.summary: Improve Apple Pay Support when there are multiple processors of the same currency
issues.description:
Our current logic for Apple Pay matches the payment type of Apple Pay to the the processor that has a matching CurrencyCode. The concern is that the some Currency Codes have multiple processors so it could choose the wrong one.

Example:

- CurrencyCode: EUR

- Processor: GNEU-EUR

- Processor: GP-EUR

Expectation:

- For an Apple Pay transaction, we should choose the processor that matches the facility of the tee time

- Scenario:

- FID - 19631

- Book an Apple Pay tee time

- Use the GNEU-EUR processor since that is what is configured at the facility

- Scenario:

- FID - 1951

- Book an Apple Pay tee times

- Use the GP-EUR processor since that is what is configured at the facility

Testing:

- Need to do full regression of Apple Pay

- Hot Deals

- $0 due online course time with payfac for reservations

- $0 due online course time without payfac for reservations

- $ due online course time with payfac for reservations

- $ due online course time without payfac for reservations

- Prepaid course times with and without payfac

- GolfPass Paid

- GolfPass Free

- Renewals

- No Shows

- Specifically test EUR and AUD currency as those were reported with issues in prod

- Find an Apple Pay transaction with one of the old ChargeBys for Apple Pay → Make sure you can refund that after the data cleanup script is run

- Work with Yoel to set up test data

- Do a no show with and old ChargeBy as well

issues.summary: Modernize Affiliate API dependencies to remediate security vulnerabilities
issues.description:
Modernize the Affiliate API's application dependencies and supporting configuration to remediate identified security vulnerabilities and improve compatibility with .NET Framework 4.8.

Changes

- Updated and revalidated application dependencies to ensure compatibility with .NET Framework 4.8.

- Corrected package, assembly, and analyzer references impacted by dependency upgrades.

- Modernized and consolidated dependency injection configuration used throughout the application.

- Added and corrected service registrations required for application startup and runtime dependency resolution.

- Improved application startup and configuration initialization to align with current dependency versions.

- Removed obsolete configuration artifacts and legacy project references that were no longer required.

- Reviewed and updated client-side dependency configuration and JavaScript resources.

- Evaluated Bootstrap and front-end dependencies while preserving compatibility with the existing Bootstrap 3 user interface.

- Investigated reported security vulnerabilities, including a Handlebars vulnerability, and verified that Handlebars is not directly referenced or loaded by the application.

- Improved maintainability, readability, and consistency of application configuration and dependency management.

issues.summary: Timelyst - Integrate with Club Champion API
issues.description:
Goal : Fully integrate with Club Champion API to get Fitting Services information and Fitters information base from the GolfCourse Interface Configuration within CRM database.

Requirements :

- Be able to reconciliate jobs for Club Champion Interfaces base on the CRM configurations.

- Be able to pull and ingest Fitting services.

- Be able to pull and ingest Fitters. NOTE : we know all of the available fitters but we don’t get the availabilities of the individual fitters.

- Be able to pull and ingest the availabilities.

- Be able to schedule an appointment.

- Be able to cancel an appointment.

Steps :

- Mockup a configuration for one Offering.

Similar with UCS configuration, new custom ClubChampion configuration is going to be added to the CRMGolfCourse MSSQL table record.

For example GCGolfCourse = Chicago|60|Iron,Putter,Driver

Ingest {client_id}|{clientSecret}|{username}|{password} from [dbo].[CRMInterfaceAttribute] CRM table.

Which are information needed to OAuth and GetAvailabilities, reference to Postman collection.
Exposed Config Values:

- Store Name

- Selected Fitting (Free Text – e.g., Iron, Putter, etc.)

- client_id

- clientSecret

- username

- password

- Follow the USC CacheManagerScheduleReconciliationJob workflow, generate the logic for a new interface for ClubChampion(CC). The goal is to have Hangfire create Job that will periodically sync with ClubChampion data.

Provide a list of MSSQL table changes required for us to setup a new Job for ClubChampion that sync data in the same interval as UCS.

- In the IngestionAndSync project add a new provider for ClubChampion. The goal is base on the Selected Fittings configure on the Offering document

- Setup OAuth needed to call the ClubChampion API.

- Call and fetch from GetFittingInfo endpoint to ingest the selected services.

- Generate one resource call “AnyFitter”. (because we get the availabilities for each individual fitter resource, we will track the resource availabilities within one resource but have a slot capacities N = number of fitter active base on the Get Store Info endpoint.

- Iterate each service and call and fetch from Availability Info endpoint to build out the RateSets and the Resource Availability for each service. ("page": 1 is fix)

- Implement Schedule Appointment endpoint.

- Implement Cancel Appointment endpoint.

NOTE : the timezone return use “Z” or “+00:000” which is UTC format, however, we need to treat it as local time of the Facility without doing any timezone conversion.
issues.descriptionHtml:
Goal : Fully integrate with Club Champion API to get Fitting Services information and Fitters information base from the GolfCourse Interface Configuration within CRM database.

Requirements :

  • Be able to reconciliate jobs for Club Champion Interfaces base on the CRM configurations.

  • Be able to pull and ingest Fitting services.

  • Be able to pull and ingest Fitters. NOTE : we know all of the available fitters but we don’t get the availabilities of the individual fitters.

  • Be able to pull and ingest the availabilities.

  • Be able to schedule an appointment.

  • Be able to cancel an appointment.

Steps :

  1. Mockup a configuration for one Offering.

Similar with UCS configuration, new custom ClubChampion configuration is going to be added to the CRMGolfCourse MSSQL table record.

For example GCGolfCourse = Chicago|60|Iron,Putter,Driver

Ingest {client_id}|{clientSecret}|{username}|{password} from [dbo].[CRMInterfaceAttribute] CRM table.

Which are information needed to OAuth and GetAvailabilities, reference to Postman collection.
Exposed Config Values:

  • Store Name

  • Selected Fitting (Free Text – e.g., Iron, Putter, etc.)

  • client_id

  • clientSecret

  • username

  • password

  1. Follow the USC CacheManagerScheduleReconciliationJob workflow, generate the logic for a new interface for ClubChampion(CC). The goal is to have Hangfire create Job that will periodically sync with ClubChampion data.

Provide a list of MSSQL table changes required for us to setup a new Job for ClubChampion that sync data in the same interval as UCS.

  1. In the IngestionAndSync project add a new provider for ClubChampion. The goal is base on the Selected Fittings configure on the Offering document

    1. Setup OAuth needed to call the ClubChampion API.

    2. Call and fetch from GetFittingInfo endpoint to ingest the selected services.

    3. Generate one resource call “AnyFitter”. (because we get the availabilities for each individual fitter resource, we will track the resource availabilities within one resource but have a slot capacities N = number of fitter active base on the Get Store Info endpoint.

    4. Iterate each service and call and fetch from Availability Info endpoint to build out the RateSets and the Resource Availability for each service. ("page": 1 is fix)

    5. Implement Schedule Appointment endpoint.

    6. Implement Cancel Appointment endpoint.

NOTE : the timezone return use “Z” or “+00:000” which is UTC format, however, we need to treat it as local time of the Facility without doing any timezone conversion.

issues.summary: ChatGPT MVP Follow Ups
issues.description:
Location Based Search + Tee Times  Tool

Use ChannelId 331 for all API calls

Prod API Creds: TBD

DEV API Creds: TBD

- Call this API to get the facility results and info: https://api.gnsvc.com/GolfNowAPI.svc/rest/channel/331/facility-summaries?q=geolocation&skip=0&sort-by=facilities.distance&date-min=2026-02-07&date-max=2026-02-07&proximity=35&expand=RatingSummary&trade-only=false&take=10&latitude=28.4258547&longitude=-81.2408682&isSimulator=false

-

- The above is the base API call that will get unfiltered facilities by the basic requirements to search assuming the user does not specify anything

- Take up to 10 facilities

- Potential Parameters to append based on prompts

- &trade-only=true If the prompt mentions Hot Deals or best deals

- &price-min or &price-max if the prompt mentions price restrictions

- &time-min or &time-max if the prompt mentions time restrictions

- &holes if the prompt mentions they number of holes they are looking for. The default should be 18

- &players if the prompt mentions the number of players they have. The default should be 1

- &isSimulator=truer driving rangesif the prompt mentions indoor golf, simulators o

- &sort-by=facilities.ratingand &sort-direction=desc if the prompt mentions sorting by highest rated or top rated

- &sorty-by=GreenFees and &sort-direction=asc or desc if the prompt mentions sorting by lowest or highest price

- Expose FeeMessagingFacilityDisplayRates object

- UI team will use this object for price ranges and fee message text at the top of the widget

- If NULL, they will not display the message and use the standard API fields for the display

- Expose the IsSimulator field so UI can badge facilities appropriately

- Call this API to get a sample of tee times at each FacilityId from the above: {{prod-api-base-url}}/GolfNowAPI.svc/rest/channel/331/facilities/4356/tee-times?skip=0&take=100&trade-only=false&date-min=2026-02-17&date-max=2026-02-17

- The above is the base API call that will get unfiltered tee times per facility by the basic requirements to search assuming the user does not specify anything

- Display 3 tee time tiles

- Prioritize Hot Deals if no specific filter would block those out

- If there are not 3 Hot Deals, fill the open spots with Course Times

- The tee times should match the prompted filters

- if they prompt for 9 holes, display the 9 hole tee times

- if they prompt for afternoon tee times, display the afternoon tee times

- Potential Parameters to append based on prompts

- &trade-only=true If the prompt mentions Hot Deals or best deals

- &price-min or &price-max if the prompt mentions price restrictions

- &time-min or &time-max if the prompt mentions time restrictions

- &holes if the prompt mentions they number of holes they are looking for

- &players if the prompt mentions the number of players they have

- &sorty-by=GreenFees and &sort-direction=asc or desc if the prompt mentions sorting by lowest or highest price

- Expose the IsSimulator field so UI can display tee times appropriately

- Expose Duration

- Expose HasMoreRates so the UI knows when to expand to show related rates

- Expose FeeMessagingFacilityDisplayRates object

- UI team will use this object for price details with fee included and fee message breakdown text

- If NULL, they will not display the message and use the standard API fields for the display

- Tool Description:

- Returns a list of golf facilities with available tee times for display in search results.
Each result combines facility summary data (name, location, distance, rating) with near-term tee time offers (time, price, holes, player count, deal flags).

This tool supports location-based searches using a city, area, or the user’s current location, and allows filtering and sorting by distance, date, time window, number of holes, players, price, rating, and deal types.

Use this tool whenever a user asks to find, browse, or compare golf courses or tee times in a specific area or near them.

- Associated UI: Picture above

Testing:

We need the ability for a ChatGPT user to:

- Prompt by lat/long, zip code, city, direct course name or geographical area and have facilities that return

- Show me available tee times near my house (assuming they have allowed location services and the browser has the lat/long)

- Show me available tee times in 32832

- Show me available tee times at Dubsdread golf course

- Show me available tee times in central florida

- The word “tee time” could be replaced with “golf course(s)” or “course(s)”

- Prompt by the above but with a date or date range

- Show me tee times for tomorrow

- Show me tee times for the next week

- The results should return Facility Name, Address, Summary info like Earliest/Latest Tee Time, Min/Max Price, IsSimulator

- Results should default to filtering by distance from the location based search term ascending

- If a prompt uses words like “Best” or “Highest Rated”, we should filter using our Rating filter descending

- If the prompt uses words like “By Price” or “Cheapest”, we should filter using our price filter ascending

- If no date is provided in the prompt

- Default to today’s tee times

- If it is after 2pm, display tomorrow’s tee times

- Default radius should be 35 miles

- Default facility results limit to 10

- If a user asks for more results, submit another batch of 10

- Clicking on a button should deeplink the user to the facility page UI

- https://www.golfnow.com/tee-times/facility/19058-brookside-at-the-rose-bowl-eo-nay/search

- append a utm_source=chatgptapp to all links so page views can be tracked with analytics and we can create a cookie

- As a fallback, if a prompt is not recognized, display fallback copy: TBD

Repo: https://github.com/GolfNowEng/golf-web-openai

    • Show me available tee times in 32832

    • Show me available tee times at Dubsdread golf course

    • Show me available tee times in central florida

    • The word “tee time” could be replaced with “golf course(s)” or “course(s)”

  • Prompt by the above but with a date or date range

    • Show me tee times for tomorrow

    • Show me tee times for the next week

  • The results should return Facility Name, Address, Summary info like Earliest/Latest Tee Time, Min/Max Price, IsSimulator

  • Results should default to filtering by distance from the location based search term ascending

    • If a prompt uses words like “Best” or “Highest Rated”, we should filter using our Rating filter descending

    • If the prompt uses words like “By Price” or “Cheapest”, we should filter using our price filter ascending

  • If no date is provided in the prompt

    • Default to today’s tee times

    • If it is after 2pm, display tomorrow’s tee times

  • Default radius should be 35 miles

  • Default facility results limit to 10

    • If a user asks for more results, submit another batch of 10

  • Clicking on a button should deeplink the user to the facility page UI

    • https://www.golfnow.com/tee-times/facility/19058-brookside-at-the-rose-bowl-eo-nay/search

    • append a utm_source=chatgptapp to all links so page views can be tracked with analytics and we can create a cookie

  • As a fallback, if a prompt is not recognized, display fallback copy: TBD

Repo: https://github.com/GolfNowEng/golf-web-openai

issues.summary: (USGA-Blocking) strokeAdjustment update for Compete playerRound
issues.description:
We need to have the strokeAdjustment per hole  - and subsequently the netStrokes value - reflect the roundTypeId (all 18, front 9, back 9)

- the strokeAdjustment will change for each hole based on handicap calculations / GPA services

- right now the strokeAdjustment is only referencing ALL 18 hole round type

This ticket requires that the strokeAdjustment of each hole reflects the RoundType | FrontNine => 1 , BackNine => 2 , Eighteen => 3

comments.author: Alex McNay
comments.body:
Outstanding issues

- USGA sourced courses are always showing the all 18 handicap value for the playingHandicap - even if the roundTypeId is Front9 (1) or Back9 (2)

- Example is 38017 in AWS Dev

- Dots are being distributed correctly, it’s the playingHandicap value that isn’t displaying correctly APPEARS RESOLVED BUT WILL MONITOR

- The 9 hole side that is not be played/set by the roundType is still getting half of it’s strokeAdjustments on the most difficult holes on that side. Example - first four holes are the Front9 most difficult which is correct. But the 2 on the Back9 shouldn’t get any strokes since this is a Front9 round

- Still not seeing the right flow for updating roundTypes

- When the playerRound is created it is inheriting the roundType value from the Outing Round, regardless of what is being passed in through the creation of the playerRound here - /api/v2/golfOuting/{golfOutingId}/rounds/{golfOutingRoundId}/players/{playerId}/playerRounds

- it is the Outing round roundHoleType value that initially determines how the playingHandicap is split between the holes in the hole array at the top of the playerRound call. So if the Outing Round is created with a "roundHoleType": 3 then the playingHandicap value will be divided between all 18 holes.

- The roundType value that is passed at the creation of the playerRound is determining what handicap value is used for playingHandicap. The correct value is being assigned to this field when based on what is provided to the create playerRound endpoint above.

- Updating the playerRound roundTypeId doesn’t change the playingHandicap, so a user isn’t able to move between 9 and 18 hole rounds on the same playerRound at the moment.

- however when this value is updated the strokeAdjustments are being moved around correctly for the most part,

- Front 9 and Back 9 give the correct strokeAdjustments - with the exception of issue 2 above where the other side gets a few lingering strokes

- All 18 is taking the playingHandicap and dividing it in half, which is correct except it’s using the wrong number since the playerHandicap isn’t changing from the creation of the playerRound - point 3.a.iii above
comments.bodyHtml:
Outstanding issues

  1. USGA sourced courses are always showing the all 18 handicap value for the playingHandicap - even if the roundTypeId is Front9 (1) or Back9 (2)

    1. Example is 38017 in AWS Dev

    2. Dots are being distributed correctly, it’s the playingHandicap value that isn’t displaying correctly APPEARS RESOLVED BUT WILL MONITOR

  2. The 9 hole side that is not be played/set by the roundType is still getting half of it’s strokeAdjustments on the most difficult holes on that side. Example - first four holes are the Front9 most difficult which is correct. But the 2 on the Back9 shouldn’t get any strokes since this is a Front9 round

  1. Still not seeing the right flow for updating roundTypes

    1. When the playerRound is created it is inheriting the roundType value from the Outing Round, regardless of what is being passed in through the creation of the playerRound here - /api/v2/golfOuting/{golfOutingId}/rounds/{golfOutingRoundId}/players/{playerId}/playerRounds

      1. it is the Outing round roundHoleType value that initially determines how the playingHandicap is split between the holes in the hole array at the top of the playerRound call. So if the Outing Round is created with a "roundHoleType": 3 then the playingHandicap value will be divided between all 18 holes.

      2. The roundType value that is passed at the creation of the playerRound is determining what handicap value is used for playingHandicap. The correct value is being assigned to this field when based on what is provided to the create playerRound endpoint above.

      3. Updating the playerRound roundTypeId doesn’t change the playingHandicap, so a user isn’t able to move between 9 and 18 hole rounds on the same playerRound at the moment.

        1. however when this value is updated the strokeAdjustments are being moved around correctly for the most part,

          1. Front 9 and Back 9 give the correct strokeAdjustments - with the exception of issue 2 above where the other side gets a few lingering strokes

          2. All 18 is taking the playingHandicap and dividing it in half, which is correct except it’s using the wrong number since the playerHandicap isn’t changing from the creation of the playerRound - point 3.a.iii above

issues.summary: New Interface - PrimaGolf
issues.description:
Build a new interface to PrimaGolf.

Spec available here: http://api.primagolf.fr/v1/documentation.html

Base URL: http://api.primagolf.fr/v1/

All REST requests must contain the following headers:

API-Key:contact @Kyle Young

Club-Key: QgAkTk34WmVHB5tGmYF4GyCkVhY5mtT3 (Dev system)

Content-Type: application/json

Key API calls

- Get availability & pricing
GET /courses/teetimes
Used to retrieve tee times, availability, and pricing (including multiple price points per tee time and support for 9/18 holes).

- Lock a tee time
POST /bookings/lock
Reserves a tee time for a short period (e.g. 15 minutes) while the customer completes their booking. Returns a lock_code that must be used on confirm.

- Confirm a booking
POST /bookings/confirm
Confirms a locked tee time, passing player details and selected prices. Returns one or more booking_ids that must be stored for amendments/cancellation.

- Cancel a booking
DELETE /bookings/cancel
Cancels an existing booking using date, course_id, time and the booking_ids returned from confirm.

Please build the interface with:

- The same Send Email logic used for A8 / A9 / NetGolf

- Tee Sheet Pricing and Course Pricing Distribution Controls

issues.summary: Improve Apple Pay Support when there are multiple processors of the same currency
issues.description:
Our current logic for Apple Pay matches the payment type of Apple Pay to the the processor that has a matching CurrencyCode. The concern is that the some Currency Codes have multiple processors so it could choose the wrong one.

Example:

- CurrencyCode: EUR

- Processor: GNEU-EUR

- Processor: GP-EUR

Expectation:

- For an Apple Pay transaction, we should choose the processor that matches the facility of the tee time

- Scenario:

- FID - 19631

- Book an Apple Pay tee time

- Use the GNEU-EUR processor since that is what is configured at the facility

- Scenario:

- FID - 1951

- Book an Apple Pay tee times

- Use the GP-EUR processor since that is what is configured at the facility

Testing:

- Need to do full regression of Apple Pay

- Hot Deals

- $0 due online course time with payfac for reservations

- $0 due online course time without payfac for reservations

- $ due online course time with payfac for reservations

- $ due online course time without payfac for reservations

- Prepaid course times with and without payfac

- GolfPass Paid

- GolfPass Free

- Renewals

- No Shows

- Specifically test EUR and AUD currency as those were reported with issues in prod

- Find an Apple Pay transaction with one of the old ChargeBys for Apple Pay → Make sure you can refund that after the data cleanup script is run

- Work with Yoel to set up test data

- Do a no show with and old ChargeBy as well

issues.summary: TPO - Trade Price Optimization v2 Floor Violations Bug
issues.description:
Summary

TPO v2 stored procedure RateRecChangeProcessRateSet is writing trade time prices below the allowed minimum defined by business rules.

This creates customer-facing underpricing and revenue leakage on trade times.

Severity

High

Customer-facing prices are being written below contractually allowed minimums.

Business Rules

Per Revenue guidance, the minimum allowed trade-time price for standard soft-floor facilities is:

AllowedMin = MIN(Floor, MinCourseRate × 0.90)

A $0.01 penny tolerance is allowed, meaning prices at AllowedMin - $0.01 are acceptable.

Expected behavior:

- When MinCourseRate × 0.90 >= Floor, the floor is binding and price must not go below Floor - $0.01.

- When MinCourseRate × 0.90 < Floor, competitive-match pricing may apply and TPO may price down to MinCourseRate × 0.90 - $0.01.

- Prices within $0.01 of the allowed minimum are acceptable.

For HasHardFloor = 1, the expected behavior should be verified, but current business understanding is that the contractual floor should be strictly enforced with no competitive-match override.

Bug Details

Two underpricing patterns have been identified:

- Standard soft-floor bug (HasHardFloor = 0): TPO can write prices below MIN(Floor, MinCourseRate × 0.90) beyond the $0.01 tolerance.

- Hard-floor bypass (HasHardFloor = 1): TPO can write prices below the contractual floor where hard-floor enforcement is expected to prevent that.

Suspected Root Cause

The pricing logic appears to have a branch that applies MinCourseOnlineRate × 0.9000 without clamping the result against the configured floor.

When MinCourseOnlineRate × 0.90 is above the floor, the final price should be clamped to the floor. Current logic can write the discounted value directly, resulting in below-floor pricing.

For HasHardFloor = 1, the logic appears to fall through to an existing adjusted rate value. Observed behavior suggests that value may already be below the configured floor before final pricing is written.

Expected Behavior

TPO v2 should never write a trade time price below the allowed minimum, except for the documented $0.01 penny tolerance.

For standard soft-floor facilities:

AllowedMin = MIN(Floor, MinCourseRate × 0.90)

For hard-floor facilities, pending dev/business confirmation:

AllowedMin = Floor

Actual Behavior

TPO v2 can write trade time prices below the allowed minimum.

This occurs in both:

- Standard soft-floor facilities where the configured floor should still be binding.

- Hard-floor facilities where the contractual floor is expected to be inviolable.

Proposed Fix

Update RateRecChangeProcessRateSet so the final adjusted trade price is explicitly clamped to the correct allowed minimum before being written.

Recommended changes:

- Replace any direct MinCourseOnlineRate × 0.9000 assignment with logic that compares the result against the configured floor.

- Ensure the final AdjustedRate cannot fall below the applicable allowed minimum.

- Confirm whether HasHardFloor = 1 must always use Floor as the allowed minimum.

- Preserve the existing $0.01 penny tolerance behavior.

- Review the upstream construction of AdjustedRate to ensure below-floor values are not introduced before the final write.

Acceptance Criteria

- TPO v2 no longer writes prices below MIN(Floor, MinCourseRate × 0.90) for standard soft-floor facilities, except for the $0.01 tolerance.

- TPO v2 no longer writes prices below Floor for hard-floor facilities if hard-floor enforcement is confirmed as strict.

- Competitive-match pricing still works when MinCourseRate × 0.90 < Floor for non-hard-floor facilities.

- Existing penny-tolerance behavior remains intact.

- Diagnostic query returns no current underpriced trade times after fix deployment.

- Dev confirms and documents the intended HasHardFloor = 1 behavior.

Impact

This issue can cause customer-facing underpricing, contractual floor violations, and trade-time revenue leakage.

Validation

After deployment, validate by rerunning the diagnostic query for current and upcoming inventory to confirm no trade time prices are below the applicable allowed minimum.

NOTE: dbo.RateRecCoursePriceCache - Check this
CRMTeeTimeRateRecUpdateRateSet

comments.author: Yoel Martin Pardo
comments.body:
Example provided by @Joshua Marquess :

Apr 15 2026 7:35:25:667PM (UTC) - Tee Time (13691677) was update in 'GNC' by Niamh Doyle (33837).
'Player Rule' was: 14, new value is 0
'Status' was: VACANCY, new value is NOVACANCY
Apr 15 2026 7:32:13:433PM (UTC) - Tee Time (13691677) was update in 'GNC' by Jeremy Myers (30801).
'Online Rate' was: 40.99, new value is 46.00
04/15/2026 7:05 PM: TPO v2 online rate was: 46.00 new rate: 40.99 min course: 50.00
Apr 15 2026 6:50:22:070PM (UTC) - Tee Time (13691677) was update in 'GNC' by Jeremy Myers (30801).
'Online Rate' was: 40.99, new value is 46.00
04/15/2026 3:10 PM: TPO v2 online rate was: 45.99 new rate: 40.99 min course: 50.00
Apr 15 2026 1:40:01:067PM (UTC) - Tee Time (13691677) was update in 'GNC' by Jeremy Myers (30801).
'Online Rate' was: 40.99, new value is 45.99
04/15/2026 11:02 AM: TPO v2 online rate was: 41.99 new rate: 40.99 min course: 50.00
04/14/2026 5:09 PM: TPO v2 online rate was: 46.00 new rate: 41.99 min course: 50.00
Apr 14 2026 3:28:27:343PM (UTC) - Tee Time (13691677) was update in 'GNC' by Niamh Doyle (33837).
'Online Rate' was: 41.99, new value is 46.00
'Initial Rate' was: 41.99, new value is 46.00
Tee time inserted by system based on TeeTimeID: 12167901

Internal note: We have been looking into this issue and we have found that the system updated the online rate from 46.00 to 41.99 and from 46.00 to 40.99 because it is reading some data from these two tables RateRecOverrideRateSet and RateRecDaysOutOverrideRateSet. The stored procedure CRMTeeTimeRateRecUpdateRateSet is looking into table RateRecOverrideRateSet to get the to OverrideFloorRate and if we have that info that will be our FloorRate instead the one that we can see on the Trade Price Matrix, and also looking into RateRecDaysOutOverrideRateSet to get the Rate and use that to determine what the MatrixAdjustment will be.  This is searched by PlayDate, RateSetID, DaysOut, TBID, DWID and TeeTimeRule. This will override the regular configuration that we see on the Trade Price matrix page on GNC. There is history table where we stored data that was used to calculated the new Online Rate, example of the table below:

SELECT * FROM CRMTeeTimeRateRecHistory WITH (NOLOCK) WHERE TTTeeTimeID = 13691677
ORDER BY ModifiedDate desc

We do not really know where on GNC we can see the data inserted into those tables, but we have found that these stored procedures are the one inserted data into those tables:

Stored procedures:

[dbo].[RateRecDemandProcessRateSetBatch]
[dbo].[RateRecDemandProcessRateSet]
[dbo].[RateRecDemandProcess_v2]

The last person that updated stored procedure CRMTeeTimeRateRecUpdateRateSet  was Bryan Geiger. Our guess is that he would be familiarized with this process and we would like to understand the used of these tables in order to provide a prover fix for this issue.

For the example above we found that OverrideFloorRate was $36 and the Rate was 16% and 19%, that was why the Online rate resulted on 41.99 and 40.99 instead of using the floor configured on the Trade Price Matrix that is $46 and the Adjustment Rate that are between 13% and 8%.

We requested some information to DS team to see from where those SPs are begin triggered. We will provide that info once we get it from them.
comments.bodyHtml:
Example provided by @Joshua Marquess :

Apr 15 2026 7:35:25:667PM (UTC) - Tee Time (13691677) was update in 'GNC' by Niamh Doyle (33837).
      'Player Rule' was: 14, new value is 0
      'Status' was: VACANCY, new value is NOVACANCY
Apr 15 2026 7:32:13:433PM (UTC) - Tee Time (13691677) was update in 'GNC' by Jeremy Myers (30801).
      'Online Rate' was: 40.99, new value is 46.00
04/15/2026 7:05 PM: TPO v2 online rate was: 46.00 new rate: 40.99 min course: 50.00
Apr 15 2026 6:50:22:070PM (UTC) - Tee Time (13691677) was update in 'GNC' by Jeremy Myers (30801).
      'Online Rate' was: 40.99, new value is 46.00
04/15/2026 3:10 PM: TPO v2 online rate was: 45.99 new rate: 40.99 min course: 50.00
Apr 15 2026 1:40:01:067PM (UTC) - Tee Time (13691677) was update in 'GNC' by Jeremy Myers (30801).
      'Online Rate' was: 40.99, new value is 45.99
04/15/2026 11:02 AM: TPO v2 online rate was: 41.99 new rate: 40.99 min course: 50.00
04/14/2026 5:09 PM: TPO v2 online rate was: 46.00 new rate: 41.99 min course: 50.00
Apr 14 2026 3:28:27:343PM (UTC) - Tee Time (13691677) was update in 'GNC' by Niamh Doyle (33837).
      'Online Rate' was: 41.99, new value is 46.00
      'Initial Rate' was: 41.99, new value is 46.00
Tee time inserted by system based on TeeTimeID: 12167901

Internal note: We have been looking into this issue and we have found that the system updated the online rate from 46.00 to 41.99 and from 46.00 to 40.99 because it is reading some data from these two tables RateRecOverrideRateSet and RateRecDaysOutOverrideRateSet. The stored procedure CRMTeeTimeRateRecUpdateRateSet is looking into table RateRecOverrideRateSet to get the to OverrideFloorRate and if we have that info that will be our FloorRate instead the one that we can see on the Trade Price Matrix, and also looking into RateRecDaysOutOverrideRateSet to get the Rate and use that to determine what the MatrixAdjustment will be. This is searched by PlayDate, RateSetID, DaysOut, TBID, DWID and TeeTimeRule. This will override the regular configuration that we see on the Trade Price matrix page on GNC. There is history table where we stored data that was used to calculated the new Online Rate, example of the table below:

SELECT * FROM CRMTeeTimeRateRecHistory WITH (NOLOCK) WHERE TTTeeTimeID = 13691677
ORDER BY ModifiedDate desc

We do not really know where on GNC we can see the data inserted into those tables, but we have found that these stored procedures are the one inserted data into those tables:

Stored procedures:

[dbo].[RateRecDemandProcessRateSetBatch]
[dbo].[RateRecDemandProcessRateSet]
[dbo].[RateRecDemandProcess_v2]

The last person that updated stored procedure CRMTeeTimeRateRecUpdateRateSet was Bryan Geiger. Our guess is that he would be familiarized with this process and we would like to understand the used of these tables in order to provide a prover fix for this issue.

For the example above we found that OverrideFloorRate was $36 and the Rate was 16% and 19%, that was why the Online rate resulted on 41.99 and 40.99 instead of using the floor configured on the Trade Price Matrix that is $46 and the Adjustment Rate that are between 13% and 8%.

We requested some information to DS team to see from where those SPs are begin triggered. We will provide that info once we get it from them.

comments.author: Yoel Martin Pardo
comments.body: Internal note: @Joshua Marquess found that records on RateRecOverrideRateSet had wrong floorRate because Saturdays and Sundays should have $46, but Saturdays has $36 while Sundays and Mondays has $46. We took another look at those stored procedures and we found that RateRecDemandProcessRateSetBatch stored procedure is always update the setting the DATEFIRST to be 1 even before the merge (this will update RateRecOverrideRateSet  table or insert new records. Here is the issue) while RateRecDemandProcess_v2 stored procedure updates that to 7 before the merge. Setting that to 1 before the merge is going to cause this issue since it is specifying that the first day of the week is Monday while in the configuration we have it as Sunday.
comments.bodyHtml: Internal note: @Joshua Marquess found that records on RateRecOverrideRateSet had wrong floorRate because Saturdays and Sundays should have $46, but Saturdays has $36 while Sundays and Mondays has $46. We took another look at those stored procedures and we found that RateRecDemandProcessRateSetBatch stored procedure is always update the setting the DATEFIRST to be 1 even before the merge (this will update RateRecOverrideRateSet table or insert new records. Here is the issue) while RateRecDemandProcess_v2 stored procedure updates that to 7 before the merge. Setting that to 1 before the merge is going to cause this issue since it is specifying that the first day of the week is Monday while in the configuration we have it as Sunday.

issues.summary: Add cart/tax include parameters to tee-time/book for EZL (EZTP and EZ-RTS) integration
issues.description:
Summary

CORE needs to support the new cart/tax include parameter contract required by https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/RTS-7614#icft=RTS-7614 so RTS/EZRTS/EZP can receive a per-player/per-slot breakdown during tee-time/book, persist it in ETNv4, and send the correct values to G1/EZSuite POS during tender.

This is part of the forward path on the new booking API. It should not be treated as a legacy API remediation effort.

Check with @Juan Gonzalez
https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/RTS-7614

---
Expected Behavior

- CORE can provide the RTS-required optional cart/tax fields per player slot in the tee-time/book flow.

- When values are present, CORE sends them exactly as calculated/known by CORE.

- When values are absent, CORE omits them or sends null; CORE should not default or fabricate values.

- RTS can use the received values as its exclusive source for the ETNv4 cart/tax breakdown tracked under https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/RTS-7614#icft=RTS-7614.

---
Current Behavior / Problem

- https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/RTS-7614#icft=RTS-7614 is waiting on a CORE-side ticket to track the dependency for sending the new cart/tax parameter set.

- Existing CORE/reservation models include external charge detail concepts, but RTS needs a specific per-slot parameter contract on tee-time/book for greens fee tax, cart fee, cart fee tax, and partial-prepay status.

- Without CORE sending this breakdown, RTS cannot reliably persist the values needed for EZTP/EZRTS tender flows without deriving from unreliable ETNv4 fields.

---
Scope Guardrails

- Include the CORE-side request/DTO/model/mapping work needed to support the RTS-required parameter set on the new tee-time/book API path.

- Include tests proving optional/null behavior and value preservation.

- Do not implement a separate fix for the legacy booking API. For legacy API impact, the intended resolution is migration/use of the new API path.

- Do not require RTS storage, ETNv4 schema changes, or POS tender packet changes in this CORE ticket; those are tracked by https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/RTS-7614#icft=RTS-7614.

- Do not derive, supplement, or fall back to ETNv4 fields such as dbo.TeeOffTransactionAuditItem, dbo.CCAuthorization, or dbo.TTPrepaid.PrepaidFeeAmount.

---
Requested Parameters

All fields are optional and should be represented per slot/player where the booking contract supports slot-level detail.

- includes_greens_fee_tax

- greens_fee_tax_pct

- greens_fee_tax_amount_ppd

- greens_fee_tax_amount_due

- includes_cart_fee

- cart_fee_amount_ppd

- cart_fee_amount_due

- includes_cart_fee_tax

- cart_fee_tax_pct

- cart_fee_tax_amount_ppd

- cart_fee_tax_amount_due

- is_partial_prepaid

---
Technical Notes

- Source dependency: https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/RTS-7614#icft=RTS-7614, "Support Cart/Tax Include Parameters on tee-time/book".

- Existing CORE code paths that may be relevant:

- DotNet2/TeeTimesAPI/GolfNow.API.2.1/GolfNow.API.Business/Reservation/MakeReservationRequest.cs

- DotNet2/TeeTimesAPI/GolfNow.API.2.1/GolfNow.API.Business/Reservation/Workflow/MakeReservation/Activities/MakeReservationActivity.cs

- DotNet2/TeeTimesAPI/GolfNow.API.2.1/GolfNow.API.Contracts/DataContracts/Order/Items/ExternalChargeDetails.cs

- DotNet2/TeeTimesAPI/GolfNow.API.2.1/GolfNow.API.Contracts/DataContracts/Order/Items/ExternalChargeLineItem.cs

- DotNet2/TeeTimesAPI/GolfNow.API.2.1/GolfNow.API.Service/Converters/ServiceBusinessMapingExtensions.cs

- Internal Services/GolfNow.Services.Reservation/GolfNow.Services.Reservation.Provider/Workflow/Reservation/Activities/External/ApplyExternalChargeDetailsActivity.cs

- Current local model names already include ExternalChargeDetails and ExternalChargeLineItem; engineering should decide whether to extend that structure or add a dedicated RTS-facing cart/tax breakdown model.

---
Acceptance Criteria

Existing Behavior Preserved

- Existing booking flows continue to work when no cart/tax include parameters are supplied.

- Existing external charge detail behavior is not regressed for current callers.

New Behavior

- [ ] CORE supports all RTS-requested parameters above as optional values in the new tee-time/book flow.

- [ ] CORE sends provided values through to RTS without recalculating, defaulting, or deriving from ETNv4 fallback fields.

- [ ] Omitted parameters remain absent/null downstream.

- [ ] Per-slot/player data remains associated with the correct booked player/slot.

- [ ] Unit and/or integration coverage validates populated, partially populated, and omitted parameter sets.

Not Included Unless Product Expands Scope

- No legacy booking API remediation is included in this story.

- No RTS ETNv4 persistence or POS tender payload implementation is included in this story.

---
Test Matrix

Scenario

Expected Result

tee-time/book with all cart/tax fields populated

Values are accepted and passed downstream exactly as supplied.

tee-time/book with only greens fee tax values populated

Populated values pass downstream; omitted cart/cart tax values remain null/absent.

tee-time/book with no new fields

Existing booking behavior remains unchanged.

Multi-player booking with different per-slot values

Each slot retains its own cart/tax breakdown.

Partial prepaid booking

is_partial_prepaid and ppd/due splits reflect the supplied values.

---
Open Questions / Product Decisions

- Confirm the final wire-format and field casing expected by RTS for tee-time/book.

- Confirm whether the CORE source of truth should be the existing ExternalChargeDetails/line-item model or a dedicated cart/tax include DTO.

- Confirm whether is_partial_prepaid should remain a Boolean even though https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/RTS-7614#icft=RTS-7614 currently describes both not-prepaid and fully-prepaid as No.

---
Related

- https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/RTS-7614#icft=RTS-7614

- https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/RTS-4339#icft=RTS-4339

- https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/TTSUPPORT-7211#icft=TTSUPPORT-7211

Communication Note

For https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/TTSUPPORT-7307#icft=TTSUPPORT-7307/new-vs-legacy wording: CORE resolved the issue on the new booking API path. The intended resolution for legacy API exposure is migration/use of the new API, not a separate legacy investigation under this ticket.

comments.author: Tyler Olechnowicz
comments.body:
QA Test Instructions

- Book a tee time reservation using a rate set configured for the EZL tee sheet and the new EZL API.

- Verify the reservation is created successfully.

- Coordinate with the EZL team to review the tee-time/book request received by EZL.

- Confirm the new prepaid_and_tax fields are present and populated as expected by EZL.

- Repeat the test with:

- A tax-exclusive tee time.

- A tax-inclusive tee time.

- For both scenarios, validate with the EZL team that the fee, tax, and tax-inclusion values received by EZL match the expected reservation pricing.
comments.bodyHtml:
QA Test Instructions

  1. Book a tee time reservation using a rate set configured for the EZL tee sheet and the new EZL API.

  2. Verify the reservation is created successfully.

  3. Coordinate with the EZL team to review the tee-time/book request received by EZL.

  4. Confirm the new prepaid_and_tax fields are present and populated as expected by EZL.

  5. Repeat the test with:

    • A tax-exclusive tee time.

    • A tax-inclusive tee time.

  6. For both scenarios, validate with the EZL team that the fee, tax, and tax-inclusion values received by EZL match the expected reservation pricing.

issues.summary: reIndexByRange Scorecard endpoint is not triggering USGA Mapping process
issues.description:
The current process of mapping USGA courses to their Compete counter parts in our DB is to:

- populate the GolfCourse.CourseMapping table in the compete DB with a file provided by our course mapping process - a tool developed by Yu-Sheng Tu that takes a course export from the USGA side and matches their course information up with the like course record on our side - or a backup of the table from dev, exported before a rebase.

- After that table is populated with those matched up records the courses need to be run through the GolfNow.Services.Scorecard - /api/searchAdministration/golfCourses/reIndexByRange end point.

- This call sends the course id’s specified in the range parameters to RabbitMQ to process and update the courses data and data source in the DB.

This second step is currently not working as expected. The course range that is getting submitted to RabbitMQ is not getting reIndexed correctly. Records in the support site logs are showing the job being submitted, however the records that should be updated to the USGA sourced data are not.

If these same records are reIndexed individually through the same process but the individual CourseId endpoint here - /api/searchAdministration/golfCourses/{golfCourseId} - then the process works as expected.

This will need to be resolved before going live with the USGA integration so we are able to index all of the courses in production.

may have been an issue caused by the AWS cutover

http://scorecard-dev.golfnowdmz.local/api/searchAdministration/golfCourses/reIndexByRange?start=1&end=1000&indexOnly=true

  2. After that table is populated with those matched up records the courses need to be run through the GolfNow.Services.Scorecard - /api/searchAdministration/golfCourses/reIndexByRange end point.

If these same records are reIndexed individually through the same process but the individual CourseId endpoint here - /api/searchAdministration/golfCourses/{golfCourseId} - then the process works as expected.

This will need to be resolved before going live with the USGA integration so we are able to index all of the courses in production.

issues.summary: (Compete) Keep playerRoundType and OutingRoundType in sync at all times
issues.description:
- roundType is NOT being updated when the dynamic playingHandicap value is updated

- must have a way to keep outing and playeround data be in sync, especially where GPA services are concerned

- playerRoundType; OutingRoundType

Background

- a user sets up an outing with a specific number of holes, and therefore OutingRoundType

- All 18, Front 9 only, Back 9 only

- the user, and also graph/scorecard, have the ability to change the resulting PlayerRoundTypes

- if that PlayerRoundType is changed, the OutingRoundType is now out of sync

- the OutingRoundType dictates which type of score we’re sending to the USGA for posting scores

- if there is a mismatch between the given player round type and outing round type, we will send the wrong course teemarker and scoring details to the USGA

- the score will be rejected or post the wrong data successfully--both bad

@Alex McNay will help by adding examples of each object and any additional clarity in Expectation

https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-13962?focusedCommentId=877420

@Matthew Heins can assist with needs from the frontend perspective
issues.descriptionHtml:
• roundType is NOT being updated when the dynamic playingHandicap value is updated

    • must have a way to keep outing and playeround data be in sync, especially where GPA services are concerned

    • playerRoundType; OutingRoundType

Background

  1. a user sets up an outing with a specific number of holes, and therefore OutingRoundType

    1. All 18, Front 9 only, Back 9 only

  2. the user, and also graph/scorecard, have the ability to change the resulting PlayerRoundTypes

  3. if that PlayerRoundType is changed, the OutingRoundType is now out of sync

  4. the OutingRoundType dictates which type of score we’re sending to the USGA for posting scores

  5. if there is a mismatch between the given player round type and outing round type, we will send the wrong course teemarker and scoring details to the USGA

    1. the score will be rejected or post the wrong data successfully--both bad

@Alex McNay will help by adding examples of each object and any additional clarity in Expectation

https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-13962?focusedCommentId=877420

@Matthew Heins can assist with needs from the frontend perspective
