issues.summary: FeeMessaging Not Working After Move
Update SFMC Confirmation Email APIs
issues.description:
In Notification Service we need to change the SFMC API from the current one to the new TransactionalSend API documented in the attached file.

- MIDS (These should be the same as the current ones but providing to be safe):

- GN US:

- TeeOff:

- GN UK:

- GN AU:

- GN EU:

- We should probably create a new provider for this new API so we can fall back to our current structure if something goes wrong in prod

- We need the ability to send golfer and golf course emails with the new api

- We need the ability to send simulator and regular golf course emails

- We need the ability to send reservation confirmations and cancellation emails

- Send one big payload that includes all info for golfer, golf course, simulator ,etc

- This payload should have been created in https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-13647

- Document to SFMC team which fields to ignore

- The SFMC team will handle logic on which path to go down depending on the fields in the payload

- For GolfCourse emails, we need to send a separate payload to each facility email address. SFMC does not handle sending comma separated lists of emails addresses

- **Mike Corliss says we have the ability to send to multiple recipients. Please work with him on how to execute

- With the new API, we need to update the logic that currently stores requestKey in our db and instead we need to store messageKey

- This is the primary key that allows for better service and support

Release Plan:

- Like last years implementation, we will launch GN EU first so we can validate in prod

- We will slowly release other Channels post release validation

issues.summary: Update EZ Legacy API Call (AddConfirmationNote) to Include Param5 – SponsorID
issues.description:
Summary

When calling the EZ Legacy API endpoint AddConfirmationNote, we are currently not passing Param5 (SponsorID).

We need to update the API call to include this parameter going forward.

---
Background

Endpoint: AddConfirmationNote

Current Parameters:

- Param1 – InternetUserID

- Param2 – InternetPassword

- Param3 – ConfirmID

- Param4 – ConfirmationNote

- Param5 – SponsorID (int) ← Currently not being sent

Example documentation snippet shows:

Param5  SponsorID  int

However, current logs confirm the call only sends up through Param4 (ConfirmationNote).

---
Requested Change

Modify the EZ Legacy API call to include:

Param5 = SponsorID

This value should be populated appropriately based on the booking context.

---
Reason for Change

The EZ Dev Team is implementing a workaround related to recent Hot Deal work.
Passing SponsorID will help ensure related functionality works properly and prevent edge case issues.

---
Acceptance Criteria

-  AddConfirmationNote API call includes Param5.

-  SponsorID is correctly populated and passed as an integer.

-  No regression issues with confirmation note creation.

-  Verified in logs that Param5 is being transmitted.

We need to update the Process Payment workflow to allow Booking Engine users to not send a payment method for the following:

issues.summary: Add IsCaddyRequested parameter support to EZL/EZLRTS tee sheet interface
issues.description:
Background

In 2023, we added support for passing the IsCaddyRequested parameter as a pass-through to BRS and BRSG1 tee sheet interfaces to support ClubUp (caddy service) integrations. However, this parameter was not added to the EZL tee sheet interface.

Current State

- ✅ BRS tee sheet: Passes IsCaddyRequested parameter (line 163 in BRSTeeSheet.cs)

- ✅ BRSG1 tee sheet: Passes IsCaddyRequested parameter (line 199 in BRSG1TeeSheet.cs)

- ❌ EZL tee sheet: Does NOT pass IsCaddyRequested parameter

Issue

The EZL tee sheet (code: "EZL") does not handle the IsCaddyRequested parameter when making reservations. This means courses using EZLRTS tee sheet systems cannot indicate caddy requests through our booking flow, creating an inconsistent experience across different tee sheet providers.

Expected Behavior

When a booking is made through the EZL tee sheet interface with IsCaddyRequested = true, the caddie request should be submitted to the EZLinks Integrators API via the dedicated Caddie Request endpoint (see RTS-2163), similar to how BRS and BRSG handle it through their respective interfaces.

---
Technical Details

Important Update (per Viitalii Slobodianyk): A completely new endpoint was added in RTS-2163 to support caddie requests for EZL/EZRTS. The EZL implementation should use this dedicated endpoint rather than adding a parameter to the existing booking payload.

⚠️ BRS/BRSG1 must remain untouched. BRS and BRSG1 are still using the old method (passing IsCaddyRequested directly in the booking payload). Do NOT modify BRS/BRSG1 — only EZL will use the new Caddie Request API endpoint.

New Caddie Request Endpoint (from RTS-2163):

- Type: POST

- URL path: Under the "Tee Time" path in the Integrators API

- GrantArea: APIReservationSystem

- Input Parameters:

- SponsorID (required)

- ConfirmID (required)

- Caddie-Requested (required, Boolean)

- Behavior: Writes value to ETNv4.dbo.TTQuick.QuickEntryCaddy (False = 0, True = 1)

- Response: Indicates success or failure

- Failure reasons: missing parameters, SponsorID lacks access to course, ConfirmID doesn't exist

File to modify: Framework/GolfNow.Framework.TeeSheets/Interfaces/EZL/EZLinksTeeSheet.cs

Method to update: BookTeeTimeAsync (starting around line 2208)

What's needed: After a successful booking, if request.TeeTime.IsCaddyRequested is true, call the Caddie Request POST endpoint with the SponsorID and the ConfirmID from the booking response.

Reference implementations:

- BRSTeeSheet.cs:163 (old method — DO NOT CHANGE)

- BRSG1TeeSheet.cs:199 (old method — DO NOT CHANGE)

- RTS-2163 — Caddie Request endpoint spec (EZL uses this)

---
Acceptance Criteria

- After a successful EZL booking with IsCaddyRequested = true, the Caddie Request endpoint is called with the appropriate SponsorID and ConfirmID

- Parameter defaults to false/0 when not explicitly set (no endpoint call needed)

- BRS and BRSG1 implementations remain unchanged (they use the existing pass-through method)

- Existing EZL bookings continue to work without regression

- Handles endpoint failure gracefully (booking should not fail if caddie request fails)

---
Additional Notes: The parameter is already available in the TR API (Reservation.IsCaddyRequested) and flows through to the MakeReservationRequest object, so the plumbing exists—we just need to call the new Caddie Request endpoint after a successful EZL booking when the flag is set.

issues.summary: Update cancellation/refund logic to refund player slots in descending order
issues.description:
Reservation Service currently handles cancellations by cancelling the highest player number first. For example in a 4 player reservation. A one player cancellation will remove the 4th slot. However when refunding, specifically in SendOrderToInterface workflows and sending data back to BRSG/EZRTS, we refund player slot 1 first. So this creates a mismatch. We need to update refund logic to match cancellation logic.

- VendorService GetBookingInfo now has a Status field

- cancelled = refund eligible

- active = refund eligible

- refunded = already refunded and not eligible

- Logic

- When refunding, look for the player slot numbers in descending order and refund the amount with the largest slot number with a status of cancelled

- If no statuses are set to cancelled, look for the player slot numbers in descending order and refund the amount with the largest slot number with a status of active

- If no statuses of cancelled or active exist, throw an exception - This would likely be a super edge case - please confirm with Desimo and B2B QA if this is even possible

- Happy Path Testing

- Book a 4 player reservation with Send Order to Interface Enabled

- Cancel and refund one player through GNC

- Expect to see the 4th player slot removed from the tee sheet

- Expect to see player 4s green fee refunded in the Order

- Work with B2B QA on various other testing scenarios and tee sheet validation
issues.descriptionHtml:
Reservation Service currently handles cancellations by cancelling the highest player number first. For example in a 4 player reservation. A one player cancellation will remove the 4th slot. However when refunding, specifically in SendOrderToInterface workflows and sending data back to BRSG/EZRTS, we refund player slot 1 first. So this creates a mismatch. We need to update refund logic to match cancellation logic.

  1. VendorService GetBookingInfo now has a Status field

    1. cancelled = refund eligible

    2. active = refund eligible

    3. refunded = already refunded and not eligible

  2. Logic

    1. When refunding, look for the player slot numbers in descending order and refund the amount with the largest slot number with a status of cancelled

      1. If no statuses are set to cancelled, look for the player slot numbers in descending order and refund the amount with the largest slot number with a status of active

      2. If no statuses of cancelled or active exist, throw an exception - This would likely be a super edge case - please confirm with Desimo and B2B QA if this is even possible

  3. Happy Path Testing

    1. Book a 4 player reservation with Send Order to Interface Enabled

    2. Cancel and refund one player through GNC

    3. Expect to see the 4th player slot removed from the tee sheet

    4. Expect to see player 4s green fee refunded in the Order

  4. Work with B2B QA on various other testing scenarios and tee sheet validation

issues.summary: [Commerce] Add IsCaddyRequested to Commerce Proxy
issues.description:
On API 2.1 and TransparentRedirect, we currently support a pass through for IsCaddyRequested that submits the data for BRS/BRSG tee sheets. EZL/EZRTS on the otherhand has a brand new API to support Caddy Requests. We need Commerce to support both the legacy BRS version as well as the new EZL API version.

BRS/BRSG

TR Add Reservation currently has Reservation.IsCaddyRequested. We need an equivalent request field for the Commerce Proxy so BE teams can submit caddy requests.

- Field Name: IsCaddyRequested

- Data Type: Boolean

Validate testing on both API and BE UI (Coordinate with B2B team)

EZL/EZRTS

curl --location 'https://integrations-qa.np.ezlinksgolf.com/v1/tee-time/caddie-request' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \

issues.descriptionHtml:
On API 2.1 and TransparentRedirect, we currently support a pass through for IsCaddyRequested that submits the data for BRS/BRSG tee sheets. EZL/EZRTS on the otherhand has a brand new API to support Caddy Requests. We need Commerce to support both the legacy BRS version as well as the new EZL API version.

BRS/BRSG

TR Add Reservation currently has Reservation.IsCaddyRequested. We need an equivalent request field for the Commerce Proxy so BE teams can submit caddy requests.

  • Field Name: IsCaddyRequested

  • Data Type: Boolean

Validate testing on both API and BE UI (Coordinate with B2B team)

EZL/EZRTS

curl --location 'https://integrations-qa.np.ezlinksgolf.com/v1/tee-time/caddie-request' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--header 'Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..jz0QJ2hA1a2DjOWDL2F2Yg0VSruI-crurFBgh4vnpaE' \
--data '{
  "sponsor_id": 4279,
  "confirm_id": 346024001,
  "caddie_requested": true
}'

  • Additional Details in https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/RTS-2163

  • Related CORE ticket to support: https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-14396

Testing:

  • Confirm BRS/BRSG successfully submits caddie request to tee sheet

  • Confirm EZL/EZRTS successfully submits caddie request to tee sheet

  • This is not a required field

  • Failure in the Caddie Request should not fail the reservation

  • Work with the B2B team to confirm successful submission to both tee sheet platforms

issues.summary: Configure CertificateGuid to retrieve Apple Pay Payment Method Key.
issues.description: We need to configure the CertificateGuid on our side so it can be used to retrieve the Apple Pay Payment Method Key. This will be set per environment and will not need to be passed from the UI.
issues.descriptionHtml: We need to configure the CertificateGuid on our side so it can be used to retrieve the Apple Pay Payment Method Key. This will be set per environment and will not need to be passed from the UI.

comments.author: Yoel Martin Pardo
comments.body:
QA: This ticket is to have the CertificateGuid stored on CRMConfig table and use it to get the PaymentMethodKey from payment processing. You do not need to pass the CertificateGuid on the ApplePaymentMethod, but either if you pass that in, we use whatever is on the config.

Important note: Please run the update statement below to set the proper CertificateGuid on the environment:

USE[CRM]
GO

UPDATE dbo.CRMConfig
SET [Value] = '5BBE48CA-4866-4253-9A08-79B0535447B9'
WHERE Entity = 'PaymentProcessing'
AND EntityID = 'CertificateGuid'
AND [Key] = 'ApplePayCertificateGuid'

GO

issues.summary: CLONE - [Commerce] Issue with displaying and making a reservation when tax is inclusive
issues.description:
Environment: QA

- There is a rate set where Is Tax Included checkbox is checked in GNC Admin

STR:

- Navigate to https://xyz-david-clark-smartplay-commerce.qa-phx-ui-be.svc.golf/?date=2025-12-30

- Log in

- Select a tee time belonging to the rate set from the preconditions

- Add to cart and proceed to checkout

- View the Summary section > estimated tax

AR: The tax value > 0 but it’s not the total tax value. In the provided video it’s 20 cents less than the accurate tax value

If a user attempts to complete such a purchase, the process-payment call fails with the following error:

{error: "Unknown error occurred. Error Track ID: 79909608-b689-42aa-b634-98db17de5282",…}

code: "UNKNOWN_ERROR"

error: "Unknown error occurred. Error Track ID: 79909608-b689-42aa-b634-98db17de5282"

Note: Is reproducing both for a prepaid and a non-prepay rate set

ER: If tax is inclusive it should not be displayed neither at checkout nor on the confirmation page. User should be able to complete a reservation without errors

comments.bodyHtml:
Slack thread - https://versant-golfnow.slack.com/archives/C09MHR4RJ3F/p1776174113607999

payments.api-payment-processing processed the request at the SingleItemController.PartialCredit endpoint span. During execution, the provider method PartialTransactionItemCredit threw an exception (either a PaymentProviderException or ProcessFailureException). The global ApiExceptionFilterAttribute caught this exception, logged it via POST log.dev.golfnowdmz.com/api/v1/logging/logError span, and returned HTTP 400. The DetermineHttpStatusCode method in the exception filter defaults most error types to HttpStatusCode.BadRequest.

issues.summary: Home Course Selection/Management
issues.description:
We need to create the ability to assign a Home Course for a user and manage the restrictions around Home Course changes.

Requirements

- One customer can have a configurable amount of GolfFacilityIds assigned as Home Course

- Create a platform configuration for maximum number of Home Courses Allowed

- Configuration should be set to 1 for launch but we should support >1 in the future where a customer could potentially have more than 1

- Do not allow selection of Master Facilities

- Create an exclusion list of GolfFacilityIds just in case certain courses refuse to participate

- If a user selects a course on this list, throw an error saying it is not applicable for Home Course selection

- In Prod, this should be empty but we should test by adding some in Dev

- Log the CreatedTimeStamp, LastModifiedTimestamp and LastModifiedBy for selection and modifications

- LastModifiedBy should be the CustomerNumber or CustomerID or SystemUserId if it was done by a service member through GNC

- Create a history log of the changes made for each customer

- Home Course Selection/Modifications

- Any Full Customer without an applicable membership (without a product with BenefitID ##) or no membership at all (Created in https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-14428)

- You are allowed two selections/modifications in a 1 year period

- One initial selection would start the 1 year clock

- One additional modification within that 1 year period

- Purchasing a product with BenefitId ##

- This will restart the 1 year period with the start date matching the subscription start date

- This new period will exclude any previous modifications and restart at 2

- Multiple purchases of a product within the 1 year period will not affect the date range

- Example: The initial purchase of a monthly product will start the 1 year period but the renewal of the monthly product will not restart at the end of 30 days, it will still be the original 1 year period. After the 1 year period of 12 renewed monthly memberships, you will then start a new 1 year

- As a user that has already selected their home course and they either purchase an applicable product, renew their applicable product or are a non member and have crossed the 365 day mark

- Your previous selection will remain as is and roll over to the new period as a new Home Course record

- You are allowed two modifications with the new 365 day period

- Monthly product renewals do not reset the modification count or 1 year period

- Guest customers should not be able to select a Home Course

- GNC should have the ability to override the Home Course selection at any time for support purposes

- GNC Modifications should be logged in Change Tracking

- GNC Modifications should not count towards the two allowed modification count

- If you have two stacked memberships with one starting in the future, you should not be able to make modifications to the future dated membership Home Course until the subscription starts

- Future dated memberships are now out of scope since all users get a 365 day period no matter what

- There should be an optional reason for choosing a home course

- Reasons:

- Convenience/location

- Price/value

- Course conditions

- Social/community

- Course amenities

- Personal history/familiarity

- There should be an optional reason for modifying home course

- Reasons:

- Course closed

- Course conditions deteriorated

- I moved

- Snow bird

- Accidental selection

- Course no longer fits my wants/needs

Deliverables

- An API for frontend to set a Home Course

- Needs ChannelId in the request so we can store where the Home Course was set/modified

- An API for frontend to modify a Home Course selection

- Needs ChannelId in the request so we can store where the Home Course was set/modified

- Error messaging around modification limit restrictions

- You have reached your Home Course modification limit. Please reach out to GolfNow Support for further assistance.

- Error messaging around selecting the same facility twice when modifying

- [FacilityName] is already selected as your Home Course.

- Can GetCustomer and GetCustomerSubscription APIs also return the Home Course GolfFacilityId? This is a nice to have

- A GNC UI for managing/Supporting Home Course - https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-14437

We need to create the ability to assign a Home Course for a user and manage the restrictions around Home Course changes.

Requirements

  • Home Course Selection/Modifications

After the 1 year period of 12 renewed monthly memberships, you will then start a new 1 year

    • As a user that has already selected their home course and they either purchase an applicable product, renew their applicable product or are a non member and have crossed the 365 day mark

      • Your previous selection will remain as is and roll over to the new period as a new Home Course record

      • You are allowed two modifications with the new 365 day period

      • Monthly product renewals do not reset the modification count or 1 year period

    • Guest customers should not be able to select a Home Course

    • GNC should have the ability to override the Home Course selection at any time for support purposes

    • If you have two stacked memberships with one starting in the future, you should not be able to make modifications to the future dated membership Home Course until the subscription starts

      • Future dated memberships are now out of scope since all users get a 365 day period no matter what

  • There should be an optional reason for choosing a home course

    • Reasons:

      • Convenience/location

      • Price/value

      • Course conditions

      • Social/community

      • Course amenities

      • Personal history/familiarity

    • There should be an optional reason for modifying home course

      • Reasons:

        • Course closed

        • Course conditions deteriorated

        • I moved

        • Snow bird

        • Accidental selection

        • Course no longer fits my wants/needs

Deliverables

  • An API for frontend to set a Home Course

    • Needs ChannelId in the request so we can store where the Home Course was set/modified

  • An API for frontend to modify a Home Course selection

    • Needs ChannelId in the request so we can store where the Home Course was set/modified

  • Error messaging around modification limit restrictions

    • You have reached your Home Course modification limit. Please reach out to GolfNow Support for further assistance.

  • Error messaging around selecting the same facility twice when modifying

    • [FacilityName] is already selected as your Home Course.

  • Can GetCustomer and GetCustomerSubscription APIs also return the Home Course GolfFacilityId? This is a nice to have

  • A GNC UI for managing/Supporting Home Course - https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-14437

comments.author: Luis Vallejo Mursuli
comments.body:
Hi @Brian Pugh  and @Daniel McNaughton ,

I’ve been reviewing CORE-14427 in detail to prepare for implementation, and I want to make sure we align on a few areas where the requirements are currently ambiguous or open-ended. Clarifying these upfront will help avoid rework and ensure the APIs and data model are designed correctly for both launch and future extensibility.

---
1. Data Model / ReferenceId

- The ticket mentions ReferenceId = CustomerSubscriptionId for members, and possibly 1 (or another value) for non-members to avoid nulls.

- Should ReferenceId be nullable for non-members, or do we want a sentinel value?

- If using a sentinel, should we instead model this explicitly (e.g., IsMember flag or ReferenceType) to avoid overloading semantics?

---
2. Benefit Eligibility

Since I’m also assigned to CORE-14428, and it needs to be implemented in the same sprint, I want to clarify the eligibility rule before wiring it into CORE-14427.

CORE-14428 asks us to insert the new "Home Course Eligible" benefit and associate it with the applicable products. Should CORE-14427 rely only on that new benefit to determine whether a user is eligible for member-based Home Course behavior, or are there any other existing/current benefits that should also be considered?

---
3. Selection / Modification Limits

There appears to be a potential inconsistency:

- Non-member:

- “Two selections/modifications in a 365-day period” (initial + 1 modification)

- Member:

- “Two selections per subscription period” (initial + 1 modification)

- Later:

- “You are allowed two modifications with the new period”

Can we confirm:

- Is the total allowance 2 actions per period (initial + 1 modification)?

- Or initial selection + 2 modifications (3 total actions) after rollover?

---
4. Rollover Behavior

- When a user renews or crosses the 365-day mark:

- A new Home Course record is created and previous selection “rolls over”

Questions:

- What event triggers the rollover? (subscription renewal event, scheduled job, lazy creation on next request, etc.)

- Does the rollover count as the initial selection of the new period?

- If a user does nothing (no modification), do they still retain full modification allowance?

---
5. Future-Dated Memberships

- Users cannot modify Home Course for a future subscription until it starts

Questions:

- How do we determine the active subscription context when multiple exist?

- What happens if:

- Current subscription is active and future one exists → user modifies now?

- Future subscription starts → do we automatically create a new record?

- What is the expected error response when trying to modify a future-dated record?

---
6. Multiple Home Courses (Future Support)

- Configurable max is 1 at launch, but should support >1 in future

Questions:

- Should we design APIs and DB to support multiple Home Courses now (array-based), even if config = 1?

- Are modification limits:

- Per customer globally, or

- Per Home Course slot, or

- Per subscription period regardless of count?

---
7. GNC Override Behavior

- GNC can override “at any time”

Questions:

- Does override bypass:

- Modification limits?

- Future membership restrictions?

- Exclusion list?

- Master facility restriction?

- Should overrides:

- Require a reason?

- Reset modification counters?

- Be flagged differently in audit/history?

---
8. Exclusion List

I already know how to identify whether a facility is a Master Facility, so the main open question here is around the exclusion list.

Should the exclusion list of GolfFacilityIds already exist somewhere, or do we need to include it as part of the data model/configuration changes for this work?

Additionally, should this exclusion list be managed through database/configuration only, or should there eventually be an admin/GNC management path for it?

---
9. Audit / History Logging

- We log Created/Modified timestamps and “LastModifiedBy”

Questions:

- Should we standardize LastModifiedBy as a single identifier type (e.g., always CustomerId or SystemUserId)?

- Should history include:

- Old value vs new value

- Reason (if provided)

- Source (frontend vs GNC)

- ReferenceId / period context?

---
10. API Contracts

The ticket lists APIs but does not define contracts.

We need clarity on:

- Request/response structure for:

- Set Home Course

- Modify Home Course

- Idempotency:

- If user selects the same course again → does it count as modification?

- Error responses:

- Modification limit reached

- Future membership restriction

- Invalid facility (master/excluded)

---
11. Integration with Existing APIs

- Question raised: should GetCustomer and GetCustomerSubscription return Home Course?

Can we confirm:

- Is this required for launch?

- Should we return:

- Single GolfFacilityId, or

- Full Home Course object(s)?

---
12. Acceptance Criteria / Edge Cases

To ensure completeness, we should confirm expected behavior for:

- 3rd modification attempt → error

- Rollover without user interaction

- Switching from non-member → member

- Membership expiration → non-member behavior

- Stacked memberships with overlapping dates

---
If we can align on these points, I can proceed with a concrete design (data model + API contracts + validation flow) and avoid rework later.

Thanks!

9. Audit / History Logging

10. API Contracts

11. Integration with Existing APIs

Can we confirm:

12. Acceptance Criteria / Edge Cases

To ensure completeness, we should confirm expected behavior for:

  • 3rd modification attempt → error

If we can align on these points, I can proceed with a concrete design (data model + API contracts + validation flow) and avoid rework later.

issues.summary: Support Product Refunds
issues.description:
We need an API that will allow front end clients to submit refunds with a cancellation of a product. This is to support new laws related to products with price increases. We already have API2.1 endpoint for cancellation and we have internal service APIs for refunding.

Related APIs

- {{api-base-url}}/GolfNowAPI.svc/rest/channel/331/subscription/2448350/cancel?cancelSubscriptionIfBenefitsHaveBeenUsed=true

- {{ProductServiceURL}}/SubscriptionService.svc/rest/subscription/adjust

Requirements

- Ability to cancel a subscription

- Ability to cancel a subscription even if benefits have been used

- Ability to fully refund a subscription

- Ability to partially refund a subscription

- This is a “nice to have feature” but since its already supported on InternalService, figured we could future proof it

- Cancellation should trigger a cancellation email that includes refund details

- Cancellation and refund should update in database and GNC History widget

Refund Types:

Full refund =0

Partial = 1

For full refunds RefundAmount is not required

Related APIs

  • {{api-base-url}}/GolfNowAPI.svc/rest/channel/331/subscription/2448350/cancel?cancelSubscriptionIfBenefitsHaveBeenUsed=true

  • {{ProductServiceURL}}/SubscriptionService.svc/rest/subscription/adjust

Requirements

  • Ability to cancel a subscription

  • Ability to cancel a subscription even if benefits have been used

  • Ability to fully refund a subscription

  • Ability to partially refund a subscription

    • This is a “nice to have feature” but since its already supported on InternalService, figured we could future proof it

  • Cancellation should trigger a cancellation email that includes refund details

  • Cancellation and refund should update in database and GNC History widget

Refund Types:

Full refund =0

Partial = 1

For full refunds RefundAmount is not required

issues.summary: Create endpoint to validate cancellation eligibility for Products
issues.description:
There is a new law that states when the price increases of a renewing product, we must give the customer 15 days post-renewal to fully cancel and refund their subscription. This ticket is to allow the front end clients to know when to display the button.

- Probably best to add a field to the response of this endpoint:

-  https://api.gnsvc.com/GolfNowAPI.svc/rest/channel/331/customers/{{EmailAddress}}/subscriptions?productTypeId=9

Requirements

- Create a boolean flag for something like “IsEligibleForCancellation”

- Eligibility for Cancellation/Refund

- Active subscriptions

- ProductTypeId=9

- RenewedInto ProductId IN (71)

- This should be configurable because new ProductIds may be added later

- The subscription was created as part of a renewal - Automatic renewal through the processor or manual through GNC

- Manually renewing through GNC is not eligible for cancellations

- Billing State/Prov is in a configured state

- New York will be the only one for now but this could expand to multiple state or multiple countries

- Today’s date is <= 15 days since the purchase/transaction date of the subscription

- This day limit should be configurable

Test Cases

- Happy Path for Eligibility

- Today’s Date = 3/6/26

- I previously had PPID 64 and i renewed into a PPID under ProductId 71 on purchase/transaction date 3/1/26

- My Billing Address State for the subscription is in New York

- IsEligibleForCancellation=True

- Not a valid State or date

- Today’s Date = 3/6/26

- I previously had PPID 64 and i renewed into a PPID under ProductId 71 on purchase/transaction date 12/31/25

- My Billing Address State for the subscription is in Florida

- IsEligibleForCancellation=False

- Not within 15 day range

- Today’s Date = 3/6/26

- I previously had PPID 64 and i renewed into a PPID under ProductId 71 on purchase/transaction date 12/31/25

- My Billing Address State for the subscription is in New York

- IsEligibleForCancellation=False

- Not an applicable renewal PPID

- Today’s Date = 3/6/26

- I previously had PPID 64 and i renewed into a PPID under ProductId 36 on purchase/transaction date 3/1/26

- My Billing Address State for the subscription is in New York

- IsEligibleForCancellation=False

- Not an applicable transaction - Not a renewal

- Today’s Date = 3/6/26

- I did a standalone purchase of a PPID under ProductId 71 on purchase/transaction date 3/6/26

- My Billing Address State for the subscription is in New York

- IsEligibleForCancellation=False

- Subscription already cancelled or expired

- IsEligibleForCancellation=False

comments.author: Raghav Kancherla
comments.body:
Testing steps:

- Create a new customer and subscribe to any product under productTypeId 9 using a New york address and zip code.

- Update the product pricing table RenewalProductPrice for the productPriceId selected in step 1 to any productPriceId under ProductId 71, if that's not available, use any other ProductPriceId under a different ProductId

- Insert this record only if productId 71 is not avialble:

INSERT INTO [Product].[CancellationEligibilityConfig]
(ProductID, ProductTypeId, StateProvinceCode, CountryCode, DayLimit, IsActive)
VALUES
(<ProductId used in step 2>, 9, 'NY', 'US', 15, 1)

- Back date the subscription in step 1 and run the product renewal app, this should renew the product tot he ProductPriceId selected in step 2

- Run the {{api21_url}}/GolfNowAPI.svc/rest/channel/331/customers/{{apiEmailaddyoc}}/subscriptions end point and you will see 2 subscriptions with only the 2ns subscription having  "IsEligibleForCancellation": true
comments.bodyHtml:
Testing steps:

  1. Create a new customer and subscribe to any product under productTypeId 9 using a New york address and zip code.

  2. Update the product pricing table RenewalProductPrice for the productPriceId selected in step 1 to any productPriceId under ProductId 71, if that's not available, use any other ProductPriceId under a different ProductId

  3. Insert this record only if productId 71 is not avialble:

INSERT INTO [Product].[CancellationEligibilityConfig]
(ProductID, ProductTypeId, StateProvinceCode, CountryCode, DayLimit, IsActive)
VALUES
(<ProductId used in step 2>, 9, 'NY', 'US', 15, 1)

  1. Back date the subscription in step 1 and run the product renewal app, this should renew the product tot he ProductPriceId selected in step 2

  2. Run the {{api21_url}}/GolfNowAPI.svc/rest/channel/331/customers/{{apiEmailaddyoc}}/subscriptions end point and you will see 2 subscriptions with only the 2ns subscription having "IsEligibleForCancellation": true

issues.summary: Updates to Reservation Confirmation Template
issues.description:
On the Tee Time Reservation email sent to golfers, there are two different bodies that reference benefits of GolfPass. One as a member and one as a non-member. We need to update the text in these versions. See attached for the two versions.

GolfPass Member - Anyone with ProductID [36, 37, 42, 62, 69, 71] - Attached image Screenshot 2026-03-02 at 3.23.43 PM.png

- Can we confirm what validation we are using to determine what member they are? We got a report that some members are receiving the non member version. Confirm with Brian when you find the logic - Luis Confirmed the logic is based on ProductId

- Change “Extra GolfPass Rewards Points” to: Earn or redeem GolfPass Points on tee time bookings

Non-Member OR anyone with a ProductId that does not match the above list- Attached image image (24).png

- Change “Earn and redeem GolfPass Points…” to: Earn GolfPass Points on tee time bookings and redeem for a discount on GolfPass+ membership

- Remove $40 Taylormade credit

- Change the Peacock benefit to: Complimentary access to Peacock Premium (up to 12 months)

issues.summary: EZTP Tee sheet Pricing for $0 TT's Doesn't Load - Round 2
issues.description:
This ticket was originally worked on in https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-13275  but it does not look to be working in prod. We need to revisit this and confirm we have this working. Below is the cloned details from the original ticket.

---
We have a request from the business to allow EZTP to create RackTimes at $0 for member rates. In this case, tee times should be blocked based on the player rule, not the price. Additionally, Tee Sheet Pricing should not affect the expected behavior.

Example 1:

- Tee Sheet Pricing is enabled

- RackTime is sent to us with a price = $0 and at least one player availability

- RackTime should be left unblocked

- RackTime is sent to us with a price = $0 and there is zero player availability

- RackTime should be blocked

- RackTime is sent to us with a price > $0 and at least one player availability

- RackTime should be left unblocked

- RackTime is sent to us with a price > $0 and there is zero player availability

- RackTime should be blocked

Example 2:

- Tee Sheet Pricing is disabled

- RackTime is sent to us with a price = $0 and at least one player availability

- RackTime should be left unblocked

- RackTime is sent to us with a price = $0 and there is zero player availability

- RackTime should be blocked

- RackTime is sent to us with a price > $0 and at least one player availability

- RackTime should be left unblocked

- RackTime is sent to us with a price > $0 and there is zero player availability

- RackTime should be blocked

QA:

- Please coordinate with EZL team and ask them to send in a $0 member rate with at least one available player, as well as a $0 member rate with zero player availability

- Please test both Tee Sheet Pricing scenarios (enabled vs disabled)

- Please test rates equal to $0, as well as rates greater than $0

- As with all EZL tickets, full regressions ezl bookings
issues.descriptionHtml:
This ticket was originally worked on in https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-13275 but it does not look to be working in prod. We need to revisit this and confirm we have this working. Below is the cloned details from the original ticket.

We have a request from the business to allow EZTP to create RackTimes at $0 for member rates. In this case, tee times should be blocked based on the player rule, not the price. Additionally, Tee Sheet Pricing should not affect the expected behavior.

Example 1:

  • Tee Sheet Pricing is enabled

    • RackTime is sent to us with a price = $0 and at least one player availability

      • RackTime should be left unblocked

    • RackTime is sent to us with a price = $0 and there is zero player availability

      • RackTime should be blocked

    • RackTime is sent to us with a price > $0 and at least one player availability

      • RackTime should be left unblocked

    • RackTime is sent to us with a price > $0 and there is zero player availability

      • RackTime should be blocked

Example 2:

  • Tee Sheet Pricing is disabled

    • RackTime is sent to us with a price = $0 and at least one player availability

      • RackTime should be left unblocked

    • RackTime is sent to us with a price = $0 and there is zero player availability

      • RackTime should be blocked

    • RackTime is sent to us with a price > $0 and at least one player availability

      • RackTime should be left unblocked

    • RackTime is sent to us with a price > $0 and there is zero player availability

      • RackTime should be blocked

QA:

  • Please coordinate with EZL team and ask them to send in a $0 member rate with at least one available player, as well as a $0 member rate with zero player availability

  • Please test both Tee Sheet Pricing scenarios (enabled vs disabled)

  • Please test rates equal to $0, as well as rates greater than $0

  • As with all EZL tickets, full regressions ezl bookings

issues.summary: [Compete] User removed from outing hits an error when Front-end tries to move them into a solo round
issues.description:
When a player is in an Outing round, and they are removed from that round by another player, until recently, they were moved into their own solo outing round and whatever scores they had made were maintained and moved over.

We are now seeing when this process happen, the user hits a graph/scorecard error and they are not put into their own round and just removed from the first round entirely.

We need to see what FK issue is being generated and resolve the error on Scorecard and potentially Graph as well

issues.summary: Trade Price Matrix UI Update
issues.description:
The revenue team has requested that we make udpates to the “New” Trade Price Matrix screen to match the changes that the database structure has received. We will be keeping the original trade price matrix screen as is but only updating the V2 page: https://golfnowcentral.com/Inventory/TradePriceMatrixV2

Requirements:

- Add back the Evaluation Range as it exists and functions now on the original page. this should just be a copy of what currently is on the old page.

- Update the Pricing Matrix and replace the Time of day with Days Out

- The Days out broken into 11 sections.

- Days 0 - 8 Should each have their own Sections (9 sections in total)

- Days 9-30 Should all be ONLY ONE Section

- Days 31 - 180 Should all be ONLY one Section

- Under each Days Out Section we need another layer of granularity called “Players Remaining”

- Each Days Out Section should have a row for Players remaining 4 - 1

- This should be 4 different rows for these players remaining

See Screenshot below of a mockup of this idea:

Acceptance criteria:

- Ensure that the “Create Matrix Using Default” button still works for newly created trade rate sets

- Changes apply only to TradePriceMatrixV2 Which is the page “Trade Price Matrix (NEW)”. The original page remains unchanged.

- The Evaluation Range section is restored and functions exactly as it does on the original page.

- The Time of Day segmentation is removed and replaced with Days Out.

- Days Out structure includes:

- Individual sections for Days 0–8 (9 total sections).

- One grouped section for Days 9–30.

- One grouped section for Days 31–180.

- Each Days Out section contains 4 rows for Players Remaining (4, 3, 2, 1).

- All sections load correctly, display properly, and persist/save data as expected.

NOTE:
LINK DS ticket for Database structure of UI

issues.summary: Merge the LIT Hold Period and Autoload UI
issues.description:
Hold Period management is currently split between the Hold Period UI and the Autoload UI, creating duplicate configuration management for Locked In Trade (LIT). When a rate set is enabled for LIT, hold periods should be visible and managed from both areas.

Proposed Change

- Merge the Hold Period UI with the Autoload UI for LIT-enabled rate sets

- When a rate set has LIT enabled:

- Hold periods must display on the Autoload page

- Hold periods must continue to display on the existing Hold Period page

- Ensure both views reflect the same underlying hold data (no duplication or drift)

- Any changes made on one page will subsequently update on the other UI as well

Acceptance Criteria

- LIT-enabled rate sets automatically surface hold periods in Autoload UI

- Hold periods shown in Autoload and Hold Period pages remain in sync

- No additional manual configuration required to manage LIT hold periods

- Existing non-LIT behavior remains unchanged
issues.descriptionHtml:
Hold Period management is currently split between the Hold Period UI and the Autoload UI, creating duplicate configuration management for Locked In Trade (LIT). When a rate set is enabled for LIT, hold periods should be visible and managed from both areas.

Proposed Change

  • Merge the Hold Period UI with the Autoload UI for LIT-enabled rate sets

  • When a rate set has LIT enabled:

    • Hold periods must display on the Autoload page

    • Hold periods must continue to display on the existing Hold Period page

  • Ensure both views reflect the same underlying hold data (no duplication or drift)

  • Any changes made on one page will subsequently update on the other UI as well

Acceptance Criteria

  • LIT-enabled rate sets automatically surface hold periods in Autoload UI

  • Hold periods shown in Autoload and Hold Period pages remain in sync

  • No additional manual configuration required to manage LIT hold periods

  • Existing non-LIT behavior remains unchanged

comments.author: Raghav Kancherla

issues.summary: API Errors with Overlapping Facility Closures causes Duplicate Tee Time Rate Details
issues.description:
Summary
A customer using the GolfNow mobile app sees a raw WCF/SOAP fault popup when opening golf course pages. The visible screen is the course tee-time screen, but the failing backend request is the authenticated customer reservation-history call:

/rest/customers/edomin2@gmail.com/reservations?channelid=5151&expand=Facilities&take=17&skip=0&sort-direction=asc

The API throws:

System.ArgumentException: An item with the same key has already been added.
at System.Linq.Enumerable.ToDictionary(...)
at GolfNow.API.Business.Reservation.ReservationProvider.SearchReservations(...)

Impact
This appears isolated to golfer edomin2@gmail.com / CustomerNumber = 23667034, but it can occur for any customer whose reservation history includes a tee time duplicated by overlapping facility closure map rows. Because the failing request is customer-level and runs across course pages, the same customer can see the error when opening any facility.

Root Cause
Customer reservation 418289152 references TeeTimeID = 2026769635. That tee time overlaps two active FacilityClosurePlayFacilityMap rows for play facility 7002:

ID 123099 | 2026-01-02 to 2026-03-20 | IsActive = 1 | IsWalkingOnly = 1
ID 124941 | 2026-02-09 to 2026-02-12 | IsActive = 1 | DisableInventory = 1

Inventory.CRMTeeTimeSelect joins to FacilityClosurePlayFacilityMap on active rows where TTDateTime BETWEEN StartDate AND EndDate. Since both rows match, the proc returns two rows for the same TeeTimeID.

GolfNowAPI then converts rate details into a dictionary keyed by BigTeeTimeRateId:

var rateDetails = teeTimeInventoryService.GetTeeTimeRateDetails(rateIds)
.ToDictionary(rd => rd.BigTeeTimeRateId);

Because duplicate rows are returned for BigTeeTimeRateId = 2026769635, ToDictionary throws and the reservation-history API returns a server fault.

Evidence
ErrorLogID:

26922733

Failing request:

customers/edomin2@gmail.com/reservations?channelid=5151&expand=Facilities&take=17&skip=0&sort-direction=asc

Customer:

CustomerNumber: 23667034
CustomerID: 5AF183FC-A024-4831-87FD-826BFA1CB293
Email: edomin2@gmail.com

Bad reservation / tee time:

ReservationID: 418289152
TeeTimeID: 2026769635
OwnerID: 5151
PlayDate: 2026-02-10 09:40

Duplicate source:

FacilityClosurePlayFacilityMap rows matching TeeTimeID 2026769635: 2

Code references:

- ReservationProvider.cs

- [InventorySearchDao.cs](/Users/206416620@bwt3.com/Desktop/gn-main/Internal Services/GolfNow.Services.Inventory/GolfNow.Services.Inventory.DAL/DAO/InventorySearchDao.cs:442)

- Inventory.CRMTeeTimeSelect

Expected Result
Customer reservation-history API should return successfully even if a historical tee time overlaps multiple facility closure map rows.

Actual Result
API throws duplicate dictionary key exception and returns a raw server fault, which mobile displays to the customer.

Recommended Fix
Primary backend fix: update Inventory.CRMTeeTimeSelect so FacilityClosurePlayFacilityMap cannot multiply tee-time rows. Use OUTER APPLY TOP (1) or otherwise collapse applicable closure rows to one row per tee time.

Defensive API fix: make ReservationProvider.SearchReservations resilient to duplicate rate detail rows:

var rateDetails = teeTimeInventoryService.GetTeeTimeRateDetails(rateIds)
.GroupBy(rd => rd.BigTeeTimeRateId)
.ToDictionary(g => g.Key, g => g.First());

Data cleanup: review and resolve overlapping active FacilityClosurePlayFacilityMap records for play facility 7002, especially IDs 123099 and 124941.

Secondary mobile fix: do not display raw WCF/SOAP fault XML to users; show a friendly generic error or suppress this non-critical companion failure.

issues.summary: Create configuration to enable use of new EZL TT Publisher
issues.description:
EZL team is building a new XTREAM publisher that uses a different queue than XCS. We need a way to toggle a facility to use the new queue vs XCS.

- Configuration will be at the Rate Set level, exposed on Interface Config in GNC

- Toggle Name: Use Legacy Inventory Source

- Enabled = using XCS

- Disabled = using the new publisher

- Default state upon new rate set creation = Enabled using XCS

- Add Change Tracking for the toggle

- Similar to previous toggles like https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-13712

- Hide this from Master Facility Rate Set Interface Config

- We do not want GNC exposure for Master Facilities in GNC. We will do DB update if we need to make changes
issues.descriptionHtml:
EZL team is building a new XTREAM publisher that uses a different queue than XCS. We need a way to toggle a facility to use the new queue vs XCS.

  • Configuration will be at the Rate Set level, exposed on Interface Config in GNC

    • Toggle Name: Use Legacy Inventory Source

  • Enabled = using XCS

  • Disabled = using the new publisher

  • Default state upon new rate set creation = Enabled using XCS

  • Add Change Tracking for the toggle

    • Similar to previous toggles like https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-13712

  • Hide this from Master Facility Rate Set Interface Config

    • We do not want GNC exposure for Master Facilities in GNC. We will do DB update if we need to make changes

issues.summary: Add BaseSpecialRate (Per Player Rate) to Reservation Search Page
issues.description:
Sales has requested that we add a new column to the Reservations search page to show the Per player price that the reservation was sold as. This will help them to easily look up discrepancies in prices reported by the course as well as add instant visibility to rates on a facility without needing to drill into the reservation.

GNC > Customer > Reservations

Requirements:

- Add a new column into this page called “Rate”

- Pull TTRBaseSpecialRate from DB for each reservationID

GNC > Customer > Reservations

issues.summary: GN AU Password Reset Email Showing "GolfNow for Business" Logo
issues.description:
Summary

The GN AU (GolfNow Australia) consumer password reset email is rendering with the "GolfNow for Business" B2B logo instead of the correct consumer logo.

Impact

Consumer-facing branding issue — AU golfers resetting their http://GolfNow.com.au  password see a business/merchant-facing logo, which is confusing and off-brand.

Root Cause

The asset on Akamai at the URL referenced by the email template is using an outdated image URL.

Template reference: DotNet2/GolfNow.Notification/GolfNow.Notification.Send/Templates/PasswordReset.xslt, line 2461 (Qantas/AU branch)

Current Image URL: https://vs-i8p7v6z9.akamaized.net/content/email/images/NBC_Sports_Next_STACKED.png

Solution

- Replace the image URL with: https://vs-i8p7v6z9.akamaized.net/content/email/images/GN_Euro_Logo.png

Verification

After remediation, trigger a password reset on a http://GolfNow.com.au  account and verify the header image renders as the standard GolfNow logo

Summary

The GN AU (GolfNow Australia) consumer password reset email is rendering with the "GolfNow for Business" B2B logo instead of the correct consumer logo.

Current Image URL: https://vs-i8p7v6z9.akamaized.net/content/email/images/NBC_Sports_Next_STACKED.png

Verification

After remediation, trigger a password reset on a http://GolfNow.com.au account and verify the header image renders as the standard GolfNow logo

issues.summary: Update GNC-Product Search Page to support T&Cs Templates
issues.description:
- Design: Attached

- Sample Pages Affected:

- Edit PPID: https://rc2-www.golfnowcentral.com/Administration/ProductPricingEdit?productId=42&productPricingId=685

- Create PPID: https://rc2-www.golfnowcentral.com/Administration/ProductPricingEdit?productId=47&productPricingId=-1

- Add a new Section Header below Tags and above Owner Settings

- Section Header: Terms & Conditions

- New Field Label: Applicable Owner Group

- This will be an owner selection box similar to lower on the page

- For new PPID creation, one box should pre-populate as empty on the page

- Add a button below the box that says “Add Applicable Owner Group”

- Clicking the button should open a new empty Applicable Owner Group box

- There can be multiple Applicable Owner Group boxes because one PPID can be configured with multiple T&Cs texts depending on the channel

- Drop down Box Label: T&Cs Template

- This should be to the right of the Applicable Owner Group box

- There should only be one of these per Applicable Owner Group

- One Applicable Owner Group can only have one T&Cs template

- You can use the same Template for multiple Applicable Owner Groups

- Drop down box list will be the Template Names created in https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-13302

- Selecting a template will display the Template Body from https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-13302 below the drop down box

- This should default to a NONE selection for new creations

- DoubleOptInContext

- This should be a checkbox to identify how the copy should be exposed in the API https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-13299

- It should be Read Only unless you have edit permissions

- Requirements:

- For ProductTypeId=9, this will be a required field when creating a new product or editing an existing

- For all other ProductTypes, this is not required

- Display an error message like “Please select a Terms & Conditions template for this Product Price Id” if you attempt to save/create without updating

- Add a delete button to the left of Applicable Owner Group

- There should only be a delete button on 2nd, 3rd, 4th etc rows because the first row is always required

- This delete action should delete the entire row

- When creating a new product, the page should load with one Applicable Owner Group box and T&Cs Template dropdown  visible by default

- When Copying an existing PPID, this should copy the previously selected configurations as well

- An OwnerId cannot exist in two separate Applicable Owner Groups within one PPID

- Saving/Creating the PPID on this page should submit the TemplateId to the PPID Relational table created in https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-13302
issues.descriptionHtml:
1. Design: Attached

  2. Sample Pages Affected:

    1. Edit PPID: https://rc2-www.golfnowcentral.com/Administration/ProductPricingEdit?productId=42&productPricingId=685

    2. Create PPID: https://rc2-www.golfnowcentral.com/Administration/ProductPricingEdit?productId=47&productPricingId=-1

  3. Add a new Section Header below Tags and above Owner Settings

    1. Section Header: Terms & Conditions

    2. New Field Label: Applicable Owner Group

      1. This will be an owner selection box similar to lower on the page

        1. For new PPID creation, one box should pre-populate as empty on the page

      2. Add a button below the box that says “Add Applicable Owner Group”

        1. Clicking the button should open a new empty Applicable Owner Group box

      3. There can be multiple Applicable Owner Group boxes because one PPID can be configured with multiple T&Cs texts depending on the channel

    3. Drop down Box Label: T&Cs Template

      1. This should be to the right of the Applicable Owner Group box

      2. There should only be one of these per Applicable Owner Group

      3. One Applicable Owner Group can only have one T&Cs template

      4. You can use the same Template for multiple Applicable Owner Groups

      5. Drop down box list will be the Template Names created in https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-13302

      6. Selecting a template will display the Template Body from https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-13302 below the drop down box

      7. This should default to a NONE selection for new creations

  4. DoubleOptInContext

    1. This should be a checkbox to identify how the copy should be exposed in the API https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-13299

    2. It should be Read Only unless you have edit permissions

  5. Requirements:

    1. For ProductTypeId=9, this will be a required field when creating a new product or editing an existing

      1. For all other ProductTypes, this is not required

      2. Display an error message like “Please select a Terms & Conditions template for this Product Price Id” if you attempt to save/create without updating

    2. Add a delete button to the left of Applicable Owner Group

      1. There should only be a delete button on 2nd, 3rd, 4th etc rows because the first row is always required

      2. This delete action should delete the entire row

    3. When creating a new product, the page should load with one Applicable Owner Group box and T&Cs Template dropdown visible by default

    4. When Copying an existing PPID, this should copy the previously selected configurations as well

    5. An OwnerId cannot exist in two separate Applicable Owner Groups within one PPID

    6. Saving/Creating the PPID on this page should submit the TemplateId to the PPID Relational table created in https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-13302

issues.summary: Submit T&Cs to CustomerSubscription record upon purchase
issues.description:
We will be legally required to store the T&Cs at time of product purchase so we can prove what the user agreed to at a specific time.

- OrderCheckout - https://rc2-api.gnsvc.com/GolfNowAPI.svc/rest/channel/5151/customers/{{EmailAddress}}/orders

- Upon successful purchase, log the terms text of the purchased product to Product.Purchase table

- Also log the VersionNumber of the template for the applicable terms text

- The T&Cs will likely have date and price variables in them, the text stored needs to include the variables at time of purchase, not at time of calling for the data post transaction

- The response fields would be ProductTerms

- Logic for Checkout:

- Validate the PPID in the request of the checkout

- Validate the ChannelID that matches the request

- Submit the terms text and version number that matches the above two validations
issues.descriptionHtml:
We will be legally required to store the T&Cs at time of product purchase so we can prove what the user agreed to at a specific time.

  1. OrderCheckout - https://rc2-api.gnsvc.com/GolfNowAPI.svc/rest/channel/5151/customers/{{EmailAddress}}/orders

    1. Upon successful purchase, log the terms text of the purchased product to Product.Purchase table

      1. Also log the VersionNumber of the template for the applicable terms text

    2. The T&Cs will likely have date and price variables in them, the text stored needs to include the variables at time of purchase, not at time of calling for the data post transaction

    3. The response fields would be ProductTerms

  2. Logic for Checkout:

    1. Validate the PPID in the request of the checkout

    2. Validate the ChannelID that matches the request

    3. Submit the terms text and version number that matches the above two validations

issues.summary: Capture Pre-Block TTDateTime in Inventory Automated Refresh Process
issues.description:
Summary

When Inventory Automated Refresh (IAR) blocks a trade time and rolls it to a new time slot, the parent tee time's TTDateTime is overwritten to 23:00 (11pm), erasing the original play time. Revenue analytics need to track where trade times moved FROM (the pre-block time) to analyze inventory movement patterns and revenue impact.

Request: Capture and log the parent tee time's TTDateTime value BEFORE overwriting it to 23:00, so revenue analytics can build a complete paper trail of all tee time movements.

---
Business Context

Requesting Team

Data Science - Revenue Analytics (Dalton, Allegretti)

Use Case

DS-21792: Track tee time movements caused by inventory refresh to understand:

- How often trade times move from morning slots to afternoon slots

- Revenue impact when times are pushed to less desirable slots

- Effectiveness of Inventory Automated Refresh in returning times to original load times

- Pattern analysis for capacity planning

Current Limitation

Revenue team has a fact table that captures price changes from TTHistory, but cannot track time movements because:

- TTHistory logs: "Tee time inserted by InventoryAutomatedRefresh based on TeeTimeIDs: 123456"

- Parent TeeTimeID can be identified (123456)

- But: Parent's TTDateTime is already 23:00 by query time (overwritten during block)

- Result: No way to determine what time slot the tee time moved FROM

---
Expected Behavior (After Enhancement)

When IAR blocks a trade time and rolls it to a new slot:

- Before overwriting parent TTDateTime to 23:00:

- Read current TTDateTime value

- Include it in the TTHistory entry for the NEW tee time

- Updated TTHistory format:

Tee time inserted by InventoryAutomatedRefresh based on TeeTimeIDs: 123456 (PriorTime: 2026-04-15 14:30:00)

- Alternative format (if clearer):

Tee time inserted by InventoryAutomatedRefresh from 2:30 PM based on TeeTimeIDs: 123456

Parsing Example

Revenue analytics can parse TTHistory to reconstruct movement chain:

TeeTimeID  Created              TTHistory
---------- -------------------- ----------------------------------------------------------
2093781359 2026-03-20 10:00:00  "TeeTimeLoadID: 49670142"                    → 10:00 AM
2096365430 2026-03-28 17:13:03  "...based on TeeTimeIDs: 2093781359 (PriorTime: 10:00)"  → 2:00 PM
2109595319 2026-03-31 09:45:12  "...based on TeeTimeIDs: 2096365430 (PriorTime: 14:00)"  → 11:00 AM

Movement chain: 10am → 2pm → 11am

---
Current Behavior

TTHistory captures:

- Source TeeTimeID(s) that were consolidated: "based on TeeTimeIDs: 123456, 789012"

- Event type: "Tee time inserted by InventoryAutomatedRefresh"

- Timestamp of new tee time creation

TTHistory does NOT capture:

- The TTDateTime value of source tee time(s) before block

- Reason: Parent tee time's TTDateTime is overwritten to 23:00 during block process

- By the time revenue analytics queries, original time slot information is lost

---
Proposed Solution

Implementation Approach

Location: Cache Manager/3.0/GolfNow.Inventory.CacheAndSync.Domain/Sync/TradeTimes.cs (Inventory Automated Refresh logic)

Change: Capture pre-block TTDateTime and append to history string

Current code (approximate location line ~1047):

var log = String.Format("Tee time inserted by InventoryAutomatedRefresh based on TeeTimeIDs: {0}",
string.Join(" , ", originalTeeTimes.Value.Select(r => r.Item2.ID.ToString()).ToList()));

Enhanced code:

// Capture pre-block TTDateTime values
var teeTimeDetails = originalTeeTimes.Value
.Select(r => $"{r.Item2.ID} (PriorTime: {r.Item2.DateTime:yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss})")
.ToList();

var log = String.Format("Tee time inserted by InventoryAutomatedRefresh based on TeeTimeIDs: {0}",
string.Join(" , ", teeTimeDetails));

// Result: "Tee time inserted by InventoryAutomatedRefresh based on TeeTimeIDs: 123456 (PriorTime: 2026-04-15 14:30:00), 789012 (PriorTime: 2026-04-15 14:30:00)"

---
Implementation Details

Files to Modify

Primary Change:

- Cache Manager/3.0/GolfNow.Inventory.CacheAndSync.Domain/Sync/TradeTimes.cs

- Line ~1047: Enhance log string to include PriorTime

Data Model:

- CRMTeeTime.TTHistory field (varchar) - existing field, no schema change

- Format: Append (PriorTime: yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss) to existing log string

---
Testing & Verification

Test Scenario 1: Single Source Tee Time

- AutoLoad creates trade time at 8:00 AM (TeeTimeID: 1000)

- IAR blocks and rolls to 10:00 AM (creates TeeTimeID: 2000)

- Verify: TeeTimeID 2000 TTHistory contains: "...based on TeeTimeIDs: 1000 (PriorTime: 2026-05-20 08:00:00)"

- Verify: TeeTimeID 1000 TTDateTime is now 23:00

Test Scenario 2: Multiple Source Tee Times (Consolidation)

- IAR consolidates TeeTimeID 1000 (2:00 PM) and 1001 (2:00 PM) → creates 2000 (2:00 PM)

- Verify: TeeTimeID 2000 TTHistory contains both source times with PriorTime values

- Verify: Both 1000 and 1001 have TTDateTime = 23:00

Test Scenario 3: Multi-Step Roll Chain

- TeeTime 1000 at 8:00 AM → rolled to TeeTime 2000 at 2:00 PM

- TeeTime 2000 at 2:00 PM → rolled to TeeTime 3000 at 11:00 AM

- Verify: Can reconstruct chain: 8am → 2pm → 11am

Validation Query

-- Parse TTHistory to extract PriorTime
SELECT
tt.TTTeeTimeID,
tt.TTDateTime AS CurrentTime,
h.History,
-- Extract PriorTime using string parsing
CASE
WHEN h.History LIKE '%PriorTime:%'
THEN SUBSTRING(h.History,
CHARINDEX('PriorTime: ', h.History) + 11,
19)  -- 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss' = 19 chars
ELSE NULL
END AS PriorTime
FROM CRMTeeTime tt
JOIN CRMTeeTimeHistoryDetail h ON h.TeeTimeID = tt.TTTeeTimeID
WHERE h.History LIKE '%InventoryAutomatedRefresh%'
ORDER BY h.Timestamp;

---
Acceptance Criteria

- History Capture

- [ ] When IAR blocks/rolls a trade time, new tee time's TTHistory includes PriorTime for source tee time(s)

- [ ] Format: "Tee time inserted by InventoryAutomatedRefresh based on TeeTimeIDs: {ID} (PriorTime: {TTDateTime})"

- [ ] Multiple source tee times: All listed with respective PriorTime values

- Data Accuracy

- [ ] PriorTime value matches source tee time's TTDateTime BEFORE block operation

- [ ] Timestamp format: yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss (SQL-friendly, unambiguous)

- Edge Case Handling

- [ ] Source tee time with TTDateTime = 23:00 → logs as PriorTime: 23:00 (not skipped)

- [ ] Multiple source tee times → all captured in single history string

- [ ] TTHistory varchar length validated (no truncation)

- Parsing Validation

- [ ] Revenue analytics can extract PriorTime using SQL string functions

- [ ] Sample query provided for analytics team

- [ ] Test data demonstrates multi-step roll chain reconstruction

- Regression Testing

- [ ] IAR continues to function normally (no functional changes)

- [ ] Block/roll process still completes successfully

- [ ] New tee times created with correct attributes (rate, rule, etc.)
issues.descriptionHtml:
Summary

When Inventory Automated Refresh (IAR) blocks a trade time and rolls it to a new time slot, the parent tee time's TTDateTime is overwritten to 23:00 (11pm), erasing the original play time. Revenue analytics need to track where trade times moved FROM (the pre-block time) to analyze inventory movement patterns and revenue impact.

Request: Capture and log the parent tee time's TTDateTime value BEFORE overwriting it to 23:00, so revenue analytics can build a complete paper trail of all tee time movements.

Business Context

Requesting Team

Data Science - Revenue Analytics (Dalton, Allegretti)

Use Case

DS-21792: Track tee time movements caused by inventory refresh to understand:

  • How often trade times move from morning slots to afternoon slots

  • Revenue impact when times are pushed to less desirable slots

  • Effectiveness of Inventory Automated Refresh in returning times to original load times

  • Pattern analysis for capacity planning

Current Limitation

Revenue team has a fact table that captures price changes from TTHistory, but cannot track time movements because:

  1. TTHistory logs: "Tee time inserted by InventoryAutomatedRefresh based on TeeTimeIDs: 123456"

  2. Parent TeeTimeID can be identified (123456)

  3. But: Parent's TTDateTime is already 23:00 by query time (overwritten during block)

  4. Result: No way to determine what time slot the tee time moved FROM

Expected Behavior (After Enhancement)

When IAR blocks a trade time and rolls it to a new slot:

  1. Before overwriting parent TTDateTime to 23:00:

    • Read current TTDateTime value

    • Include it in the TTHistory entry for the NEW tee time

  2. Updated TTHistory format:

    Tee time inserted by InventoryAutomatedRefresh based on TeeTimeIDs: 123456 (PriorTime: 2026-04-15 14:30:00)

  3. Alternative format (if clearer):

    Tee time inserted by InventoryAutomatedRefresh from 2:30 PM based on TeeTimeIDs: 123456

Parsing Example

Revenue analytics can parse TTHistory to reconstruct movement chain:

TeeTimeID  Created              TTHistory
---------- -------------------- ----------------------------------------------------------
2093781359 2026-03-20 10:00:00  "TeeTimeLoadID: 49670142"                    → 10:00 AM
2096365430 2026-03-28 17:13:03  "...based on TeeTimeIDs: 2093781359 (PriorTime: 10:00)"  → 2:00 PM
2109595319 2026-03-31 09:45:12  "...based on TeeTimeIDs: 2096365430 (PriorTime: 14:00)"  → 11:00 AM

Movement chain: 10am → 2pm → 11am

Current Behavior

TTHistory captures:

  • Source TeeTimeID(s) that were consolidated: "based on TeeTimeIDs: 123456, 789012"

  • Event type: "Tee time inserted by InventoryAutomatedRefresh"

  • Timestamp of new tee time creation

TTHistory does NOT capture:

  • The TTDateTime value of source tee time(s) before block

  • Reason: Parent tee time's TTDateTime is overwritten to 23:00 during block process

  • By the time revenue analytics queries, original time slot information is lost

Proposed Solution

Implementation Approach

Location: Cache Manager/3.0/GolfNow.Inventory.CacheAndSync.Domain/Sync/TradeTimes.cs (Inventory Automated Refresh logic)

Change: Capture pre-block TTDateTime and append to history string

Current code (approximate location line ~1047):

var log = String.Format("Tee time inserted by InventoryAutomatedRefresh based on TeeTimeIDs: {0}", 
    string.Join(" , ", originalTeeTimes.Value.Select(r => r.Item2.ID.ToString()).ToList()));

Enhanced code:

// Capture pre-block TTDateTime values
var teeTimeDetails = originalTeeTimes.Value
    .Select(r => $"{r.Item2.ID} (PriorTime: {r.Item2.DateTime:yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss})")
    .ToList();

var log = String.Format("Tee time inserted by InventoryAutomatedRefresh based on TeeTimeIDs: {0}", 
    string.Join(" , ", teeTimeDetails));

// Result: "Tee time inserted by InventoryAutomatedRefresh based on TeeTimeIDs: 123456 (PriorTime: 2026-04-15 14:30:00), 789012 (PriorTime: 2026-04-15 14:30:00)"

Implementation Details

Files to Modify

Primary Change:

  • Cache Manager/3.0/GolfNow.Inventory.CacheAndSync.Domain/Sync/TradeTimes.cs

    • Line ~1047: Enhance log string to include PriorTime

Data Model:

  • CRMTeeTime.TTHistory field (varchar) - existing field, no schema change

  • Format: Append (PriorTime: yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss) to existing log string

Testing & Verification

Test Scenario 1: Single Source Tee Time

  1. AutoLoad creates trade time at 8:00 AM (TeeTimeID: 1000)

  2. IAR blocks and rolls to 10:00 AM (creates TeeTimeID: 2000)

  3. Verify: TeeTimeID 2000 TTHistory contains: "...based on TeeTimeIDs: 1000 (PriorTime: 2026-05-20 08:00:00)"

  4. Verify: TeeTimeID 1000 TTDateTime is now 23:00

Test Scenario 2: Multiple Source Tee Times (Consolidation)

  1. IAR consolidates TeeTimeID 1000 (2:00 PM) and 1001 (2:00 PM) → creates 2000 (2:00 PM)

  2. Verify: TeeTimeID 2000 TTHistory contains both source times with PriorTime values

  3. Verify: Both 1000 and 1001 have TTDateTime = 23:00

Test Scenario 3: Multi-Step Roll Chain

  1. TeeTime 1000 at 8:00 AM → rolled to TeeTime 2000 at 2:00 PM

  2. TeeTime 2000 at 2:00 PM → rolled to TeeTime 3000 at 11:00 AM

  3. Verify: Can reconstruct chain: 8am → 2pm → 11am

Validation Query

-- Parse TTHistory to extract PriorTime
SELECT 
    tt.TTTeeTimeID,
    tt.TTDateTime AS CurrentTime,
    h.History,
    -- Extract PriorTime using string parsing
    CASE 
        WHEN h.History LIKE '%PriorTime:%'
        THEN SUBSTRING(h.History, 
            CHARINDEX('PriorTime: ', h.History) + 11, 
            19)  -- 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss' = 19 chars
        ELSE NULL
    END AS PriorTime
FROM CRMTeeTime tt
JOIN CRMTeeTimeHistoryDetail h ON h.TeeTimeID = tt.TTTeeTimeID
WHERE h.History LIKE '%InventoryAutomatedRefresh%'
ORDER BY h.Timestamp;

Acceptance Criteria

  1. History Capture

    • [ ] When IAR blocks/rolls a trade time, new tee time's TTHistory includes PriorTime for source tee time(s)

    • [ ] Format: "Tee time inserted by InventoryAutomatedRefresh based on TeeTimeIDs: {ID} (PriorTime: {TTDateTime})"

    • [ ] Multiple source tee times: All listed with respective PriorTime values

  2. Data Accuracy

    • [ ] PriorTime value matches source tee time's TTDateTime BEFORE block operation

    • [ ] Timestamp format: yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss (SQL-friendly, unambiguous)

  3. Edge Case Handling

    • [ ] Source tee time with TTDateTime = 23:00 → logs as PriorTime: 23:00 (not skipped)

    • [ ] Multiple source tee times → all captured in single history string

    • [ ] TTHistory varchar length validated (no truncation)

  4. Parsing Validation

    • [ ] Revenue analytics can extract PriorTime using SQL string functions

    • [ ] Sample query provided for analytics team

    • [ ] Test data demonstrates multi-step roll chain reconstruction

  5. Regression Testing

    • [ ] IAR continues to function normally (no functional changes)

    • [ ] Block/roll process still completes successfully

    • [ ] New tee times created with correct attributes (rate, rule, etc.)

issues.summary: Add User SystemID and Name to Customer Subscription Control History in GNC
issues.description:
The business has requested agent specific information (SystemUserID and username) to know who purchased or cancelled a customer subscription. We need to add this information to the Subscription Control History in GNC.

Example:

- Corrie Thomas (SUID 35237) purchases a membership for customer Nathan Farris

- Add a line item under the Transactions history that reads: ‘Purchasing Agent: cthomas - 35237’ {TIMESTAMP}

- Brian Pugh then cancels the membership

- An additional line up is added that reads: ‘Cancelling Agent: bpugh1 - 44566 - {TIMESTAMP}

- Do not include these two line items if the data is NULL

comments.author: Kris Warthen
comments.body:
QA Notes from linked https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-12849

- If the CRMGolfFacility.GFTeeTimePolicyOverRide value is populated for Hot Deals, the UI shows the over ride message but the email contains the standard Hot Deal Tee Time Policy. What should the email have?

- For a course time, CRMGolfFacility.GFTeeTimePolicy IS NOT NULL it should be what is in the email (existing functionality)

- Ignore other columns in this case, even is SF table is populated do not include it

- For a course time, if CRMGolfFacility.GFTeeTimePolicy IS NULL and SalesForce.TeeTimePolicy.OtherTeeTimePolicyItems is NOT NULL, the email should contain SalesForce.TeeTimePolicy.OtherTeeTimePolicyItems text (current bug/update needed).

- For a course time, if CRMGolfFacility.GFTeeTimePolicy and SalesForce.TeeTimePolicy.OtherTeeTimePolicyItems are BOTH NULL, there is default template text that should be included

issues.summary: Create GNC Page to Showcase Locked In Trade Holds
issues.description:
As a Revenue manager, I would like the ability to have visibility and editability for all of the locked in trade holds on a given rate set.

currently, there is no way to see what trade holds are present on a rate set without them loading in tee times and hoping that the time that is loaded is on the same slot as the trade hold. Typically, they are having to log into the tee sheet to confirm.

For this ticket, we need to add a new GNC page under the Inventory tab that shows all of the upcoming trade holds for that given rate set.

---
Also, there needs to be a way to edit these trade holds on a case by case basis (one hold at a time). They should be able to manually enter a new tee time on each of them, as well as just cancel them off the tee sheet.

---
Detailed Requirements - Post-meeting with Revenue:

- Change Name of existing Page - “Trade Reservation Hold - Configuration”

- New page would be “Trade Reservation Hold - Update”

- Show Both the Live Trade time and the Hold time and status of each (as indicated by the player icons on the corresponding live time from Update Tee times)

- Allow user to edit by typing over the Hold Time to make a change to the hold time

- this should adopt same text logic as Update Tee times

- This will trigger the API to instantly attempt a Cancel Hold and Create Trade Hold at the new time

- Include Roll Logic to attempt to create a hold at the nearest available tee time if the new hold time is not available

- Show the user the response from these changes in a popup window on the page

- Show the Create Hold API response if the Cancel is successful. Either successful message or the error if present.

- Show the user the error from the cancel Hold endpoint if this fails so that they know why they cannot make the change

- Research the ability to also update the Trade Live time if possible. Potentially at the very least, provide a redirect link to the tee time Details page to make the update there

- Also provide a button to cancel the hold for each individual hold time

- At the bottom of the page, show the Trade Hold Period Rate Fence

- this should always show the current days rate fence

---
sample image of changes:
issues.descriptionHtml:
As a Revenue manager, I would like the ability to have visibility and editability for all of the locked in trade holds on a given rate set.

currently, there is no way to see what trade holds are present on a rate set without them loading in tee times and hoping that the time that is loaded is on the same slot as the trade hold. Typically, they are having to log into the tee sheet to confirm.

For this ticket, we need to add a new GNC page under the Inventory tab that shows all of the upcoming trade holds for that given rate set.

Also, there needs to be a way to edit these trade holds on a case by case basis (one hold at a time). They should be able to manually enter a new tee time on each of them, as well as just cancel them off the tee sheet.

Detailed Requirements - Post-meeting with Revenue:

  1. Change Name of existing Page - “Trade Reservation Hold - Configuration”

    1. New page would be “Trade Reservation Hold - Update”

  2. Show Both the Live Trade time and the Hold time and status of each (as indicated by the player icons on the corresponding live time from Update Tee times)

  3. Allow user to edit by typing over the Hold Time to make a change to the hold time

    1. this should adopt same text logic as Update Tee times

    2. This will trigger the API to instantly attempt a Cancel Hold and Create Trade Hold at the new time

    3. Include Roll Logic to attempt to create a hold at the nearest available tee time if the new hold time is not available

  4. Show the user the response from these changes in a popup window on the page

    1. Show the Create Hold API response if the Cancel is successful. Either successful message or the error if present.

    2. Show the user the error from the cancel Hold endpoint if this fails so that they know why they cannot make the change

  5. Research the ability to also update the Trade Live time if possible. Potentially at the very least, provide a redirect link to the tee time Details page to make the update there

  6. Also provide a button to cancel the hold for each individual hold time

  7. At the bottom of the page, show the Trade Hold Period Rate Fence

    1. this should always show the current days rate fence
