issues.summary: Add NonRefundable Support to Course Reservation Edit Page - GNC
Rate Periods - Disable the Affiliate Source Owner ID for Simulator Rate Sets
issues.description:
Background

When a facility is activated as a Simulator, the system should not default releasing to the Affiliate Source Owner ID. Additionally, Owner-related fields should not be available when the associated Rate Set is configured as a Simulator.

Currently, users are still able to select Affiliate Customer Group ID and Owner fields on the Rate Periods page and they also are enabled by default.

---
Requirements

1. Prevent Default Behavior

- When a facility is activated as a Simulator, the system must not default releasing to Affiliate Source Owner ID.

2. Disable Owner Fields for Simulator Rate Sets

- If a Rate Set Type = Simulator:

- Disable Owner Group for Affiliates

- Prevent selection of Affiliate Customer Group ID on the Rate Periods page

3. UI Behavior

- On the Rate Periods page:

- If the associated Rate Set is marked as Simulator:

- Affiliate Customer Group ID field must be:

- Disabled (read-only), or

- Hidden (preferred approach to be confirmed)

- Owner-related fields must be:

- Disabled or hidden

- Users should not be able to override this behavior

---
Acceptance Criteria

- Given a Rate Set with Type = Simulator
When a user navigates to the Rate Periods page
Then the Affiliate Customer Group ID field is disabled or not selectable.

- Given a Rate Set with Type = Simulator
Then Owner ID and Owner Group fields are disabled or hidden.

- Given a facility activated as a Simulator
Then the system does not default releasing to Affiliate Source Owner ID.

- Given a non-Simulator Rate Set
Then existing Owner and Affiliate selection behavior remains unchanged.

issues.summary: OpenAI Apps - MCP Server
issues.description:
Create new dockerized .NET MCP Server

Official MCP site and documentation https://modelcontextprotocol.io/docs/getting-started/intro

Location Based Search + Tee Times  Tool

ChatGPT recommended to only have one tool to support all similar searches even if they contain different combinations of filters. It also recommended that one tool be used for facility search + tee time search. https://chatgpt.com/share/e/69861bbf-3de0-800f-8efc-0cd8a1759a85 . Based on new design requirements, the initial launch of this will not include sample tee times and rate selections but we should still keep the code present because it will be exposed in subsequent phases.

Use ChannelId 331 for all API calls

Prod API Creds: TBD

DEV API Creds: TBD

- Call this API to get the facility results and info: https://api.gnsvc.com/GolfNowAPI.svc/rest/channel/331/facility-summaries?q=geolocation&skip=0&sort-by=facilities.distance&date-min=2026-02-07&date-max=2026-02-07&proximity=35&expand=RatingSummary&trade-only=false&take=10&latitude=28.4258547&longitude=-81.2408682&isSimulator=false

-

- The above is the base API call that will get unfiltered facilities by the basic requirements to search assuming the user does not specify anything

- Take up to 10 facilities

- Potential Parameters to append based on prompts

- &trade-only=true If the prompt mentions Hot Deals or best deals

- &price-min or &price-max if the prompt mentions price restrictions

- &time-min or &time-max if the prompt mentions time restrictions

- &holes if the prompt mentions they number of holes they are looking for. The default should be 18

- &players if the prompt mentions the number of players they have. The default should be 1

- &isSimulator=trueif the prompt mentions indoor golf, simulators or driving ranges

- &sort-by=facilities.ratingand &sort-direction=desc if the prompt mentions sorting by highest rated or top rated

- &sorty-by=GreenFees and &sort-direction=asc or desc if the prompt mentions sorting by lowest or highest price

- Expose FeeMessagingFacilityDisplayRates object

- UI team will use this object for price ranges and fee message text at the top of the widget

- If NULL, they will not display the message and use the standard API fields for the display

- Expose the IsSimulator field so UI can badge facilities appropriately

- Call this API to get a sample of tee times at each FacilityId from the above: {{prod-api-base-url}}/GolfNowAPI.svc/rest/channel/331/facilities/4356/tee-times?skip=0&take=100&trade-only=false&date-min=2026-02-17&date-max=2026-02-17

- The above is the base API call that will get unfiltered tee times per facility by the basic requirements to search assuming the user does not specify anything

- Display 3 tee time tiles

- Prioritize Hot Deals if no specific filter would block those out

- If there are not 3 Hot Deals, fill the open spots with Course Times

- The tee times should match the prompted filters

- if they prompt for 9 holes, display the 9 hole tee times

- if they prompt for afternoon tee times, display the afternoon tee times

- Potential Parameters to append based on prompts

- &trade-only=true If the prompt mentions Hot Deals or best deals

- &price-min or &price-max if the prompt mentions price restrictions

- &time-min or &time-max if the prompt mentions time restrictions

- &holes if the prompt mentions they number of holes they are looking for

- &players if the prompt mentions the number of players they have

- &sorty-by=GreenFees and &sort-direction=asc or desc if the prompt mentions sorting by lowest or highest price

- Tool Description:

- Returns a list of golf facilities with available tee times for display in search results.
Each result combines facility summary data (name, location, distance, rating) with near-term tee time offers (time, price, holes, player count, deal flags).

This tool supports location-based searches using a city, area, or the user’s current location, and allows filtering and sorting by distance, date, time window, number of holes, players, price, rating, and deal types.

Use this tool whenever a user asks to find, browse, or compare golf courses or tee times in a specific area or near them.

- Associated UI: Picture above

Testing:

We need the ability for a ChatGPT user to:

- Prompt by lat/long, zip code, city, direct course name or geographical area and have facilities that return

- Show me available tee times near my house (assuming they have allowed location services and the browser has the lat/long)

- Show me available tee times in 32832

- Show me available tee times at Dubsdread golf course

- Show me available tee times in central florida

- The word “tee time” could be replaced with “golf course(s)” or “course(s)”

- Prompt by the above but with a date or date range

- Show me tee times for tomorrow

- Show me tee times for the next week

- The results should return Facility Name, Address, Summary info like Earliest/Latest Tee Time, Min/Max Price, IsSimulator

- Results should default to filtering by distance from the location based search term ascending

- If a prompt uses words like “Best” or “Highest Rated”, we should filter using our Rating filter descending

- If the prompt uses words like “By Price” or “Cheapest”, we should filter using our price filter ascending

- If no date is provided in the prompt

- Default to today’s tee times

- If it is after 2pm, display tomorrow’s tee times

- Default radius should be 35 miles

- Default facility results limit to 10

- If a user asks for more results, submit another batch of 10

- Clicking on a button should deeplink the user to the facility page UI

- https://www.golfnow.com/tee-times/facility/19058-brookside-at-the-rose-bowl-eo-nay/search

- append a utm_source=chatgptapp to all links so page views can be tracked with analytics and we can create a cookie

- As a fallback, if a prompt is not recognized, display fallback copy: TBD

Repo: https://github.com/GolfNowEng/golf-web-openai
issues.descriptionHtml:
Create new dockerized .NET MCP Server

Official MCP site and documentation https://modelcontextprotocol.io/docs/getting-started/intro

Location Based Search + Tee Times Tool

ChatGPT recommended to only have one tool to support all similar searches even if they contain different combinations of filters. It also recommended that one tool be used for facility search + tee time search. https://chatgpt.com/share/e/69861bbf-3de0-800f-8efc-0cd8a1759a85 . Based on new design requirements, the initial launch of this will not include sample tee times and rate selections but we should still keep the code present because it will be exposed in subsequent phases.

Use ChannelId 331 for all API calls

Prod API Creds: TBD

DEV API Creds: TBD

  • Call this API to get the facility results and info: https://api.gnsvc.com/GolfNowAPI.svc/rest/channel/331/facility-summaries?q=geolocation&skip=0&sort-by=facilities.distance&date-min=2026-02-07&date-max=2026-02-07&proximity=35&expand=RatingSummary&trade-only=false&take=10&latitude=28.4258547&longitude=-81.2408682&isSimulator=false

    • The above is the base API call that will get unfiltered facilities by the basic requirements to search assuming the user does not specify anything

    • Take up to 10 facilities

    • Potential Parameters to append based on prompts

      • &trade-only=true If the prompt mentions Hot Deals or best deals

      • &price-min or &price-max if the prompt mentions price restrictions

      • &time-min or &time-max if the prompt mentions time restrictions

      • &holes if the prompt mentions they number of holes they are looking for. The default should be 18

      • &players if the prompt mentions the number of players they have. The default should be 1

      • &isSimulator=trueif the prompt mentions indoor golf, simulators or driving ranges

      • &sort-by=facilities.ratingand &sort-direction=desc if the prompt mentions sorting by highest rated or top rated

      • &sorty-by=GreenFees and &sort-direction=asc or desc if the prompt mentions sorting by lowest or highest price

    • Expose FeeMessagingFacilityDisplayRates object

      • UI team will use this object for price ranges and fee message text at the top of the widget

      • If NULL, they will not display the message and use the standard API fields for the display

    • Expose the IsSimulator field so UI can badge facilities appropriately

  • Call this API to get a sample of tee times at each FacilityId from the above: {{prod-api-base-url}}/GolfNowAPI.svc/rest/channel/331/facilities/4356/tee-times?skip=0&take=100&trade-only=false&date-min=2026-02-17&date-max=2026-02-17

    • The above is the base API call that will get unfiltered tee times per facility by the basic requirements to search assuming the user does not specify anything

    • Display 3 tee time tiles

    • Prioritize Hot Deals if no specific filter would block those out

    • If there are not 3 Hot Deals, fill the open spots with Course Times

      • The tee times should match the prompted filters

        • if they prompt for 9 holes, display the 9 hole tee times

        • if they prompt for afternoon tee times, display the afternoon tee times

    • Potential Parameters to append based on prompts

      • &trade-only=true If the prompt mentions Hot Deals or best deals

      • &price-min or &price-max if the prompt mentions price restrictions

      • &time-min or &time-max if the prompt mentions time restrictions

      • &holes if the prompt mentions they number of holes they are looking for

      • &players if the prompt mentions the number of players they have

      • &sorty-by=GreenFees and &sort-direction=asc or desc if the prompt mentions sorting by lowest or highest price

  • Tool Description:

    • Returns a list of golf facilities with available tee times for display in search results.
      Each result combines facility summary data (name, location, distance, rating) with near-term tee time offers (time, price, holes, player count, deal flags).

      This tool supports location-based searches using a city, area, or the user’s current location, and allows filtering and sorting by distance, date, time window, number of holes, players, price, rating, and deal types.

      Use this tool whenever a user asks to find, browse, or compare golf courses or tee times in a specific area or near them.

  • Associated UI: Picture above

Testing:

We need the ability for a ChatGPT user to:

  • Prompt by lat/long, zip code, city, direct course name or geographical area and have facilities that return

    • Show me available tee times near my house (assuming they have allowed location services and the browser has the lat/long)

    • Show me available tee times in 32832

    • Show me available tee times at Dubsdread golf course

    • Show me available tee times in central florida

    • The word “tee time” could be replaced with “golf course(s)” or “course(s)”

  • Prompt by the above but with a date or date range

    • Show me tee times for tomorrow

    • Show me tee times for the next week

  • The results should return Facility Name, Address, Summary info like Earliest/Latest Tee Time, Min/Max Price, IsSimulator

  • Results should default to filtering by distance from the location based search term ascending

    • If a prompt uses words like “Best” or “Highest Rated”, we should filter using our Rating filter descending

    • If the prompt uses words like “By Price” or “Cheapest”, we should filter using our price filter ascending

  • If no date is provided in the prompt

    • Default to today’s tee times

    • If it is after 2pm, display tomorrow’s tee times

  • Default radius should be 35 miles

  • Default facility results limit to 10

    • If a user asks for more results, submit another batch of 10

  • Clicking on a button should deeplink the user to the facility page UI

    • https://www.golfnow.com/tee-times/facility/19058-brookside-at-the-rose-bowl-eo-nay/search

    • append a utm_source=chatgptapp to all links so page views can be tracked with analytics and we can create a cookie

  • As a fallback, if a prompt is not recognized, display fallback copy: TBD

Repo: https://github.com/GolfNowEng/golf-web-openai

issues.summary: Full EZL Cancellation Flow Test - Document Errors and Fails
issues.description:
In an effort to fix the the cancellation issues and failures that we are seeing with the EZL tee sheet, we need to do a full end to end scenario testing session that documents each issue so that we can prioritize work to fix them.

Here is the list of all testing scenarios:

https://golfnow.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/PRO/pages/10271621214/EZL+Cancellation+Flows

Please add to document or create a separate one to keep the results organized.

Work with EZL QA to test this

issues.summary: CLONE - Smartplay/Commerce Prepaid: Refund for split distribution rate set applies logic to refund amount ( BRS and EZRTS)
issues.description:
Steps to reproduce:

- Book prepaid tee time with split revenue allocation between GN and Golf Course (for example, 70/30)

- Tee Time is $100, 70 goes to G1, and 30 goes to Golfnow

- Navigate to G1 and find the order

- Do a  Full Refund on the order $70.00 in G1 UI

Expected result: $70 refund should be applied to G1 (golf course part)

Actual result: $49 is refunded from the G1 side.

- GNC/ResService accurately refunds the $70 refund

- Looks like we are sending 70% of the $70 instead of 70% of the $100 back to G1 Orders
issues.descriptionHtml:
Steps to reproduce:

  1. Book prepaid tee time with split revenue allocation between GN and Golf Course (for example, 70/30)

    1. Tee Time is $100, 70 goes to G1, and 30 goes to Golfnow

  2. Navigate to G1 and find the order

  3. Do a Full Refund on the order $70.00 in G1 UI

Expected result: $70 refund should be applied to G1 (golf course part)

Actual result: $49 is refunded from the G1 side.

  • GNC/ResService accurately refunds the $70 refund

  • Looks like we are sending 70% of the $70 instead of 70% of the $100 back to G1 Orders

issues.summary: Smartplay OrderID's Not Consistently Stored in ReservationMetadata
issues.descriptionHtml:
Whenever Send Order to Interface is enabled on a rate set, for EZRTS (EZL with a BRS facility location) and BRSG, we create a Smartplay OrderID. these OrderID’s are supposed to be saved in the Reservation Metadata

select * from ReservationMetadata AS md (NOLOCK) WHERE md.ReservationMetadataTypeId = 60
select * from ReservationMetadataType where ReservationMetadataTypeId = 60

Steps to reproduce

  1. enable a facility for a BRSG location

  2. Turn on Send Order to Interface

  3. Make a prepaid reservation

  4. Call GetBookingInfo endpoint to see if ID is created

Here is the code base that proves that we are storing the referenceID from vendor service response. 


                if (!response.Success)
                {
                    response = vendorServiceProxy.CreateOrder(vendorId, request);
                }

                if (response.Success)
                {
                    SaveReservationMetadata(response.ProviderReferenceId, reservationId, state);
                }
                else
                {
                    SaveReservationMetadata(RESERVATION_METADATA_ERROR, reservationId, state);
                }

issues.summary: Add Rate Set Level Granularity for Tee Time Notes
issues.description:
GOAL

Introduce a Tee Time Notes field at the Rate Set level that overrides the existing Facility-level Tee Time Notes setting when populated.

This enhancement enables differentiated messaging by rate set while maintaining backward compatibility with existing functionality.

---
Business Problem

Today, Tee Time Notes can be configured at:

- Tee Time Level (most granular – existing functionality)

- Facility Level (existing functionality)

However, there is no Rate Set level option.

For tee sheet pricing facilities, individual tee times cannot be manually updated. This removes the ability to leverage Tee Time level notes (Priority 1), leaving only a single facility-wide message.

As a result:

- All rate sets inherit the same note.

- Courses cannot differentiate messaging by rate type.

- Tee sheet pricing facilities lack flexibility to manage notes at scale.

Adding a  rate set level override resolves this gap while preserving existing logic.

---
Current State (Must Remain Unchanged)

1. Tee Time–Level Notes (Existing – Highest Priority)

- Configured directly on an individual tee time.

- Overrides all other note levels.

- Must continue to function exactly as it does today.

2.  Facility-Level Notes (Existing – Lowest Priority)

- Configured in Tee Time Settings.

- Applies to all rate sets and tee times unless overridden.

- Must continue to function exactly as it does today.

---
New Functionality

Rate Set–Level Tee Time Notes (Middle prio)

Add a new “Tee Time Notes” text field on the Rate Periods page.

The field must support the same formatting and character limits as the Facility-level Tee Time Notes field

---
Logic / Precedence Rules

The system must follow this hierarchy:

- Tee Time–Level Notes (existing – highest priority)

- Rate Period–Level Notes (NEW)

- Facility-Level Notes (existing – fallback)

Logic Behavior

- If Tee Time–level notes exist → use those.

- Else if Rate Period–level notes are populated → use those.

- Else → use Facility-level notes.

- If Rate Period field is blank/null → default to Facility-level notes.

No changes to the behavior of Tee Time–level or Facility-level notes.

---
Acceptance Criteria

- A new Tee Time Notes field is visible on the Rate Periods page.

- Existing Tee Time–level notes continue to override all other note levels.

- When a Rate Period Tee Time Note is entered:

- It overrides the Facility-level Tee Time Notes.

- It does NOT override Tee Time–level notes.

- It displays anywhere tee time notes are surfaced today.

- When the Rate Period field is blank:

- The system displays Facility-level Tee Time Notes (unless a Tee Time–level note exists).

- Existing facilities without Rate Period notes function exactly as they do today.

- Clearing the Rate Period note reverts behavior to Facility-level (unless Tee Time–level exists).
issues.descriptionHtml:
GOAL

Introduce a Tee Time Notes field at the Rate Set level that overrides the existing Facility-level Tee Time Notes setting when populated.

This enhancement enables differentiated messaging by rate set while maintaining backward compatibility with existing functionality.

Business Problem

Today, Tee Time Notes can be configured at:

  1. Tee Time Level (most granular – existing functionality)

  2. Facility Level (existing functionality)

However, there is no Rate Set level option.

For tee sheet pricing facilities, individual tee times cannot be manually updated. This removes the ability to leverage Tee Time level notes (Priority 1), leaving only a single facility-wide message.

As a result:

  • All rate sets inherit the same note.

  • Courses cannot differentiate messaging by rate type.

  • Tee sheet pricing facilities lack flexibility to manage notes at scale.

Adding a rate set level override resolves this gap while preserving existing logic.

Current State (Must Remain Unchanged)

1. Tee Time–Level Notes (Existing – Highest Priority)

  • Configured directly on an individual tee time.

  • Overrides all other note levels.

  • Must continue to function exactly as it does today.

2. Facility-Level Notes (Existing – Lowest Priority)

  • Configured in Tee Time Settings.

  • Applies to all rate sets and tee times unless overridden.

  • Must continue to function exactly as it does today.

New Functionality

Rate Set–Level Tee Time Notes (Middle prio)

Add a new “Tee Time Notes” text field on the Rate Periods page.

The field must support the same formatting and character limits as the Facility-level Tee Time Notes field

Logic / Precedence Rules

The system must follow this hierarchy:

  1. Tee Time–Level Notes (existing – highest priority)

  2. Rate Period–Level Notes (NEW)

  3. Facility-Level Notes (existing – fallback)

Logic Behavior

  • If Tee Time–level notes exist → use those.

  • Else if Rate Period–level notes are populated → use those.

  • Else → use Facility-level notes.

  • If Rate Period field is blank/null → default to Facility-level notes.

No changes to the behavior of Tee Time–level or Facility-level notes.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. A new Tee Time Notes field is visible on the Rate Periods page.

  2. Existing Tee Time–level notes continue to override all other note levels.

  3. When a Rate Period Tee Time Note is entered:

    • It overrides the Facility-level Tee Time Notes.

    • It does NOT override Tee Time–level notes.

    • It displays anywhere tee time notes are surfaced today.

  4. When the Rate Period field is blank:

    • The system displays Facility-level Tee Time Notes (unless a Tee Time–level note exists).

  5. Existing facilities without Rate Period notes function exactly as they do today.

  6. Clearing the Rate Period note reverts behavior to Facility-level (unless Tee Time–level exists).

issues.summary: [Commerce] Total tax equals $0 in GNC on the reservation edit screen after completing a reservation in BE
issues.description:
Best Guess

Best guess as to cause is this data is being ingested incorrectly from commerce and we’re not mapping a field correctly to have it display in GNC.

Environment: QA

STR:

- Navigate to a BE with commerce API enabled https://xyz-david-clark-smartplay-commerce.qa-phx-ui-be.svc.golf/teetimes

- Log in

- Add a tee time to cart (reproduces for all rate types: non-prepay, prepaid, partially prepaid)

- Proceed to checkout

- View the summary section and verify that tax is calculated properly

- Fill in the payment form and complete a reservation

- Go to https://golfnowcentral.dev.golfnow.io/ > Customers > Search for the user from step 2

- View the reservation history and click “edit” for the needed one

- View the reservation summary above the transaction history

AR: The total tax value = $0 despite the taxes get calculated properly in the transaction history

Note: Does not reproduce for non-commerce BEs

Prepaid reservation

Non-Prepay reservation

ER: Total tax field should show correct total tax value in GNC

issues.summary: Expose GolfPass T&Cs on Invoice, Checkout, GetProductSubscription
issues.description:
The UI teams will be required to display the T&Cs so we need this exposed on all applicable endpoints. The Template to PPID mapping comes from the work created in https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-13302

- OrderInvoice response, OrderCheckout response, GetProductsByChannel and GetProductSubscription response for a Product

- https://rc2-api.gnsvc.com/GolfNowAPI.svc/rest/channel/331/customers/{{EmailAddress}}/order-invoices

- https://rc2-api.gnsvc.com/GolfNowAPI.svc/rest/channel/5151/customers/{{EmailAddress}}/orders

- {{prod-api-base-url}}/GolfNowAPI.svc/rest/channel/331/products/get?productTypeId=9

- https://rc2-api.gnsvc.com/GolfNowAPI.svc/rest/channel/331/customers/{{EmailAddress}}/subscriptions?productTypeId=9

- Add new field named something like ProductTerms to the response of all 3 endpoints

- Return markdown code so the UI can format hyperlinks and bold text since that is expected by legal

- Logic for Invoice:

- Validate the PPID in the request of the invoice

- Validate the ChannelID that matches the request

- Return the template text that matches those two in the ProductTerms

- Logic for Checkout:

- Validate the PPID in the request of the checkout

- Validate the ChannelID that matches the request

- Return the template text that matches those two in the ProductTerms

- This ticket is specific to the submission of the Terms https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-12809

- Logic for GetProducts

- Return ProductTerms  for all ProductPriceId objects based on the ChannelId requested in the URL

- Logic for GetProductSubscription:

- simply return the stored value that was created upon initial submission https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-12809
issues.descriptionHtml:
The UI teams will be required to display the T&Cs so we need this exposed on all applicable endpoints. The Template to PPID mapping comes from the work created in https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-13302

  1. OrderInvoice response, OrderCheckout response, GetProductsByChannel and GetProductSubscription response for a Product

    1. https://rc2-api.gnsvc.com/GolfNowAPI.svc/rest/channel/331/customers/{{EmailAddress}}/order-invoices

    2. https://rc2-api.gnsvc.com/GolfNowAPI.svc/rest/channel/5151/customers/{{EmailAddress}}/orders

    3. {{prod-api-base-url}}/GolfNowAPI.svc/rest/channel/331/products/get?productTypeId=9

    4. https://rc2-api.gnsvc.com/GolfNowAPI.svc/rest/channel/331/customers/{{EmailAddress}}/subscriptions?productTypeId=9

  2. Add new field named something like ProductTerms to the response of all 3 endpoints

    1. Return markdown code so the UI can format hyperlinks and bold text since that is expected by legal

  3. Logic for Invoice:

    1. Validate the PPID in the request of the invoice

    2. Validate the ChannelID that matches the request

    3. Return the template text that matches those two in the ProductTerms

  4. Logic for Checkout:

    1. Validate the PPID in the request of the checkout

    2. Validate the ChannelID that matches the request

    3. Return the template text that matches those two in the ProductTerms

    4. This ticket is specific to the submission of the Terms https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-12809

  5. Logic for GetProducts

    1. Return ProductTerms for all ProductPriceId objects based on the ChannelId requested in the URL

  6. Logic for GetProductSubscription:

    1. simply return the stored value that was created upon initial submission https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-12809

issues.summary: New GNC Page - Update Tee Times (Trade)
issues.description:
In order to better manage trade rounds, the revenue team has requested a new page in GNC that will be similar to Update Tee Times with a few adjustments specific to trade.

Requirements:

- Search functionality

- Start Date:

- Calendar date picker

- Default to the current date

- End Date:

- Calendar date picker

- Default to the next date

- Start Time:

- Text input

- Default to 5:00AM

- End Time:

- Text input

- Default to 10:59PM

- From Rate:

- Text input

- Default to $0.00

- To Rate:

- Text input

- Default to $500.00

- Market Tier:

- Multi-select

- Default to Tier 1 and Tier 2 automatically selected

- Market:

- Multi-select

- Facility Group:

- Multi-select

- Market options:

- Northeast

- Northwest

- Southeast

- Southwest

- Interface:

- Multi-select

- Courses at Floor:

- Yes/No select

- No default selection needed

- Temporary Facility Closure:

- Yes/No select

- No default selection needed

- Status

- Single select dropdown and customizable status selector (same as current UI in GNC)

- Sort Order:

- Single select dropdown

- Sort order options:

- Play Date/Rate Set

- Play Date/Facility/Rate Set

- Facility/Rate Set/Play Date

- Rate Set/Date

- Search button

- Search results to display:

- Play Facility ID

- Play Facility

- Rate Set

- Interface Type

- Market

- Load Time

- Tee Time

- Play Date

- Status

- Players

- Unsold Trade

- Unsold Trade = Trade Capacity - Trade Sold

- Trade Capacity

- Trade Utilization

- Trade Utilization = Trade Sold / Trade Capacity

- Online Rate

- Current Discount Amount

- This comes from the Trade Price Matrix

- Floor Amount

- This comes from the Floor Amount in the Trade Price Matrix

- Course Rate Being Discounted

- Revenue at Risk

- Revenue at Risk = Unsold Trade * Online Rate

- Additional displays:

- Revenue at Risk

- Total Revenue at Risk

- Trade Utilization

- Total Trade Utilization

Apple Pay Multi Merchant Support
issues.description:
Description

Currently, we are sending the Apple Pay token to the payment processing API without including any information about the type of item being purchased when requesting the PaymentMethodKey.

We need to enhance this integration to include an additional field that specifies the item type (e.g., Product, TeeTime.) as part of the payload sent to the payment processor.

This change will allow the payment processor to correctly classify and handle transactions based on the nature of the purchased item.

Scope / Requirements

- Update the request payload sent to the payment processing API to get the PaymentMethodKey to include a new field:

- LineItem(or agreed-upon field name)

- Ensure the field is populated based on the item being purchased in the application

- Update any relevant service layers, DTOs, or serializers involved in constructing the request

Acceptance Criteria

- Apple Pay payment requests include the new LineItem field in the payload

- The value of LineItem accurately reflects the item being purchased

- Logging/monitoring confirms the field is being sent correctly

Technical Notes

- Verify API contract with payment processor for the new field

- Ensure no impact to non-Apple Pay payment methods unless required
issues.descriptionHtml:
Description

Currently, we are sending the Apple Pay token to the payment processing API without including any information about the type of item being purchased when requesting the PaymentMethodKey.

We need to enhance this integration to include an additional field that specifies the item type (e.g., Product, TeeTime.) as part of the payload sent to the payment processor.

This change will allow the payment processor to correctly classify and handle transactions based on the nature of the purchased item.

Scope / Requirements

  • Update the request payload sent to the payment processing API to get the PaymentMethodKey to include a new field:

    • LineItem(or agreed-upon field name)

  • Ensure the field is populated based on the item being purchased in the application

  • Update any relevant service layers, DTOs, or serializers involved in constructing the request

Acceptance Criteria

  • Apple Pay payment requests include the new LineItem field in the payload

  • The value of LineItem accurately reflects the item being purchased

  • Logging/monitoring confirms the field is being sent correctly

Technical Notes

  • Verify API contract with payment processor for the new field

  • Ensure no impact to non-Apple Pay payment methods unless required

comments.author: Yoel Martin Pardo
comments.body:
QA: We will need to do regression pretty much on everything just to make sure that we are not braking anything with these changes.

Very important: We recommended to do this because we needed to update the payment processing client on customer service and we found some issues that affected storing/updating credit cards. Payment processing fixed this issues, but we want to make sure that we are not breaking anything else with this changes.

Details about changes:

We are now passing new information to payment processing when trying to get the PaymentMethodKey (LineItem) where we tell them if the PaymentMethodKey is for a product or a teetime.

We stored that information on the CRMApplePay table (column  name ItemType) when 0 means Product and 1 means TeeTime.

We also updated some stored procedures to be able to handle that new column.
comments.bodyHtml:
QA: We will need to do regression pretty much on everything just to make sure that we are not braking anything with these changes.

Very important: We recommended to do this because we needed to update the payment processing client on customer service and we found some issues that affected storing/updating credit cards. Payment processing fixed this issues, but we want to make sure that we are not breaking anything else with this changes.

Details about changes:

We are now passing new information to payment processing when trying to get the PaymentMethodKey (LineItem) where we tell them if the PaymentMethodKey is for a product or a teetime.

We stored that information on the CRMApplePay table (column name ItemType) when 0 means Product and 1 means TeeTime.

We also updated some stored procedures to be able to handle that new column.

issues.summary: Support PayStack SCA
issues.description:
We currently support SCA validations for Stripe payments. We now need to support a new provider. The goal of this is to have Paystack provider use the same API2.1 and Staging flow as Stripe so there is no impact to the various UIs.

Documentation: https://golfnow.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/TEC/pages/10476486659/Paystack+Implementation+using+3DS

Differences:

- Paystack not only supports a redirect URL (similar to Stripe), it also supports the ability to have multiple user inputted validations steps.

- The Payments team will manage/support the UI to enter various validation datapoints

- Path #1 - Similar to Stripe

- User gets SCA validation

- User validates from the provided URL

- User is redirected back to checkout to complete transaction

- Path #2

- User gets SCA validation and it opens URL to enter their birthday

- User enters birthday

- User gets secondary validation to enter phone number

- User enters phone number

- User is redirected back to checkout to complete reservation

Update Hot Deal Cancellation Policy Text
issues.description:
Please update the cancellation policies to the following:

- Hot Deal Current Text

- Hot Deals tee times are refundable up to 24 hours prior to the time of play. Refunded amounts will be available as an account credit and can be used against prepaid amounts on your next booking. Account credits are valid for 6 months from the date of issue. Cancellations made within 24 hours of your tee time are only refundable if the course is closed due to weather on the day of play.

- Hot Deal New Text

- Hot Deals tee times are refundable up to 24 hours prior to the time of play. Refunded amounts will be available as an account credit and can be used against prepaid amounts on your next Hot Deals booking. Account credits are valid for 6 months from the date of issue. Cancellations made within 24 hours of your tee time are only refundable if the course is closed due to weather on the day of play.

issues.summary: OpenAI Apps - UI components
issues.description:
Many of the CORE requirements https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-14099 carry over to the UI.

Use ChannelId 331 for all API calls

Design: https://www.figma.com/design/Kl5QZg9LireP1rRfp5tKkN/GolfNow-ChatGPT-Integration?node-id=0-1&p=f&m=dev

- Tapping on the facility info section should take you to the facility page with the filters set that match the prompt

- Sample URL: https://www.golfnow.com/tee-times/facility/4356-rio-pinar-golf-club/searchutm_source=chatgptapp

- Sample params: #facilitytype=0&sortby=Date&view=Grouping&holes=3&timemax=42&timemin=10&players=0&pricemax=10000&pricemin=0

- Tapping on the individual tee time tile should take you to the tee time details

- Sample URL: https://www.golfnow.com/tee-times/facility/4356/tee-time/2023881338

- Tapping on a CTA button should take you to the facility page with the filters set that match the prompt

- Sample URL: https://www.golfnow.com/tee-times/facility/4356-rio-pinar-golf-club/searchutm_source=chatgptapp

- Sample Params: #facilitytype=0&sortby=Date&view=Grouping&holes=3&timemax=42&timemin=10&players=0&pricemax=10000&pricemin=0

- append a utm_source=chatgptapp to all links so page views can be tracked with analytics and we can create a cookie

- Make sure this is not stripped for the app deeplinks

- Store this in the browser for 24 hours

- If a reservation is made with that stored value

- Submit ReservationMetadata at time of purchase for any purchase within 24 hours: Key 86, Value: 1

- FYI Key 86 = IsChatGPTReservation

- Support Fee Messaging

- Match the same UI logic from https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/GNSCM-8343

- Support Simulators

- If IsSimulator=True, add the same Simulator badge on the course image to match GN UI

- Change the word “Tee Times” “Available”

- Display the Duration

    • Sample URL: https://www.golfnow.com/tee-times/facility/4356/tee-time/2023881338

  • Tapping on a CTA button should take you to the facility page with the filters set that match the prompt

issues.summary: Platform Docs Site Setup
issues.description:
Background

We have historically struggled to maintain clear and useful API documentation for external partners (and even internal folks). To remedy this, we will begin deployment of an https://www.mkdocs.org/ based website that will host API reference material for teams to integrate with.

User Story

As a GolfNow developer, I want to be able to write API documentation for projects such as the Order Processing API or Inventory API in order to support integration with our API platform.

Tasks

- Setup a repo, gn-docs-platform and configure an mkdocs instance with a theme.

- Develop an organization scheme for the docs site, we will want to cover the following scopes:

- Authorization / Authentication (using gn-svc-auth and specifically our Platform Principal system)

- Inventory API

- Order Processing API

- Customer Graph API

- Each API needs to support versioning, so the folder structure should be something like:

- Inventory API

- v1

- Tee Times

- Club Fitting

- Subscriptions

- v2

- Etc

- Apply GolfNow brand colors to site

- See
issues.descriptionHtml:
Background

We have historically struggled to maintain clear and useful API documentation for external partners (and even internal folks). To remedy this, we will begin deployment of an https://www.mkdocs.org/ based website that will host API reference material for teams to integrate with.

User Story

As a GolfNow developer, I want to be able to write API documentation for projects such as the Order Processing API or Inventory API in order to support integration with our API platform.

Tasks

  1. Setup a repo, gn-docs-platform and configure an mkdocs instance with a theme.

  2. Develop an organization scheme for the docs site, we will want to cover the following scopes:

    1. Authorization / Authentication (using gn-svc-auth and specifically our Platform Principal system)

    2. Inventory API

    3. Order Processing API

    4. Customer Graph API

  3. Each API needs to support versioning, so the folder structure should be something like:

    1. Inventory API

      1. v1

        1. Tee Times

        2. Club Fitting

        3. Subscriptions

      2. v2

        1. Etc

  4. Apply GolfNow brand colors to site

    1. See

issues.summary: FinTech - PayStack - South Africa Payment Provider - AWS DEV Integration Testing
issues.description:
Slack Conversation : https://versant-golfnow.slack.com/archives/C0AGQ3P89DZ/p1771961131249149

AWS DEV GNC Location :

- Once we get the ZAR Merchant Key , we will need a script from @Raghav Kancherla  to insert the new ChargeBy record into CRM

- Use GP-ZAR in the facility Settings page for the new Facility - Update Required in GNC

- Load tee times

- BASELINE TESTING : Book a tee time using 4242424242424242 in GNC

- BASELINE TESTING : Cancel the Booking

- BASELINE TESTING : Refund the reservation

- BASELINE TESTING : Validate the CRMChargeDetails table in CRM , to see which Merchant Key was used , along with which payment processor .

- BASELINE TESTING : Co-Ordinate with Payments QA on the Database validations on their side .

CORE QA → GN WEB UI Testing : Contact the UI team to validate that the location which was generated in GNC , exists on WEB golfnow.dev.io . Once that is confirmed , do the Booking → Cancellation → Refund Flow for a future tee time on the web .

:computer:  The Web Ui team will be conducting tests of their own for validating the new Payment Processor
issues.descriptionHtml:
Slack Conversation : https://versant-golfnow.slack.com/archives/C0AGQ3P89DZ/p1771961131249149

AWS DEV GNC Location :

  1. Once we get the ZAR Merchant Key , we will need a script from @Raghav Kancherla to insert the new ChargeBy record into CRM

  2. Use GP-ZAR in the facility Settings page for the new Facility - Update Required in GNC

  3. Load tee times

  4. BASELINE TESTING : Book a tee time using 4242424242424242 in GNC

  5. BASELINE TESTING : Cancel the Booking

  6. BASELINE TESTING : Refund the reservation

  7. BASELINE TESTING : Validate the CRMChargeDetails table in CRM , to see which Merchant Key was used , along with which payment processor .

  8. BASELINE TESTING : Co-Ordinate with Payments QA on the Database validations on their side .

CORE QA → GN WEB UI Testing : Contact the UI team to validate that the location which was generated in GNC , exists on WEB golfnow.dev.io . Once that is confirmed , do the Booking → Cancellation → Refund Flow for a future tee time on the web .

:computer: The Web Ui team will be conducting tests of their own for validating the new Payment Processor

issues.summary: EZRTS HF Assist - Bulk Sync
issues.description:
Assisting the EZLRTS / EZL / G1 team , in configuring a GNC location , where we needed to add rack times , check rate periods , interface configuration pages , and connection to tee sheet .

Current Issue : EZL returning back an EZL 22 error code , where its saying “ Tee Time Not Available “ , despite the fact that the tee time was confirmed to be available and open on both the EZTP and EZRTS tee sheets .

This is for an HF from the EZRTS team prospective on

Update : They ended up using another location for the testing flows , and was able to sign off with that nj_atlantis .

issues.summary: AWS DEV - Trouble Shoot Issues in GNC and Test Fixes
issues.description:
We have started to see issues in AWS DEV GNC , after a few changes to resolve Snyk issues were pushed to AWS DEV .

These changes disrupted the ability to change facilities , navigate to other pages and load tee times .

Most were JQuery issues , and were being resolved one by one , as the Developers local environment didn't show any of the issues , but they only popped up once the build was complete in AWS DEV .

issues.description:
We saw issues in API 2.1 where we were not able to make new customers  , and in customer service where we could not look up customers in GNC .

We also saw issues in TR , where we couldn't get an TR token .

issues.summary: Merchant & Payment Processor Configuration
issues.description: Implement the attached design for a Merchant of Record collection, a Payment Processor collection, and a way to map a Merchant of Record to a Payment Processor. Resulting product should include API endpoints in the Platform API to manage these objects, auditing on the objects to ensure we track changes, history collection to be able to see past versions of records, data validations, as well as UI on GNC 2.1 to support managing these entities.
issues.descriptionHtml: Implement the attached design for a Merchant of Record collection, a Payment Processor collection, and a way to map a Merchant of Record to a Payment Processor. Resulting product should include API endpoints in the Platform API to manage these objects, auditing on the objects to ensure we track changes, history collection to be able to see past versions of records, data validations, as well as UI on GNC 2.1 to support managing these entities.

issues.summary: Geosearch2.0 : optimization
issues.description:
We were seeing some higher memory usage in mongodb cluster that could be due to higher traffic. However we will investigate any query / index optimization that we can do to lower the memory usage.

# Search Page Faults Optimization

## Context
- Node1 memory elevation causing Search Page Faults spike
- 2.5M GeoLocation records, 29K PointOfInterest records
- Indexes: LOCATION_SEARCH_KEY_V2, POI_KEY_SEARCH_V2

## Tasks

### 1. [ ] Cap `take` parameter on GeoplacesController
- **File:** `Geosearch.API/Controllers/V1/GeoplacesController.cs`
- **Endpoints:** `/geoplaces/lookup` (line 55), `/geoplaces/city` (line 172)
- **Change:** Add `take = Math.Clamp(take, 1, 50)` at the top of both methods
- **Impact:** Prevents unbounded Atlas Search scans from a single request
- **Requires:** Code change only (no index rebuild)

### 2. [ ] Raise `minGrams` from 1 to 3 on autocomplete fields
- **Indexes:** `LOCATION_SEARCH_KEY_V2` (Name, Names, Admin1Name), `POI_KEY_SEARCH_V2` (Name, SearchKey)
- **Change:** `minGrams: 1` → `minGrams: 3` on all autocomplete fields
- **Impact:** ~40-60% reduction in search index size; eliminates single/double char gram explosion
- **Tradeoff:** Autocomplete won't return results until user types 3+ characters
- **Requires:** Index rebuild

### 3. [ ] Merge dual parallel Location aggregations into single query
- **File:** `Geosearch.Repository.Mongo/Repositories/GeoLocationRepository.cs` (lines 132-192)
- **Current:** Two `$search` aggregations run via `Task.WhenAll()` (location + postal), then merge/re-sort in memory
- **Change:** Combine into single `$search` compound query with `should` clauses for both postal and non-postal matches
- **Impact:** Halves concurrent index segment reads per request; reduces page fault pressure
- **Requires:** Code change only (no index rebuild)

### 4. [ ] Reduce `maxExpansions` from 50 to 20
- **Files:**
- `Geosearch.Repository.Mongo/Helpers/PoiQueryDefinitionHelper.cs` — 3 usages (TextSearchInACircle, TextSearchWithNear)
- `Geosearch.Repository.Mongo/Helpers/LocationQueryDefinitionHelper.cs` — 3 usages (TextSearchWithNear, PostalCodeSearchWithNear, TextSearchWithNearByCity)
- **Change:** `maxExpansions: 50` → `maxExpansions: 20` in all fuzzy configurations
- **Impact:** Fewer fuzzy variants per query = fewer index segment reads; minor tradeoff on typo tolerance
- **Requires:** Code change only (no index rebuild)

### 5. [ ] Add `.Limit()` and projections to unbounded POI queries
- **File:** `Geosearch.Repository.Mongo/Repositories/PointOfInterestRepository.cs`
- **Methods:**
- `FindPointOfInterestByAddressAndLocation` — no `.Limit()`, no projection, returns full documents
- `GetPointOfInterests(long[] ids)` — no `.Limit()`, no projection
- **Change:** Add reasonable `.Limit()` and projections to both methods
- **Impact:** Prevents unexpected large result sets from consuming memory
- **Requires:** Code change only (no index rebuild)

### 6. [ ] Reduce `maxGrams` from 10 to 7 on autocomplete fields
- **Indexes:** `LOCATION_SEARCH_KEY_V2` (Name, Names, Admin1Name, PostalCode), `POI_KEY_SEARCH_V2` (Name, SearchKey)
- **Change:** `maxGrams: 10` → `maxGrams: 7` on all autocomplete fields
- **Impact:** ~30% fewer stored grams per term; minimal user-facing impact since longer prefixes handled by `string` type mappings
- **Requires:** Index rebuild

issues.summary: Premier Card Miami - Subscriptions Being Created Despite Purchase Errors

Additionally, the subscriptions all have an end date that expires 24 hours before the start date.

comments.author: Luis Vallejo Mursuli
comments.body:

comments.body: This is caused by an exhaustion of the records in Customer.MembershipProviderNumber which is not being gracefully handled. Opened up https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/GNDS-285 to resolve by topping up this table with 100K IDs.

issues.summary: Update Geo Search configuration
issues.description:
We got some feedback on our GN-EU app from one of our German teammates over in Marketing about our Geo Searches endpoint (/v1/geoplaces/lookup), its items number 4 & 5 in this doc: https://bwt3-my.sharepoint.com/:w:/p/206637259/IQCCZ1Lb_3afTbWNEbrvHj2vAeYmlNAvtjZY54yJYeSp1AQ?e=fIlcxl . Brian said mentioned it should be a simple fix by adding alternate spelling

- Test that typing in one of the alternate spellings of cities returns the result in the same priority as the primary spelling

- Example

- Primary - Munich

- Alternate - München

- Typing in München would still return as Munich in the UI but will be first on the list - Future ticket will be created to support translating location names

- Typing in Munich would be first on the list
issues.descriptionHtml:
We got some feedback on our GN-EU app from one of our German teammates over in Marketing about our Geo Searches endpoint (/v1/geoplaces/lookup), its items number 4 & 5 in this doc: https://bwt3-my.sharepoint.com/:w:/p/206637259/IQCCZ1Lb_3afTbWNEbrvHj2vAeYmlNAvtjZY54yJYeSp1AQ?e=fIlcxl . Brian said mentioned it should be a simple fix by adding alternate spelling

  • Test that typing in one of the alternate spellings of cities returns the result in the same priority as the primary spelling

    • Example

      • Primary - Munich

      • Alternate - München

      • Typing in München would still return as Munich in the UI but will be first on the list - Future ticket will be created to support translating location names

      • Typing in Munich would be first on the list

issues.summary: Modernize Affiliate API dependencies to remediate security vulnerabilities
issues.description:
Modernize the Affiliate API's application dependencies and supporting configuration to remediate identified security vulnerabilities and improve compatibility with .NET Framework 4.8.

Changes

- Updated and revalidated application dependencies to ensure compatibility with .NET Framework 4.8.

- Corrected package, assembly, and analyzer references impacted by dependency upgrades.

- Modernized and consolidated dependency injection configuration used throughout the application.

- Added and corrected service registrations required for application startup and runtime dependency resolution.

- Improved application startup and configuration initialization to align with current dependency versions.

- Removed obsolete configuration artifacts and legacy project references that were no longer required.

- Reviewed and updated client-side dependency configuration and JavaScript resources.

- Evaluated Bootstrap and front-end dependencies while preserving compatibility with the existing Bootstrap 3 user interface.

- Investigated reported security vulnerabilities, including a Handlebars vulnerability, and verified that Handlebars is not directly referenced or loaded by the application.

- Improved maintainability, readability, and consistency of application configuration and dependency management.
issues.descriptionHtml:
Modernize the Affiliate API's application dependencies and supporting configuration to remediate identified security vulnerabilities and improve compatibility with .NET Framework 4.8.

Changes

  • Updated and revalidated application dependencies to ensure compatibility with .NET Framework 4.8.

  • Corrected package, assembly, and analyzer references impacted by dependency upgrades.

  • Modernized and consolidated dependency injection configuration used throughout the application.

  • Added and corrected service registrations required for application startup and runtime dependency resolution.

  • Improved application startup and configuration initialization to align with current dependency versions.

  • Removed obsolete configuration artifacts and legacy project references that were no longer required.

  • Reviewed and updated client-side dependency configuration and JavaScript resources.

  • Evaluated Bootstrap and front-end dependencies while preserving compatibility with the existing Bootstrap 3 user interface.

  • Investigated reported security vulnerabilities, including a Handlebars vulnerability, and verified that Handlebars is not directly referenced or loaded by the application.

  • Improved maintainability, readability, and consistency of application configuration and dependency management.

comments.author: Tyler Olechnowicz
comments.body:
QA Testing Instructions

- Verify the Affiliate API application loads successfully.

- Sign in with a valid account and verify authentication functions as expected.

- Perform a full regression test of the application and verify pages load and function as expected.

- Verify application navigation and menus render correctly.

- Verify browser console is free of JavaScript errors during testing.

- Confirm no regressions are observed as a result of the dependency and configuration updates.

Expected Result:
The application loads successfully, authentication works as expected, pages render and function correctly, and no regressions are introduced by the dependency and configuration updates.

issues.summary: CLONE - [Commerce] Cannot complete a non-prepay reservation
issues.description:
Environment: QA

STR:

- Navigate to https://xyz-david-clark-smartplay-commerce.qa-phx-ui-be.svc.golf/checkout

- Log in

- Add a non-prepay tee time to cart (no convenience fee should be added, so due now = $0)

- Proceed to checkout

- Fill in the payment form and attempt to complete a reservation

{error: "Unknown error occurred. Error Track ID: 5758fd11-2eb5-4506-8405-1a4a4c24ac98",…}

code: "UNKNOWN_ERROR"

error: "Unknown error occurred. Error Track ID: 5758fd11-2eb5-4506-8405-1a4a4c24ac98"

Note 1: Is reproducing for all types of users

Note 2: Can reproduce for other courses like https://highlands-reserve-golf-club-commerce.qa-phx-ui-be.svc.golf/confirmation , https://xyz-wingfield-sp-commerce.qa-phx-ui-be.svc.golf/confirmation , https://alaska-golf-course-commerce.qa-phx-ui-be.svc.golf/?course=20684&date=2026-06-02&max=999999

ER: User should be able to purchase all types of tee times without issues

issues.summary: Surface IsWaivable and IsRefundable through the FeeConfiguration data access layer
issues.description:
Summary

CORE-14444 added IsWaivable and IsRefundable columns to Revenue.FeeConfiguration at the database level, but intentionally deferred the application-layer integration. This ticket connects those columns through the data access layer so they are available to downstream business logic (waiver decisions, refund calculations) in future tickets.

This is a data plumbing ticket — it makes the values available in-memory but does NOT change any business behavior. No customer-facing impact until future tickets consume these properties behind LaunchDarkly.

---
What This Connects To

These columns enable the Customizable Transaction Fees epic (CORE-13901) to support fees with different behaviors:

- IsWaivable — Determines whether a GolfPass waived-fee-credit can be applied to this fee. Standard convenience fees are waivable; tourism/regulatory fees are not.

- IsRefundable — Determines whether this fee is included in refund calculations on cancellation. Standard fees are refundable; certain regulatory fees are not.

Downstream consumers (future tickets, not this one):

- GolfPass waiver logic in Reservation Service (LoyaltyHelper)

- Refund/cancellation workflow in Reservation Service (BuildRefundInvoiceActivity)

- Admin UI in RMS for setting these values per fee configuration

---
Areas Touched

- Stored Procedure — Revenue.GetFeeConfigurationByOwnerFacilityCourse needs to include IsWaivable and IsRefundable in its CTE and final SELECT output

- Framework DTO — GolfNow.Framework.Pricing.Fees.Data.DTO.FeeConfiguration needs corresponding boolean properties

- Sequencing — The GNDS stored procedure update should deploy before or simultaneously with the code change. DTO properties should default to true as a safety net during rolling deploys.

---
What This Does NOT Touch

- No behavior changes — this is purely making data available

- No admin UI changes (RMS models, GolfCourse Service models — separate future ticket)

- No waiver logic changes

- No refund logic changes

- No other stored procedures need updating for this ticket (other sprocs that read/write FeeConfiguration don't need these columns yet — INSERT operations are protected by DEFAULT constraints, UPDATE operations don't touch these columns)

---
Technical Reference

A detailed investigation document is attached covering:

Please be sure to do your own investigation of course

- Full stored procedure analysis (all 6 sprocs that touch the table)

- Complete data flow diagram (DB → DAL → Provider → Consumer)

- Caching assessment (confirmed: no caching on this path)

- All FeeConfiguration model locations across the codebase

- Risk assessment and sequencing considerations

- Downstream consumer mapping for future tickets

---
Acceptance Criteria

- The booking flow's fee configuration data includes IsWaivable and IsRefundable values from the database

- Existing behavior is preserved — all current fees return true for both properties (matching the database defaults)

- No customer-facing behavior changes

- DTO property defaults ensure safe behavior during rolling deploys (default to true if column not present)

---
Related

- CORE-14444 — Database columns (prerequisite, CLOSED)

- CORE-13901 — Parent epic

- CORE-14332 — LaunchDarkly gating (foundation, CLOSED)

- CORE-14117 — Fee messaging moved to post-calculation (foundation, CLOSED)

statusChanges.summary: Add IsCaddyRequested parameter support to EZL/EZLRTS tee sheet interface

added.summary: Add IsCaddyRequested parameter support to EZL/EZLRTS tee sheet interface
added.description:
Background

In 2023, we added support for passing the IsCaddyRequested parameter as a pass-through to BRS and BRSG1 tee sheet interfaces to support ClubUp (caddy service) integrations. However, this parameter was not added to the EZL tee sheet interface.

Current State

- ✅ BRS tee sheet: Passes IsCaddyRequested parameter (line 163 in BRSTeeSheet.cs)

- ✅ BRSG1 tee sheet: Passes IsCaddyRequested parameter (line 199 in BRSG1TeeSheet.cs)

- ❌ EZL tee sheet: Does NOT pass IsCaddyRequested parameter

Issue

The EZL tee sheet (code: "EZL") does not handle the IsCaddyRequested parameter when making reservations. This means courses using EZLRTS tee sheet systems cannot indicate caddy requests through our booking flow, creating an inconsistent experience across different tee sheet providers.

Expected Behavior

When a booking is made through the EZL tee sheet interface with IsCaddyRequested = true, the caddie request should be submitted to the EZLinks Integrators API via the dedicated Caddie Request endpoint (see RTS-2163), similar to how BRS and BRSG handle it through their respective interfaces.

---
Technical Details

Important Update (per Viitalii Slobodianyk): A completely new endpoint was added in RTS-2163 to support caddie requests for EZL/EZRTS. The EZL implementation should use this dedicated endpoint rather than adding a parameter to the existing booking payload.

⚠️ BRS/BRSG1 must remain untouched. BRS and BRSG1 are still using the old method (passing IsCaddyRequested directly in the booking payload). Do NOT modify BRS/BRSG1 — only EZL will use the new Caddie Request API endpoint.

New Caddie Request Endpoint (from RTS-2163):

- Type: POST

- URL path: Under the "Tee Time" path in the Integrators API

- GrantArea: APIReservationSystem

- Input Parameters:

- SponsorID (required)

- ConfirmID (required)

- Caddie-Requested (required, Boolean)

- Behavior: Writes value to ETNv4.dbo.TTQuick.QuickEntryCaddy (False = 0, True = 1)

- Response: Indicates success or failure

- Failure reasons: missing parameters, SponsorID lacks access to course, ConfirmID doesn't exist

File to modify: Framework/GolfNow.Framework.TeeSheets/Interfaces/EZL/EZLinksTeeSheet.cs

Method to update: BookTeeTimeAsync (starting around line 2208)

What's needed: After a successful booking, if request.TeeTime.IsCaddyRequested is true, call the Caddie Request POST endpoint with the SponsorID and the ConfirmID from the booking response.

Reference implementations:

- BRSTeeSheet.cs:163 (old method — DO NOT CHANGE)

- BRSG1TeeSheet.cs:199 (old method — DO NOT CHANGE)

- RTS-2163 — Caddie Request endpoint spec (EZL uses this)

---
Acceptance Criteria

- After a successful EZL booking with IsCaddyRequested = true, the Caddie Request endpoint is called with the appropriate SponsorID and ConfirmID

- Parameter defaults to false/0 when not explicitly set (no endpoint call needed)

- BRS and BRSG1 implementations remain unchanged (they use the existing pass-through method)

- Existing EZL bookings continue to work without regression

- Handles endpoint failure gracefully (booking should not fail if caddie request fails)

---
Additional Notes: The parameter is already available in the TR API (Reservation.IsCaddyRequested) and flows through to the MakeReservationRequest object, so the plumbing exists—we just need to call the new Caddie Request endpoint after a successful EZL booking when the flag is set.
added.descriptionHtml:
Background

In 2023, we added support for passing the IsCaddyRequested parameter as a pass-through to BRS and BRSG1 tee sheet interfaces to support ClubUp (caddy service) integrations. However, this parameter was not added to the EZL tee sheet interface.

Current State

- ✅ BRS tee sheet: Passes IsCaddyRequested parameter (line 163 in BRSTeeSheet.cs)

- ✅ BRSG1 tee sheet: Passes IsCaddyRequested parameter (line 199 in BRSG1TeeSheet.cs)

- ❌ EZL tee sheet: Does NOT pass IsCaddyRequested parameter

Issue

The EZL tee sheet (code: "EZL") does not handle the IsCaddyRequested parameter when making reservations. This means courses using EZLRTS tee sheet systems cannot indicate caddy requests through our booking flow, creating an inconsistent experience across different tee sheet providers.

Expected Behavior

When a booking is made through the EZL tee sheet interface with IsCaddyRequested = true, the caddie request should be submitted to the EZLinks Integrators API via the dedicated Caddie Request endpoint (see RTS-2163), similar to how BRS and BRSG handle it through their respective interfaces.

Technical Details

Important Update (per Viitalii Slobodianyk): A completely new endpoint was added in RTS-2163 to support caddie requests for EZL/EZRTS. The EZL implementation should use this dedicated endpoint rather than adding a parameter to the existing booking payload.

⚠️ BRS/BRSG1 must remain untouched. BRS and BRSG1 are still using the old method (passing IsCaddyRequested directly in the booking payload). Do NOT modify BRS/BRSG1 — only EZL will use the new Caddie Request API endpoint.

New Caddie Request Endpoint (from RTS-2163):

  • Type: POST

  • URL path: Under the "Tee Time" path in the Integrators API

  • GrantArea: APIReservationSystem

  • Input Parameters:

- SponsorID (required)

- ConfirmID (required)

- Caddie-Requested (required, Boolean)

  • Behavior: Writes value to ETNv4.dbo.TTQuick.QuickEntryCaddy (False = 0, True = 1)

  • Response: Indicates success or failure

- Failure reasons: missing parameters, SponsorID lacks access to course, ConfirmID doesn't exist

File to modify: Framework/GolfNow.Framework.TeeSheets/Interfaces/EZL/EZLinksTeeSheet.cs

Method to update: BookTeeTimeAsync (starting around line 2208)

What's needed: After a successful booking, if request.TeeTime.IsCaddyRequested is true, call the Caddie Request POST endpoint with the SponsorID and the ConfirmID from the booking response.

Reference implementations:

  • BRSTeeSheet.cs:163 (old method — DO NOT CHANGE)

  • BRSG1TeeSheet.cs:199 (old method — DO NOT CHANGE)

  • RTS-2163 — Caddie Request endpoint spec (EZL uses this)

Acceptance Criteria

  • After a successful EZL booking with IsCaddyRequested = true, the Caddie Request endpoint is called with the appropriate SponsorID and ConfirmID

  • Parameter defaults to false/0 when not explicitly set (no endpoint call needed)

  • BRS and BRSG1 implementations remain unchanged (they use the existing pass-through method)

  • Existing EZL bookings continue to work without regression

  • Handles endpoint failure gracefully (booking should not fail if caddie request fails)

Additional Notes: The parameter is already available in the TR API (Reservation.IsCaddyRequested) and flows through to the MakeReservationRequest object, so the plumbing exists—we just need to call the new Caddie Request endpoint after a successful EZL booking when the flag is set.

added.summary: Update EZ Legacy API Call (AddConfirmationNote) to Include Param5 – SponsorID
added.description:
Summary

When calling the EZ Legacy API endpoint AddConfirmationNote, we are currently not passing Param5 (SponsorID).

We need to update the API call to include this parameter going forward.

---
Background

Endpoint: AddConfirmationNote

Current Parameters:

- Param1 – InternetUserID

- Param2 – InternetPassword

- Param3 – ConfirmID

- Param4 – ConfirmationNote

- Param5 – SponsorID (int) ← Currently not being sent

Example documentation snippet shows:

Param5  SponsorID  int

However, current logs confirm the call only sends up through Param4 (ConfirmationNote).

---
Requested Change

Modify the EZ Legacy API call to include:

Param5 = SponsorID

This value should be populated appropriately based on the booking context.

---
Reason for Change

The EZ Dev Team is implementing a workaround related to recent Hot Deal work.
Passing SponsorID will help ensure related functionality works properly and prevent edge case issues.

---
Acceptance Criteria

-  AddConfirmationNote API call includes Param5.

-  SponsorID is correctly populated and passed as an integer.

-  No regression issues with confirmation note creation.

-  Verified in logs that Param5 is being transmitted.

comments.author: Alex McNay
comments.body:
Outstanding issues

- USGA sourced courses are always showing the all 18 handicap value for the playingHandicap - even if the roundTypeId is Front9 (1) or Back9 (2)

- Example is 38017 in AWS Dev

- Dots are being distributed correctly, it’s the playingHandicap value that isn’t displaying correctly APPEARS RESOLVED BUT WILL MONITOR

- The 9 hole side that is not be played/set by the roundType is still getting half of it’s strokeAdjustments on the most difficult holes on that side. Example - first four holes are the Front9 most difficult which is correct. But the 2 on the Back9 shouldn’t get any strokes since this is a Front9 round

- Still not seeing the right flow for updating roundTypes

- When the playerRound is created it is inheriting the roundType value from the Outing Round, regardless of what is being passed in through the creation of the playerRound here - /api/v2/golfOuting/{golfOutingId}/rounds/{golfOutingRoundId}/players/{playerId}/playerRounds

- it is the Outing round roundHoleType value that initially determines how the playingHandicap is split between the holes in the hole array at the top of the playerRound call. So if the Outing Round is created with a "roundHoleType": 3 then the playingHandicap value will be divided between all 18 holes.

- The roundType value that is passed at the creation of the playerRound is determining what handicap value is used for playingHandicap. The correct value is being assigned to this field when based on what is provided to the create playerRound endpoint above.

- Updating the playerRound roundTypeId doesn’t change the playingHandicap, so a user isn’t able to move between 9 and 18 hole rounds on the same playerRound at the moment.

- however when this value is updated the strokeAdjustments are being moved around correctly for the most part,

- Front 9 and Back 9 give the correct strokeAdjustments - with the exception of issue 2 above where the other side gets a few lingering strokes

- All 18 is taking the playingHandicap and dividing it in half, which is correct except it’s using the wrong number since the playerHandicap isn’t changing from the creation of the playerRound - point 3.a.iii above
comments.bodyHtml:
Outstanding issues

  1. USGA sourced courses are always showing the all 18 handicap value for the playingHandicap - even if the roundTypeId is Front9 (1) or Back9 (2)

    1. Example is 38017 in AWS Dev

    2. Dots are being distributed correctly, it’s the playingHandicap value that isn’t displaying correctly APPEARS RESOLVED BUT WILL MONITOR

  2. The 9 hole side that is not be played/set by the roundType is still getting half of it’s strokeAdjustments on the most difficult holes on that side. Example - first four holes are the Front9 most difficult which is correct. But the 2 on the Back9 shouldn’t get any strokes since this is a Front9 round

  1. Still not seeing the right flow for updating roundTypes

    1. When the playerRound is created it is inheriting the roundType value from the Outing Round, regardless of what is being passed in through the creation of the playerRound here - /api/v2/golfOuting/{golfOutingId}/rounds/{golfOutingRoundId}/players/{playerId}/playerRounds

      1. it is the Outing round roundHoleType value that initially determines how the playingHandicap is split between the holes in the hole array at the top of the playerRound call. So if the Outing Round is created with a "roundHoleType": 3 then the playingHandicap value will be divided between all 18 holes.

      2. The roundType value that is passed at the creation of the playerRound is determining what handicap value is used for playingHandicap. The correct value is being assigned to this field when based on what is provided to the create playerRound endpoint above.

      3. Updating the playerRound roundTypeId doesn’t change the playingHandicap, so a user isn’t able to move between 9 and 18 hole rounds on the same playerRound at the moment.

        1. however when this value is updated the strokeAdjustments are being moved around correctly for the most part,

          1. Front 9 and Back 9 give the correct strokeAdjustments - with the exception of issue 2 above where the other side gets a few lingering strokes

          2. All 18 is taking the playingHandicap and dividing it in half, which is correct except it’s using the wrong number since the playerHandicap isn’t changing from the creation of the playerRound - point 3.a.iii above

added.summary: Add cart/tax include parameters to tee-time/book for EZL (EZTP and EZ-RTS) integration
added.description:
Summary

CORE needs to support the new cart/tax include parameter contract required by https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/RTS-7614#icft=RTS-7614 so RTS/EZRTS/EZP can receive a per-player/per-slot breakdown during tee-time/book, persist it in ETNv4, and send the correct values to G1/EZSuite POS during tender.

This is part of the forward path on the new booking API. It should not be treated as a legacy API remediation effort.

Check with @Juan Gonzalez
https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/RTS-7614

---
Expected Behavior

- CORE can provide the RTS-required optional cart/tax fields per player slot in the tee-time/book flow.

- When values are present, CORE sends them exactly as calculated/known by CORE.

- When values are absent, CORE omits them or sends null; CORE should not default or fabricate values.

- RTS can use the received values as its exclusive source for the ETNv4 cart/tax breakdown tracked under https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/RTS-7614#icft=RTS-7614.

---
Current Behavior / Problem

- https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/RTS-7614#icft=RTS-7614 is waiting on a CORE-side ticket to track the dependency for sending the new cart/tax parameter set.

- Existing CORE/reservation models include external charge detail concepts, but RTS needs a specific per-slot parameter contract on tee-time/book for greens fee tax, cart fee, cart fee tax, and partial-prepay status.

- Without CORE sending this breakdown, RTS cannot reliably persist the values needed for EZTP/EZRTS tender flows without deriving from unreliable ETNv4 fields.

---
Scope Guardrails

- Include the CORE-side request/DTO/model/mapping work needed to support the RTS-required parameter set on the new tee-time/book API path.

- Include tests proving optional/null behavior and value preservation.

- Do not implement a separate fix for the legacy booking API. For legacy API impact, the intended resolution is migration/use of the new API path.

- Do not require RTS storage, ETNv4 schema changes, or POS tender packet changes in this CORE ticket; those are tracked by https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/RTS-7614#icft=RTS-7614.

- Do not derive, supplement, or fall back to ETNv4 fields such as dbo.TeeOffTransactionAuditItem, dbo.CCAuthorization, or dbo.TTPrepaid.PrepaidFeeAmount.

---
Requested Parameters

All fields are optional and should be represented per slot/player where the booking contract supports slot-level detail.

- includes_greens_fee_tax

- greens_fee_tax_pct

- greens_fee_tax_amount_ppd

- greens_fee_tax_amount_due

- includes_cart_fee

- cart_fee_amount_ppd

- cart_fee_amount_due

- includes_cart_fee_tax

- cart_fee_tax_pct

- cart_fee_tax_amount_ppd

- cart_fee_tax_amount_due

- is_partial_prepaid

---
Technical Notes

- Source dependency: https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/RTS-7614#icft=RTS-7614, "Support Cart/Tax Include Parameters on tee-time/book".

- Existing CORE code paths that may be relevant:

- DotNet2/TeeTimesAPI/GolfNow.API.2.1/GolfNow.API.Business/Reservation/MakeReservationRequest.cs

- DotNet2/TeeTimesAPI/GolfNow.API.2.1/GolfNow.API.Business/Reservation/Workflow/MakeReservation/Activities/MakeReservationActivity.cs

- DotNet2/TeeTimesAPI/GolfNow.API.2.1/GolfNow.API.Contracts/DataContracts/Order/Items/ExternalChargeDetails.cs

- DotNet2/TeeTimesAPI/GolfNow.API.2.1/GolfNow.API.Contracts/DataContracts/Order/Items/ExternalChargeLineItem.cs

- DotNet2/TeeTimesAPI/GolfNow.API.2.1/GolfNow.API.Service/Converters/ServiceBusinessMapingExtensions.cs

- Internal Services/GolfNow.Services.Reservation/GolfNow.Services.Reservation.Provider/Workflow/Reservation/Activities/External/ApplyExternalChargeDetailsActivity.cs

- Current local model names already include ExternalChargeDetails and ExternalChargeLineItem; engineering should decide whether to extend that structure or add a dedicated RTS-facing cart/tax breakdown model.

---
Acceptance Criteria

Existing Behavior Preserved

- Existing booking flows continue to work when no cart/tax include parameters are supplied.

- Existing external charge detail behavior is not regressed for current callers.

New Behavior

- [ ] CORE supports all RTS-requested parameters above as optional values in the new tee-time/book flow.

- [ ] CORE sends provided values through to RTS without recalculating, defaulting, or deriving from ETNv4 fallback fields.

- [ ] Omitted parameters remain absent/null downstream.

- [ ] Per-slot/player data remains associated with the correct booked player/slot.

- [ ] Unit and/or integration coverage validates populated, partially populated, and omitted parameter sets.

Not Included Unless Product Expands Scope

- No legacy booking API remediation is included in this story.

- No RTS ETNv4 persistence or POS tender payload implementation is included in this story.

---
Test Matrix

Scenario

Expected Result

tee-time/book with all cart/tax fields populated

Values are accepted and passed downstream exactly as supplied.

tee-time/book with only greens fee tax values populated

Populated values pass downstream; omitted cart/cart tax values remain null/absent.

tee-time/book with no new fields

Existing booking behavior remains unchanged.

Multi-player booking with different per-slot values

Each slot retains its own cart/tax breakdown.

Partial prepaid booking

is_partial_prepaid and ppd/due splits reflect the supplied values.

---
Open Questions / Product Decisions

- Confirm the final wire-format and field casing expected by RTS for tee-time/book.

- Confirm whether the CORE source of truth should be the existing ExternalChargeDetails/line-item model or a dedicated cart/tax include DTO.

- Confirm whether is_partial_prepaid should remain a Boolean even though https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/RTS-7614#icft=RTS-7614 currently describes both not-prepaid and fully-prepaid as No.
